centers.english: when something is long or high enough to reach something
centers.chinese: 某物长或高到能达到某物的情况

expressions.text: go (out/up/over etc) to something/go as far as something

examples.english: I want a rug that goes right to the edge of the room.
examples.chinese: 我要一块能延伸到房间边上的小地毯。

examples.english: You'll have to move the television a little this way. The plug won't go as far as the wall from there.
examples.chinese: 你得把电视机朝这边挪一点，那边插头够不到墙上。

expressions.text: go all the way to something/ go right the way to something

examples.english: The fence went all the way over to the other side of the park.
examples.chinese: 这道篱笆一直延伸到公园的另一头。

examples.english: It won't work - the ladder won't reach.
examples.chinese: 不行，这梯子够不到。

examples.english: The snow almost reached my knees.
examples.chinese: 雪深得几乎到了我的膝盖。

examples.english: You see, the paint doesn't quite reach the edge of the paper.
examples.chinese: 你看，油漆连纸边也没涂到。

expressions.text: reach as far as/down to etc

examples.english: I don't think these curtains will reach down to the floor.
examples.chinese: 我觉得这些窗帘够不到地上。

examples.english: She was wearing grey socks that went right up to her knees.
examples.chinese: 她穿着齐膝高的灰袜子。

examples.english: After the flood, the water level in the river almost went up to the top of the dam.
examples.chinese: 洪水过后，河的水位几乎涨到堤坝顶部了。

expressions.text: go all the way up to something/ go right the way up to something

examples.english: The Christmas tree went all the way up to the ceiling.
examples.chinese: 这棵圣诞树一直够到了天花板。

examples.english: The rope went down to the bottom of the cliff.
examples.chinese: 这根绳子一直伸到悬崖的底部。

examples.english: She had long blonde hair that went down to her waist.
examples.chinese: 她长长的金发一直垂到腰部。

expressions.text: go all the way down to something/ go right the way down to something

examples.english: The scar on his face went from his eye all the way down to his chin.
examples.chinese: 他脸上的那条疤痕从他眼睛一直延伸到下巴。

examples.english: In the shallow end of the pool, the water comes up to my waist.
examples.chinese: 在水池较浅的那一头，水及到我腰部。

examples.english: Freddie's been growing so fast - he already comes up to my shoulder.
examples.chinese: 弗雷迪长得这么快，现在身高已到我的肩了。

examples.english: I can't wear this sweater - it almost comes down to my knees!
examples.chinese: 这件毛衣我穿不了，它几乎到我膝盖了！

examples.english: If you go into a mosque, you should wear sleeves that at least come down to your elbows.
examples.chinese: 如果你进入清真寺，应该穿袖子至少长达肘部的衣服。

examples.english: We followed a small track that stretched down to the sea.
examples.chinese: 我们沿一条伸往海边的小道走。

examples.english: We could see the mountains stretching as far as Vermont.
examples.chinese: 我们可以看到群山绵延，远及佛蒙特。

examples.english: Today, just 5% remains of the original wooded land that stretched from the Atlantic to the Mississippi.
examples.chinese: 原先从大西洋一直延伸到密西西比的那片林地如今只剩下5%了。

expressions.text: stretch all the way to something/ stretch right the way to something

examples.english: The oil slick stretched all the way to the horizon.
examples.chinese: 浮油一直延伸至地平线。

expressions.text: stretch into the distance/stretch as far as the eye can see

examples.english: There were poppy fields stretching as far as the eye could see.
examples.chinese: 罂粟田绵延，一望无际。

examples.english: The River Nile extends as far as Lake Victoria.
examples.chinese: 尼罗河一直延伸至维多利亚湖。

examples.english: Smith Point is a small piece of land extending a hundred yards or so into the water.
examples.chinese: 史密斯岬角是伸入水里一百码左右的一小块土地。

expressions.text: extend all the way from something to something/ extend right the way from something to something

examples.english: The Soviet Union extended all the way from the Baltic Sea to the Pacific Ocean.
examples.chinese: 苏联领土一直从波罗的海延伸至太平洋。

centers.english: when a number or amount reaches a particular level
centers.chinese: 数字或量达到某一水平

examples.english: Gold prices have reached their lowest level in 15 years.
examples.chinese: 黄金的价格跌到15年来最低价位。

examples.english: Wind speeds reached over 100 mph in coastal districts.
examples.chinese: 沿海地区的风速高达每小时100英里以上。

examples.english: The value of most houses in this area has reached over £200,000.
examples.chinese: 这一地区大部分房子的价格都达到20万英镑以上。

examples.english: Inflation continued to rise, reaching a peak of 28%.
examples.chinese: 通货膨胀率继续上升，达到了28%的最高点。

examples.english: The temperature hit 40°C in parts of the country yesterday.
examples.chinese: 昨天国内某些地区气温高达摄氏40度。

examples.english: If sales continue to increase, output may hit the 500,000 mark this year.
examples.chinese: 如果销售额继续上升，今年的产量可能达到50万的目标。

examples.english: Analysts say that the value of the Euro could hit rock bottom in the next few months.
examples.chinese: 分析师说接下来的几个月，欧元的币值可能降至最低点。

examples.english: As oil production increased, prices hit an all-time low.
examples.chinese: 随着石油产量的增加，油价跌至前所未有的低点。

expressions.text: attain a height/speed etc of something

examples.english: When migrating, birds may attain a height of three thousand metres or more.
examples.chinese: 鸟在迁移时可飞至3,000米或更高的高度。

examples.english: The latest model is capable of attaining speeds in excess of 300 kph.
examples.chinese: 最新的型号时速能达到300公里以上。

examples.english: Share prices attained a high of $3.27.
examples.chinese: 股价高达3.27美元。

examples.english: The plane touched the speed of sound in a power dive.
examples.chinese: 飞机在俯冲骤降时达到了音速。

examples.english: Yesterday, the dollar touched a seventeen-week high of 1.4748 marks.
examples.chinese: 昨天美元升至17周来的最高点，1美元兑1.4748马克。

centers.english: when someone can reach something

examples.english: Can you get that book down for me? I can't reach.
examples.chinese: 你能帮我把那本书拿下来吗？我够不着。

examples.english: There's no point in having a shelf so high that you can't reach it.
examples.chinese: 要一个高得够不着的架子是没有意义的。

examples.english: She had fallen and broken her ankle and couldn't get to the phone.
examples.chinese: 她跌倒摔伤了膝盖，够不到电话。

examples.english: Can you get to your coffee, if I put it here?
examples.chinese: 我把咖啡放在这里的话，你够得到吗？

centers.english: to reach a place that you are travelling to

examples.english: We didn't reach the hotel until midnight.
examples.chinese: 直到午夜时分我们才到达旅馆。

examples.english: Some letters are taking up to two weeks to reach their destination.
examples.chinese: 一些信件要花两个星期才能送达目的地。

examples.english: In winter, parts of Northern Canada can only be reached by plane.
examples.chinese: 在冬天，加拿大北部的一些地方只有乘飞机才能到达。

examples.english: By the time we got to New York, it was snowing.
examples.chinese: 我们到达纽约时，已经开始下雪了。

examples.english: What time did you get home last night?
examples.chinese: 昨晚你什么时候到家的?

examples.english: We only got as far as the end of the road, then the car broke down.
examples.chinese: 我们才开到路口，汽车就抛锚了。

examples.english: The roads were so bad that I wasn't sure we would make it.
examples.chinese: 路况这么糟，我都不肯定我们能够到达。

examples.english: If we run, we should be able to make it before the bus leaves.
examples.chinese: 我们跑的话，应该能在公共汽车开走前赶上。

examples.english: Even though he couldn't swim, he managed to make it to the riverbank.
examples.chinese: 他虽然不会游泳，还是想办法到了河岸边。

examples.english: Thousands of refugees made it across the border.
examples.chinese: 数千名难民设法越过了边境。

examples.english: We'll be lucky if we make San Fernando by nightfall.
examples.chinese: 我们天黑前能赶到圣费尔南多就算幸运了。

examples.english: The team aimed to make the South Pole and back in a month.
examples.chinese: 考察组的目标是在一个月之内往返南极。

examples.english: Heavy rains have prevented food supplies from getting through.
examples.chinese: 下大雨使食品供应没法送达。

examples.english: Rescue teams finally got through to the survivors by digging a tunnel.
examples.chinese: 营救小组挖了一条地道，终于到了幸存者的身边。

examples.english: We chose to live in this area because both New York and Boston are easily accessible from here.
examples.chinese: 我们选择住在这个地区，是因为从这里到纽约和波士顿都很方便。

examples.english: The banks of the River Holbeck are easily accessible to walkers and anglers.
examples.chinese: 步行者和垂钓者要到霍尔贝克河的河岸非常方便。

examples.english: Because of the snow, many parts of the countryside are only accessible by helicopter.
examples.chinese: 由于下雪，郊外的许多地区只有乘直升机才能到达。

centers.english: a place that is difficult or impossible to reach
centers.chinese: 很难或无法到达的地方

examples.english: The country consists mainly of dense jungles and inaccessible mountain ranges.
examples.chinese: 该国主要是茂密的丛林和难以攀越的山脉。

examples.english: The bathroom is situated at the top of a flight of stairs, making it inaccessible to the disabled.
examples.chinese: 浴室设在一段楼梯的顶层，这使残疾人士难以到达。

centers.english: to enter a place

examples.english: It was getting cold, so we went in.
examples.chinese: 天冷起来，我们就进屋了。

examples.english: There was a man at the door trying to stop people from going in.
examples.chinese: 门口有个人试图拦着人们不让进去。

examples.english: Don't go in my room - it's a mess.
examples.chinese: 不要进我房间，里面很乱。

examples.english: Make sure you wipe your feet before you go into the house.
examples.chinese: 进屋前一定要擦擦脚。

examples.english: That must be Nina coming in right now.
examples.chinese: 现在一定是尼娜进来了。

examples.english: As soon as Adrian came in, everyone stopped talking.
examples.chinese: 阿德里安一进来，大家就不说话了。！

examples.english: Why don't you come in the house for a little while and get warmed up.
examples.chinese: 你为什么不进屋待一会儿来暖暖身子呢。

examples.english: When you first come into the building, you'll see the elevators just across the lobby.
examples.chinese: 一进大楼，你就能看到穿过大厅就是电梯。

examples.english: You need a ticket to enter.
examples.chinese: 你需要有票才能进去。

examples.english: The army entered the city from the north.
examples.chinese: 军队从北面进城。

examples.english: As soon as he entered the room, he knew there was something wrong.
examples.chinese: 他一进房间就知道，有什么不对劲了。

examples.english: We queued in the rain for two hours and still didn't get in.
examples.chinese: 我们在雨中排了两小时的队，可仍没进去。

examples.english: You usually have to wait a while before you can get in the club.
examples.chinese: 一般要等一段时间才能进入俱乐部。

examples.english: You shouldn't have any trouble getting into the concert - they've only sold half the tickets.
examples.chinese: 你要进去听音乐会应该不会有什么问题—他们只卖出一半的门票。

examples.english: Brown gained admission by claiming to be a newspaper photographer.
examples.chinese: 布朗自称是报社的摄影记者，于是获准进入。

examples.english: We had to talk to several guards to gain admission to the courtyard.
examples.chinese: 我们跟好几个卫兵商量后才获准进入院子。

examples.english: Two men with guns burst in and told us to lie on the floor.
examples.chinese: 两名持枪男子闯进来叫我们躺在地上。

examples.english: I ran back to Iris's and burst in on Polly who was ironing in the kitchen.
examples.chinese: 我跑回艾里斯的家，冲向正在厨房熨衣服的波莉。

examples.english: Lotty burst into the room waving a letter in the air.
examples.chinese: 洛蒂扬着一封信冲进房间。

examples.english: I was studying when Ben suddenly barged in.
examples.chinese: 我正在学习，本突然闯了进来。

examples.english: It's impossible to concentrate when people keep barging in on you.
examples.chinese: 老是有人闯进来打扰你，你便不能集中精力。

examples.english: Some of the strikers came barging into the meeting and demanded to speak with the directors.
examples.chinese: 一些罢工者闯进会场要求和董事谈话。

examples.english: I waited until everybody was sitting quietly before making my entrance.
examples.chinese: 我一直等到大家都静静地坐好之后才入场。

examples.english: She walked slowly down the staircase, making a grand entrance.
examples.chinese: 她缓缓走下楼梯，大模大样地登场。

examples.english: Katie breezes in at eleven o'clock each morning, two hours late.
examples.chinese: 凯蒂每天上午迟到两个小时，到11点才不慌不忙地进来。

examples.english: Giles just breezed into the office, used the phone, and then breezed out again.
examples.chinese: 贾尔斯飘然走进办公室，打完电话之后又飘然地出去了。

centers.english: to tell someone that they can come into your house, room etc
centers.chinese: 叫某人进你的家、你的房间等

examples.english: Come in and sit down. I'll be ready in a minute.
examples.chinese: 进来坐，我马上就准备好。

examples.english: Marge, it's so good to see you! Come in! Come in!
examples.chinese: 玛吉，见到你太好了！进来！进来！

examples.english: ‘Come in,’ she said in answer to my second knock.
examples.chinese: “进来。”我敲了第二下门时她答道。

examples.english: Stella didn't know whether to ask him in or not.
examples.chinese: 斯特拉不知道要不要请他进来。

examples.english: A salesman came around this morning and I made the mistake of inviting him in.
examples.chinese: 今天上午来了一个推销员，我犯了个错误，把他请了进来。

expressions.text: ask sb in/invite sb in for

examples.english: She seemed so upset, I felt I had to ask her in for a cup of tea.
examples.chinese: 她好像非常难过，我觉得一定要叫她进来喝杯茶。

examples.english: Hi! Come on in! Can I fix you something to drink?
examples.chinese: 嗨，进来吧！我给你弄点喝的，好吗?

examples.english: ‘Mike, could I talk with you a minute?’ ‘Sure, come on in.’
examples.chinese: “迈克，我能和你谈谈吗？”“行啊，进来吧。”

centers.english: to enter somewhere quietly or secretly
centers.chinese: 悄悄地或偷偷地进入一个地方

examples.english: When he was drunk he would sneak in late, hoping his wife was asleep.
examples.chinese: 他喝醉了就会到很晚才偷偷地溜进房间，指望妻子已经睡着了。

examples.english: He had a passion for bebop and was sneaking into jazz clubs at age 14.
examples.chinese: 他热爱比博普音乐，14岁就偷偷地溜进一些爵士乐俱乐部听演奏。

examples.english: We wanted to sneak my dad in, so my mom wouldn't see.
examples.chinese: 我们想帮爸爸溜进去不让妈妈看到。

examples.english: Maggie opened the door silently and slipped in.
examples.chinese: 玛吉轻轻打开门，溜了进去。

examples.english: A few latecomers had slipped into the room and were standing at the back of the audience.
examples.chinese: 一些晚来的观众溜进房间，站在观众席后面。

centers.english: to enter a place illegally or by using force
centers.chinese: 非法或强行进入一个地方

examples.english: A man was arrested for trying to enter the actress's Beverly Hills home.
examples.chinese: 一名男子因为企图进入这位女演员在贝弗利山的住宅而遭到拘捕。

examples.english: It appears the burglars entered through a back window.
examples.chinese: 窃贼看来是从后窗进来的。

examples.english: How did you get in? I thought the door was locked.
examples.chinese: 你是怎么闯进来的？我以为门锁了呢。…

examples.english: Some animals hadn't gotten in the shed and made a mess.
examples.chinese: 不知道是什么动物进了小屋弄得一团糟。

examples.english: Thieves had apparently got into the apartments by posing as electricians.
examples.chinese: 小偷显然是假扮成电工混进公寓的。

examples.english: If anyone tries to break in, the alarm will go off.
examples.chinese: 如果有人想入室行窃，警报系统就会自动响起。

examples.english: Thieves broke into the gallery and made off with paintings valued at over $2 million.
examples.chinese: 窃贼闯入画廊，盗走了价值二百多万美元的画作。

examples.english: The police gained entry by smashing down the door.
examples.chinese: 警察砸开门冲了进去。

examples.english: Somehow the woman had gained access to his dressing room and was waiting there when he came off the stage.
examples.chinese: 那名女子不知用什么办法进入了他的化妆室，在他走下舞台的时候正在那里等着他。

examples.english: They've blocked the door. We'll have to force our way in.
examples.chinese: 他们把门封了，我们得破门进去。

examples.english: Police eventually forced their way into the building and arrested the gunman.
examples.chinese: 警方最后强行闯进大楼，逮捕了那名持枪歹徒。

examples.english: You can't just go into his apartment when he's not there - that's breaking and entering.
examples.chinese: 你不可以在他不在的时候随便就进入他的寓所——那是破门侵入。

examples.english: He was caught in the school at night and has been charged with breaking and entering.
examples.chinese: 晚上他在学校被人抓住，并被控以破门侵入的罪名。

examples.english: Get out of the yard! Can't you see the sign? It says ‘No Trespassing.’
examples.chinese: 从院子里出去！难道你没看到这块牌子吗？上面写着“禁止闯入”。

examples.english: Carlson was fined $1000 for trespassing on government property.
examples.chinese: 卡尔森由于擅自闯入政府属地而被罚款1,000美元。

examples.english: Trespassers will be prosecuted.
examples.chinese: 非法侵入者将受到起诉。

examples.english: The barbed wire fences and security shields made the air base very difficult to penetrate.
examples.chinese: 带刺的铁丝网和安全防御设施使空军基地难以突破。

centers.english: to enter a country

examples.english: Everyone entering the country must show a passport.
examples.chinese: 凡入境者都必须出示护照。

examples.english: The barbed wire fences and mine fields are designed to stop people leaving or entering.
examples.chinese: 带刺的铁丝网和雷区是为了阻止人们进出而设的。

examples.english: Congress is considering raising the number of skilled workers who may enter the country each year.
examples.chinese: 国会正在考虑增加每年进入本国的技术工人的人数。

examples.english: Many people cross the border illegally in search of work.
examples.chinese: 许多人非法过境寻找工作。

examples.english: Every day more and more desperate refugees were crossing the border into Kenya.
examples.chinese: 每天有越来越多绝望的难民越过边界进入肯尼亚。

examples.english: Her father immigrated to America from China in 1947.
examples.chinese: 1947年，她父亲从中国移民到美国。

examples.english: Born in Jamaica, Rigby had immigrated to England 30 years before.
examples.chinese: 里格比出生于牙买加，30年前移民到英格兰。

examples.english: Most immigration to New York City has been from the Caribbean, Europe, and Asia.
examples.chinese: 纽约市大多数移民来自加勒比海地区、欧洲和亚洲。

centers.english: someone who enters another country

examples.english: The new immigrants come mainly from Asia and Latin America.
examples.chinese: 新移民主要来自亚洲和拉丁美洲。

examples.english: Jae Min's parents are immigrants from South Korea.
examples.chinese: 闵才的父母是韩国移民。

examples.english: The winery was started by an Italian immigrant to California.
examples.chinese: 这个酒厂是一个从意大利移民来加州的人创办的。

examples.english: The bill would have cut off government aid even to legal immigrants.
examples.chinese: 这个议案甚至削减了对合法移民的政府补助。

examples.english: The government has been unable to provide enough tents for all the refugees.
examples.chinese: 政府没有能力提供足够的帐篷给所有的难民。

examples.english: Most of the refugees from the former war zone have now been sent back.
examples.chinese: 从以前战区来的大部分难民都已遣返。

examples.english: Britain has traditionally been a safe haven for political refugees.
examples.chinese: 英国一向是政治难民的安全避难所。

examples.english: Too often asylum-seekers are treated like criminals.
examples.chinese: 寻求避难的人往往受到罪犯一样的对待。

examples.english: Officially recognized asylum-seekers cannot be deported.
examples.chinese: 经官方承认的避难者不得驱逐出境。

examples.english: Most people in the UK believe that immigration has enriched the economy and national culture.
examples.chinese: 英国大多数人认为移民繁荣了经济，丰富了民族文化。

examples.english: Immigration reached its peak in the 1950s.
examples.chinese: 20世纪50年代移民达到了高峰。

examples.english: Immigration officials stopped and arrested the man at JFK airport.
examples.chinese: 移民官员在肯尼迪机场截住那人将他逮捕。

centers.english: when a large number of people enter a country, place, city etc
centers.chinese: 大量的人进入某个国家、地方、城市等

examples.english: Once the region was declared safe, tourists started flooding in again.
examples.chinese: 这一地区一旦被宣布安全，游客又开始大量涌入了。

examples.english: An estimated 50,000 people poured into London over the weekend for the opening of the Commonwealth Games.
examples.chinese: 据估计，周末有50,000人涌入伦敦，观看英联邦运动会的开幕式。

examples.english: People kept crowding in, and one woman started to panic.
examples.chinese: 人们不断地挤进来，一名妇女惊慌了起来。

examples.english: More than 100 people crowded into the fire station for Thursday night's council meeting.
examples.chinese: 有一百多人拥进消防站参加星期四晚上的理事会议。

examples.english: After the game they all trooped in to eat.
examples.chinese: 比赛结束后，他们都一起进去吃饭。

examples.english: Every morning we had to troop into the school hall for roll call.
examples.chinese: 每天早晨我们都必须列队去学校礼堂点名。

examples.english: A few fans had already started to trickle in.
examples.chinese: 一些球迷已开始陆续进场了。

examples.english: The bell rang, and the students trickled into the class.
examples.chinese: 上课铃响了，学生们陆陆续续地走进教室。

examples.english: The influx of migrants to the city is estimated at 1,000 per week.
examples.chinese: 每个星期估计有1,000名移民涌入本市。

examples.english: The sudden influx of families needing work and housing caused some problems at first.
examples.chinese: 既需要工作又需要住房的家庭突然大量地涌入，在开始的时候造成了一定的困难。

examples.english: The company has employed a number of new staff to cope with the flood of visitors to the site.
examples.chinese: 公司雇用了一些新员工来应付涌至该地的大批游客。

examples.english: A flood of refugees poured over the bridge to escape the fighting.
examples.chinese: 大批难民拥着过桥，躲避战争。

centers.english: to let someone enter a place

examples.english: Let me in! It's freezing out here.
examples.chinese: 让我进去！外面冷得要命。

examples.english: There's Ryan at the door. Let him in, would you?
examples.chinese: 瑞安在门口，你让他进来好吗?

examples.english: Who let those guys in? They don't belong here.
examples.chinese: 谁让那些家伙进来的？他们不是这里的人。

examples.english: Don't let anybody in the house while I'm gone.
examples.chinese: 我不在的时候不要让任何人进来。

examples.english: His girlfriend was there and let me into the apartment.
examples.chinese: 他的女朋友在，并让我进了公寓。

examples.english: Children under 17 will not be admitted.
examples.chinese: 17岁以下的少年儿童不得入内。

examples.english: They refused to admit Paul to the performance because of what he was wearing.
examples.chinese: 他们因为保罗的衣着而不肯让他进去看演出。

centers.english: to not let someone enter

examples.english: He bought a new security system to keep out intruders.
examples.chinese: 他买了一个新的安保装置防止有人非法闯入。

examples.english: Family members can go in to visit him, but we need to keep everyone else out.
examples.chinese: 家属可以进去探望他，但是其他人进去我们都要阻止。

examples.english: Try to keep Ed out of the bedroom while I finish wrapping his present.
examples.chinese: 我在给埃德包礼物时你不要让他进卧室。

examples.english: He slammed the door, shutting out the dogs.
examples.chinese: 他砰地一下关上门，把几条狗都关在了门外。

examples.english: John shut everybody out of the kitchen so that he could prepare his grand surprise.
examples.chinese: 约翰把大家都关在厨房外面，以便准备一个大大的惊喜。

examples.english: Her husband threw her out of the trailer without shoes or clothes and locked her out.
examples.chinese: 她丈夫把她从拖车式活动房屋里赶出来，把她光着脚光着身子锁在门外。

examples.english: I can't believe I locked myself out of the house again.
examples.chinese: 我真不敢相信，我又把自己锁在门外了。

examples.english: Immigration officials refused her entry because they thought she was planning to stay.
examples.chinese: 移民局官员禁止她入境，因为他们认为她打算长期居留。

examples.english: The management reserves the right to refuse entry to anyone who is improperly dressed.
examples.chinese: 管理层保留拒绝衣冠不整者进入公司的权利。

examples.english: Hundreds of disappointed fans were turned away at the gates.
examples.chinese: 数百名球迷被拒之门外，满心失望。

examples.english: The club's so popular, we have to turn people away every night.
examples.chinese: 俱乐部非常受欢迎，每天晚上我们都不得不回绝一些人。

examples.english: The tavern banned Ted for starting a fight.
examples.chinese: 这家酒吧因为特德挑衅打架而禁止他进去。

examples.english: We've had to bar visitors from the garden because some of the pathways aren't safe.
examples.chinese: 我们不得不禁止游客进入花园，因为有些小路不太安全。

examples.english: After the incident at the country club, Chuck was banned for life.
examples.chinese: 在乡村俱乐部发生这一事件之后，查克被终生禁止入内。

centers.english: somewhere where you enter

examples.english: It took us ages to find the entrance to the park.
examples.chinese: 我们费了好长时间才找到公园的入口处。

examples.english: Davis used a side entrance to avoid the waiting reporters.
examples.chinese: 戴维斯从一个侧门进去，以躲开等候的记者。

examples.english: Over the entryway was an inscription in Latin.
examples.chinese: 入口处的上方有一块拉丁文的铭文。

examples.english: We stopped at the entry to the church to admire the architecture.
examples.chinese: 我们在教堂门口停下来，欣赏这座建筑。

examples.english: We walked all the way around the museum looking for the way in.
examples.chinese: 我们绕着博物馆走了一整圈，寻找入口。！

examples.english: There's a red flashing sign above the door saying ‘Way In’ - you can't miss it.
examples.chinese: 门的上方有个红色闪光标牌，上面写着“入口”二字，你肯定能看到。

examples.english: The entrance has been widened to give improved access for disabled people.
examples.chinese: 入口已加宽了，方便残疾人士进去。

examples.english: City officials are considering building a path to give the public access to the ruins.
examples.chinese: 市府官员正在考虑修建一条小路，以便公众能进入这个废墟。

centers.english: when something enters a space, especially through a surface
centers.chinese: 某物进入某处，尤指通过表面

examples.english: The bullet entered his rib cage from the left side.
examples.chinese: 子弹从左侧穿入他的胸腔。

examples.english: Bacteria can enter through a cut or graze on the skin.
examples.chinese: 细菌可从皮肤上割伤或擦伤的地方进入体内。

examples.english: The sun's rays can penetrate the sea to a depth of twenty metres.
examples.chinese: 太阳光线可照射到大海20米深处。

examples.english: He threw a grenade that penetrated the wall of the building and exploded inside.
examples.chinese: 他扔的那颗手榴弹穿过大楼的墙壁，在楼内发生了爆炸。

examples.english: Despite all our efforts to stop it, the floodwater was still seeping in.
examples.chinese: 尽管我们全力阻止，洪水依旧在漫进来。

examples.english: Chemicals from the plant have seeped into the city's water supply.
examples.chinese: 工厂的化学物已经渗入城市的供水系统。

examples.english: Toxic chemicals may permeate the soil, threatening the environment.
examples.chinese: 有毒化学品会渗入土壤，对环境构成威胁。

centers.english: to meet someone who you have arranged to meet

examples.english: I'll meet you outside the theatre at 7 o'clock.
examples.chinese: 我7点在戏院外面与你见面。

examples.english: The two leaders are scheduled to meet again next month to continue the peace talks.
examples.chinese: 双方领导人按计划将于下个月再次会晤，继续进行和谈。

examples.english: I used to meet her every week to discuss my work.
examples.chinese: 以前我每周都和她见面讨论我的工作情况。

examples.english: Meet me back here in half an hour - I just need to finish up a couple of things.
examples.chinese: 半小时之后回到这里和我碰头，我只需要把几件事处理完。

examples.english: Let's meet for lunch one day next week.
examples.chinese: 下周我们定个日子一起吃午饭吧。

examples.english: She's flying to New York tomorrow to meet with her agent.
examples.chinese: 明天她要乘飞机去纽约和代理人见面。

examples.english: The board of directors is meeting with representatives of the union tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 明天董事会要和工会代表会晤。

examples.english: Supervisors should meet with their employees at least every other week to share information.
examples.chinese: 主管至少应该每两个星期和员工见一次面交换信息。

examples.english: Let's meet up after work.
examples.chinese: 下班后我们聚一聚吧。

examples.english: The past few years, our family has only gotten together at Christmas.
examples.chinese: 过去几年里我们一家人只是在圣诞节才聚在一起。

examples.english: I usually meet up with my friends on a Friday night and go for a drink.
examples.chinese: 我和朋友们一般在周五晚上见面去喝一杯。

expressions.text: meet up/get together for lunch/coffee/a drink

examples.english: We must get together for lunch some time.
examples.chinese: 我们一定要找个时间聚一聚吃顿午餐。

examples.english: Dr Thomas is seeing a client at 2:30.
examples.chinese: 托马斯医生两点半要见一个病人。

examples.english: I don't think I can see you this afternoon. How about tomorrow?
examples.chinese: 今天下午我无法见你。明天怎么样？

examples.english: ‘I've come to see Mr. Greene about a job,’ he said nervously.
examples.chinese: “我来见格林先生，是找工作的事。”他胆怯地说道。

examples.english: We hooked up for lunch at Toscana in Brentwood.
examples.chinese: 我们在布伦特伍德的托斯卡纳碰头一起吃午饭。

examples.english: Matt and I went out for a drink and hooked up with Janet later on.
examples.chinese: 马特和我出去喝酒，稍后再与珍妮特碰头。

centers.english: to meet someone by chance

examples.english: I met Jill at the bus stop this morning.
examples.chinese: 今天早上我在公共汽车站碰见了吉尔。

examples.english: You'll never guess who I met yesterday!
examples.chinese: 你绝对猜不到我昨天遇见了谁！

examples.english: Ron's changed so much, you probably wouldn't recognize him if you met him on the street.
examples.chinese: 罗恩变化太大了，你在街上遇到他也许都认不出了。

examples.english: I'm glad I bumped into you. I wanted to ask you about tomorrow's history test.
examples.chinese: 我很高兴碰见你，我想问问你明天历史测验的事。

examples.english: I'm sorry I'm late - I ran into an old colleague I hadn't seen for ages.
examples.chinese: 很抱歉我来晚了，我遇见一个多年未见的老同事。

examples.english: She's always running into friends that she knows from school.
examples.chinese: 她老是碰上以前学校里的朋友。

examples.english: I saw Penny in town today.
examples.chinese: 今天我在城里遇到了彭妮。

examples.english: If you see Ted, could you tell him I want to talk to him?
examples.chinese: 如果你遇见特德，告诉他我想和他谈谈行吗？

examples.english: It was rare that she encountered interesting people through her work.
examples.chinese: 她在工作中很少遇到有意思的人。

examples.english: He encountered the young woman as she was leaving a coffee shop.
examples.chinese: 他和那个年轻女人相遇时，她正要离开咖啡厅。

examples.english: The conference provided some excellent opportunities for networking and she made some useful business contacts.
examples.chinese: 此次大会提供了极佳的与同行联系的机会，帮她建立了一些有价值的业务关系。

centers.english: when you meet someone for the first time

examples.english: Have you ever met his wife?
examples.chinese: 你和他妻子认识吗?

examples.english: I was 15 years old when I met Andrew.
examples.chinese: 我15岁时认识了安德鲁。

examples.english: Dave, meet my brother Tom.
examples.chinese: 戴恩，认识一下我的哥哥汤姆。

examples.english: ‘How did you two meet?’ ‘We were on the same exchange program in Madrid.’
examples.chinese: “你俩是怎么认识的？”“我们是马德里的同一个交换留学生项目中的成员。”

examples.english: I'd like you all to meet my girlfriend, Claudia.
examples.chinese: 我想让你们大家见见我的女友克劳迪娅。

examples.english: Where did you first meet Dr Steiner?
examples.chinese: 你最初遇见斯坦纳博士是在哪个地方？

examples.english: Janet and Pete first met at a mutual friend's cocktail party.
examples.chinese: 珍妮特与皮特的首次相遇是在双方都认识的一个朋友举办的鸡尾酒会上。

examples.english: ‘Hello, my name is Alan.’ ‘Hi, Alan. My name's Cindy. Nice to meet you.’
examples.chinese: “你好，我是艾伦。”“艾伦你好。我叫辛迪，见到你很高兴。”

examples.english: ‘Doug, I'd like you to meet my mother.’ ‘Oh, pleased to meet you, Mrs Haggerty.’
examples.chinese: “道格，我想介绍你认识一下我母亲。”“噢，哈格蒂太太，很高兴认识你。”

examples.english: Have you two been introduced?
examples.chinese: 有人给你们两个互相介绍过了吗？

examples.english: Yes, we were introduced last year at your party.
examples.chinese: 是啊，去年在你办的聚会上我们已经见过了。

examples.english: That's a friend of mine from college. Do you want me to introduce you to him?
examples.chinese: 那是我大学时的朋友。你要我把你介绍给他吗？

examples.english: Oh, Bob, let me introduce Rosie Webb, our new marketing manager.
examples.chinese: 噢，鲍勃，我来介绍一下罗茜·韦布，我们新来的营销部经理。

examples.english: Please allow me to introduce myself. I'm John Fetty, the head of business development.
examples.chinese: 请允许我自我介绍一下。我叫约翰·费蒂，贸易开发部的主管。

examples.english: ‘Sam, this is Julia - she's in college with me.’ ‘Hi, Julia, nice to meet you!’
examples.chinese: “萨姆，这位是朱莉娅，她是我的大学同学。”“你好，朱莉娅，认识你很高兴。”

examples.english: ‘Mom, Dad, this is my friend Matt.’ ‘Hello, Matt, we've heard a lot about you.’
examples.chinese: “妈、爸，这是我的朋友马特。”“你好，马特，我们已经听到很多有关你的事了。”

examples.english: I'm pleased to make your acquaintance.
examples.chinese: 我很高兴和你认识。

examples.english: After seeing the way Mr. Wyatt behaved at the party, I had little desire to make his acquaintance.
examples.chinese: 看到怀亚特先生在聚会上的所作所为之后，我就没有兴趣与他相识了。

centers.english: when a large group comes together in one place

examples.english: The book club meets on the first Thursday of every month.
examples.chinese: 读书俱乐部每月的第一个星期四集会。

examples.english: The bus trip leaves at 9 am - everyone should meet in front of the station at 8.30.
examples.chinese: 旅游车早上9点出发—大家应该在8点半在车站前面集合。

examples.english: A small crowd gathered to watch the fight.
examples.chinese: 一小群人聚在一起看打架。

examples.english: The family gathered on the porch to say goodbye.
examples.chinese: 全家人聚集在门廊里互相道别。

examples.english: Eager fans are already gathering outside the stadium.
examples.chinese: 热切的球迷已经开始在体育场外聚集起来。

examples.english: Prisoners must assemble in the courtyard every morning for exercise.
examples.chinese: 每天早晨囚犯必须在院子里集合做早操。

examples.english: Foreign diplomats and their wives had assembled in the Great Hall to meet the President.
examples.chinese: 外国的外交官和他们的夫人在大会堂集合与总统会面。

examples.english: People came together from all over the country to attend the funeral.
examples.chinese: 人们从全国各地来参加这个葬礼。

examples.english: Seminars provide an opportunity for students to come together and discuss a particular topic.
examples.chinese: 讨论会给学生们提供了一个聚到一起讨论某一题目的机会。

centers.english: to meet someone in order to take them somewhere
centers.chinese: 为带某人去某处而与其在某个地方见面

examples.english: We'll meet you at the airport and take you to your hotel.
examples.chinese: 我们会到机场来接你，然后送你去宾馆。

examples.english: The company is sending a car to meet Mr Hill at the station.
examples.chinese: 公司准备派车去车站接希尔先生。

examples.english: Our guide met us in the hotel lobby and spent the entire day with us.
examples.chinese: 我们的导游在旅馆大堂内接我们，然后一整天都陪着我们。

examples.english: She picks her daughter up from school every day at four.
examples.chinese: 她每天下午4点把女儿从学校接回来。

examples.english: I told Virginia I'd pick her up about 8:00.
examples.chinese: 我告诉弗吉尼娅我大约8点去接她。

examples.english: I arranged to pick up Mr Clarke and take him to his accommodation.
examples.chinese: 我已安排好去接克拉克先生，然后送他到他的住所去。

examples.english: Her father sent a taxi to collect her from the hotel.
examples.chinese: 她父亲叫了辆出租车去旅馆接她。

examples.english: If the last bus has left, I'll collect you.
examples.chinese: 如果最后一班公共汽车已经走了，那我来接你。

centers.english: a meeting in order to discuss something

examples.english: Sorry I can't come - I have to go to a meeting.
examples.chinese: 抱歉我不能来了一我得去参加个会议。

examples.english: Peter's in London for a business meeting.
examples.chinese: 彼得在伦敦参加一个商务会议。

examples.english: I was in meetings all morning and didn't get a chance to look at your proposal.
examples.chinese: 整个上午我都在开会，没有机会看你的建议。

examples.english: I'm having a meeting with my client tomorrow to go over the case.
examples.chinese: 明天我要和委托人见面研究一下案子。

examples.english: The committee will hold another meeting Wednesday to discuss the funding crisis.
examples.chinese: 星期三委员会将召开另一次会议来讨论资金紧缺的问题。

examples.english: According to people who attended the meeting, Ms. Robins refused to answer any questions about the deal.
examples.chinese: 据参加会议的人说，罗宾斯女士拒绝回答任何有关这笔交易的问题。

examples.english: The principal has called a meeting for 4.00.
examples.chinese: 院长召集4时开会。

examples.english: Didn't you give a talk at the conference last year?
examples.chinese: 去年的大会上你作过发言，对不对?

examples.english: She's an organizer of the International Conference on AIDS that the university has every year.
examples.chinese: 她是国际艾滋病大会的组织者，大会每年在该大学举行一次。

examples.english: Lewis recently spoke at a conference of women business leaders.
examples.chinese: 刘易斯最近在一次女性商界领袖讨论会上作了讲话。

examples.english: Baxter was in Boston attending a conference on the environment.
examples.chinese: 巴克斯特正在波士顿参加一个环境问题讨论会。

examples.english: The Institute of Accountants is holding its conference in Edinburgh this year.
examples.chinese: 会计师协会今年在爱丁堡召开大会。

examples.english: The Senator's speech at the Democratic Convention was well received.
examples.chinese: 参议员在民主党大会上的发言很受欢迎。

examples.english: Several hundred people are expected at the hotel next month for a huge sales convention.
examples.chinese: 估计有数百人参加下个月在这家酒店里举行的大型销售会议。

examples.english: Lofgren told a convention of church activists that she wanted the money to be spent on local childrens’ facilities.
examples.chinese: 洛夫格伦在教会积极分子大会上说，她希望这笔钱能用在当地儿童设施上。

examples.english: an annual convention of the world bank
examples.chinese: 世界银行年度大会

examples.english: The Reform Party will hold a national convention in August.
examples.chinese: 改革党将于8月份召开全国大会。

examples.english: A U.S.-Russia summit is expected to take place in late March.
examples.chinese: 3月底将召开美俄两国领导人峰会。

examples.english: A summit meeting of OPEC leaders was called to find a solution to the oil crisis.
examples.chinese: 石油输出国组织领导人召开了峰会以寻找石油危机的解决办法。

examples.english: NATO leaders are preparing for a summit conference to decide the future of the alliance.
examples.chinese: 北约领导人正在筹办一次首脑会议，以决定这一联盟的未来。

examples.english: The President will meet other Pacific Rim leaders at next week's economic summit.
examples.chinese: 总统将与其他的环太平洋地区国家领导人在下周的经济峰会上会晤。

examples.english: It was quite a small gathering but the speeches were excellent.
examples.chinese: 那是次规模相当小的集会，不过发表的演说却棒极了。

examples.english: They announced their engagement at a family gathering in Vermont.
examples.chinese: 他们在佛蒙特的一次家庭聚会上宣布订婚。

centers.english: an occasion when you meet someone, especially by chance
centers.chinese: 遇见某人，尤指偶然遇见某人的场合

examples.english: Our first meeting was in January, and I didn't see Martin again till May or June.
examples.chinese: 我们第一次相遇是在1月份，后来我直到5月或6月份才再次见到马丁。

examples.english: Her affair with Harmon started with a chance meeting followed by a few casual phone calls.
examples.chinese: 她与哈蒙的艳事最初是从一次偶遇开始的，然后随意地通了几次电话。

examples.english: He did not appear to remember our encounter last summer and just nodded when we were introduced.
examples.chinese: 他似乎不记得我们在去年夏天的相遇，当我们被别人介绍认识时，他只是点点头。

examples.english: A chance encounter in a restaurant led to her first movie role.
examples.chinese: 在餐厅中的一次偶遇使她第一次在电影中演出。

centers.english: to not meet someone even though you have arranged to
centers.chinese: 在已约定的情况下未与某人见面

examples.english: She spent the whole evening wondering why her date had stood her up.
examples.chinese: 她一晚上都在想为什么那个约会对象要让她空等。

examples.english: If he continues to stand up his friends, he's not going to have any left.
examples.chinese: 如果他继续让朋友空等，到头来就会一个朋友都没了。

expressions.text: not turn up/not show up/not show

examples.english: A few of us had arranged to meet in town, but Jenny didn't turn up.
examples.chinese: 我们几个人约好在城里见面，不过珍妮没有来。

examples.english: Peter always says he'll come, and then he just doesn't show up.
examples.chinese: 彼得总是说他会来，然后就不见人影。

examples.english: I waited for an hour but she didn't show.
examples.chinese: 我等了一小时，可她没有来。

centers.english: to climb up or down something

examples.english: Most kids love climbing trees.
examples.chinese: 大多数小孩子喜欢爬树。

examples.english: Trying not to look down, Alan began to climb.
examples.chinese: 艾伦开始往上爬，尽量不朝下看。

examples.english: Several fans climbed onto the roof of the arena to get a better view.
examples.chinese: 为了看得更清楚，一些球迷爬上了运动场的屋顶。

examples.english: One of the boys lost his footing as he was climbing up the steepest part of the cliff.
examples.chinese: 其中一名男孩在攀登悬崖最陡峭的地方时失足掉了下去。

examples.english: The burglar escaped by climbing down a drainpipe.
examples.chinese: 窃贼沿着雨水管爬下去，逃走了。

examples.english: Somehow the men had scaled the twenty-foot wall without setting off the alarm.
examples.chinese: 不知怎的，那几名男子爬上了高20英尺的墙却没有触动报警器。

examples.english: Rescuers had to scale a one-thousand-foot cliff before they could reach the injured climber.
examples.chinese: 援救者得爬上1,000英尺的悬崖，才到达受伤的登山者身边。

examples.english: At last we saw the two girls clambering down the slope to safety.
examples.chinese: 最后，我们看见那两个女孩爬下斜坡来到安全地点。

examples.english: Hundreds of people clambered to the roof of the building to watch the fire spread.
examples.chinese: 数百人爬上大楼楼顶去看肆虐的大火。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: I locked myself out of the house and had to shinny up a drainpipe to get in.
examples.chinese: 我把自己锁在了家门外，所以不得不沿着雨水管爬上去。

examples.english: We watched as small boys shinned up palm trees and brought coconuts down.
examples.chinese: 我们看着小男孩们爬到椰树上，把椰子摘了下来。

examples.english: Craig shinned down the rope to where we were standing.
examples.chinese: 克雷格从绳子上爬下来到我们站的地方。

examples.english: The men began their final ascent at six o'clock the next day.
examples.chinese: 第二天6点钟，那些男子开始了最后的攀登。

examples.english: He wrote a best-selling book about the first ascent of Everest.
examples.chinese: 他写了一本关于首次攀登珠穆朗玛峰的畅销书。

centers.english: someone who climbs hills or mountains as a sport
centers.chinese: 把爬山或登山作为一种运动的人

examples.english: The search is still continuing for a group of climbers reported missing in the Scottish highlands.
examples.chinese: 据报告有一群爬山者在苏格兰高地失踪，搜寻仍在继续。

examples.english: an experienced climber
examples.chinese: 一位有经验的登山者

examples.english: Most mountaineers dream of climbing Everest.
examples.chinese: 大多数登山运动员都梦想登上珠穆朗玛峰。

examples.english: Sports equipment stores in Alpine towns cater for the needs of walkers, hikers and mountaineers.
examples.chinese: 阿尔卑斯山区小镇上的体育用品商店满足了步行者、远足者和登山运动员的需要。

centers.english: climbing hills or mountains as a sport

examples.english: Eva's hobbies are horse-riding, climbing, and aerobics.
examples.chinese: 伊娃的爱好有骑马、爬山和有氧运动。

examples.english: strong climbing boots
examples.chinese: 结实的登山靴

examples.english: Accident insurance does not cover you for dangerous activities such as rock climbing.
examples.chinese: 意外保险责任范围并不包括你所从事的危险活动，譬如攀岩。

examples.english: I joined the mountaineering club when I went to university.
examples.chinese: 我上大学时参加了登山俱乐部。

topics.word: GET ON OR OFF A BUS, PLANE ETC

centers.english: to get on a bus, plane etc

examples.english: The train stopped in the middle of the night in Nogales. No one got on or off.
examples.chinese: 半夜里火车停靠在诺加莱斯，没有一个人上下车。

examples.english: We got on the train at Lime Street Station.
examples.chinese: 我们在莱姆街站上了火车。

examples.english: Get on a number 73 bus at the corner. That will take you to Islington High Street.
examples.chinese: 在拐角的地方乘73路公共汽车可以到伊斯灵顿大街。

examples.english: She's old and needs help getting on and off the bus.
examples.chinese: 她年纪大了，上下公共汽车需要帮助。

examples.english: ‘Can you give me a lift into town?’ ‘Sure. Get in.’
examples.chinese: “可以让我搭你的车进城吗？”“可以，上来吧。”

examples.english: Be careful getting into the boat.
examples.chinese: 上船要小心。

examples.english: I got in a taxi and went to the hospital immediately.
examples.chinese: 我坐上出租车立刻去了医院。

examples.english: Stephen caught the 6.15 to Birmingham.
examples.chinese: 斯蒂芬乘了6点15分的火车去伯明翰。

examples.english: If we're quick, we should still be able to catch our train.
examples.chinese: 如果我们快点，应该还能赶上火车。

examples.english: Jonathan looked at his watch. ‘I've got a plane to catch,’ he said.
examples.chinese: 乔纳森看了看手表。“我要赶飞机。”他说道。

examples.english: Karl hopped on the first plane back to Germany.
examples.chinese: 卡尔搭乘第一班飞机回了德国。

examples.english: ‘Hop in,’ shouted Lucy, throwing open the door of her van.
examples.chinese: “快上来，”露西喊道，猛地推开小型货车的车门。

examples.english: She hopped into a cab and told the driver to go to King's Cross.
examples.chinese: 她跳上一辆出租车，告诉司机去国王十字路口。

examples.english: ‘Why not hop in the car and come with us?’ Myrtle asked with a sly smile.
examples.chinese: “怎么不上车和我们一起去呀？”默特尔狡黠地笑着问道。

examples.english: A week later he boarded a ship bound for New York.
examples.chinese: 一周以后，他登上了去纽约的轮船。

examples.english: Before boarding the plane, Jenny tried once more to call home.
examples.chinese: 珍妮在登机之前又试着打了一次电话回家。

examples.english: A large group had assembled at the pier, waiting to embark.
examples.chinese: 有一大群人聚集在码头上等着上船。

examples.english: Their training completed, the regiment embarked for the war zone.
examples.chinese: 训练结束之后，该团登船奔赴战区。

examples.english: Embarkation will begin one hour before the ship is due to leave port.
examples.chinese: 轮船预定离开港口前一小时开始上船。

examples.english: Can you help Shelly mount?
examples.chinese: 你帮助谢利上马好吗？

examples.english: He mounted the pony and rode off.
examples.chinese: 他跨上小马驹，然后骑走了。

examples.english: It was time to get on board the ship.
examples.chinese: 是时候上船了。

examples.english: A group of men, some in military uniform, climbed aboard the plane.
examples.chinese: 一群男子，有些还穿着军服，爬上了飞机。

examples.english: The ship's crew saluted the President as he came aboard.
examples.chinese: 总统登上船时，船员向他致敬。

centers.english: to get off a bus, plane etc

examples.english: Tell the driver you want to get off at Greene Street.
examples.chinese: 跟司机说你要在格林街下车。

examples.english: Lennox got off the plane and made his way through customs.
examples.chinese: 伦诺克斯走下飞机，通过了海关。

examples.english: At the foot of the hill, she got off her bicycle and began to push it.
examples.chinese: 到了山脚下，她从自行车上下来，开始推着走。

examples.english: Do you know where we're supposed to get off?
examples.chinese: 你知道我们应该在哪里下车吗？

examples.english: Most of the passengers got out at Oxford Circus.
examples.chinese: 大多数乘客都在牛津广场下车。

examples.english: She got out of the car and slammed the door.
examples.chinese: 她下了车，砰的一声关上车门。

examples.english: The farmer got out of his car to open the gate.
examples.chinese: 那农民下车去开门。

examples.english: When you leave the train, please make sure that you have all your belongings with you.
examples.chinese: 下火车时请拿好随身携带的物品。

examples.english: Passengers leaving the ship at Alexandria should proceed to the immigration office.
examples.chinese: 在亚历山大下船的旅客必须去移民局接受检查。

examples.english: We weren't allowed to disembark until an hour after the ship had docked.
examples.chinese: 我们在轮船停靠码头一小时后才可以下船。

examples.english: The only one to disembark at Tiree was me.
examples.chinese: 在泰里岛下船的只有我一个人。

examples.english: They dismounted and led their horses through the forest.
examples.chinese: 他们下了马，牵着马穿过森林。

centers.english: moving or travelling fast

examples.english: Don't drive so fast - there's ice on the road.
examples.chinese: 别开得这么快，路面上有冰。

examples.english: The new fighter aircraft flies almost twice as fast as the old one.
examples.chinese: 这种新式战斗机飞行速度比老式的几乎快一倍。

examples.english: She walked faster and faster, then started to run.
examples.chinese: 她越走越快，然后开始跑了起来。

examples.english: He ran home as fast as he could.
examples.chinese: 他用最快的速度朝家里奔去。

examples.english: When I was a kid, I was the fastest boy in my class.
examples.chinese: 我小时候是班上跑得最快的男孩。

examples.english: Come quick, your brother's on TV.
examples.chinese: 快来，你弟弟上电视了。

examples.english: You'll have to walk quicker than that if you want to keep up with me.
examples.chinese: 如果你想跟上我就得走快一些。

examples.english: The horses ran along the track at a swift trot.
examples.chinese: 马在跑道上飞跑。

examples.english: We had to steer our ship through the swift currents of the Bering Straits.
examples.chinese: 我们必须驾船驶过白令海峡的急流。

examples.english: White clumps of cloud were moving swiftly across the sky.
examples.chinese: 朵朵白云在空中迅速飘动着。

examples.english: These animals are unrivalled for their grace and swiftness.
examples.chinese: 这些动物的优雅与速度无与伦比。

examples.english: Two cars raced past him at high speed.
examples.chinese: 两辆汽车从他身边高速驶过。

examples.english: a metal disk revolving at high speed
examples.chinese: 一张正在高速转动的金属盘

examples.english: You can travel by high-speed train from Paris to Brussels.
examples.chinese: 你可乘坐高速火车从巴黎去布鲁塞尔。

examples.english: a high-speed drill
examples.chinese: 高速钻孔机

examples.english: They headed east at top speed in pursuit of the enemy ship.
examples.chinese: 他们全速向东行驶去追击敌舰。

examples.english: At top speed, the Pave Hawk helicopter can travel 221 miles per hour.
examples.chinese: 铺路鹰直升机最高飞行时速可达每小时221英里。

examples.english: It took us an hour to get there, driving at breakneck speed.
examples.chinese: 我们用一小时到了那里，一路上开车速度快得危险。

examples.english: Jenny stepped onto her skis and sped off at breakneck speed down the glistening, white mountain.
examples.chinese: 珍妮踩上滑雪板飞速往银光闪闪、白茫茫的山下冲去。

examples.english: Going flat out, the BMW 325 will reach a speed of 140 miles per hour.
examples.chinese: 如果用全速驾驶，宝马325的速度能达到每小时140英里。

examples.english: The exercise involved running flat out for two minutes and then resting for one minute.
examples.chinese: 这项训练包括全速跑两分钟，然后休息一分钟。

examples.english: Somewhere a dog barked and, like lightning, the cat darted into some bushes.
examples.chinese: 不知哪个地方有只狗一吠，猫就像闪电般冲入了灌木丛。

examples.english: Her foot slipped on the smooth tiles but Mitch moved like lightning and caught her before she fell.
examples.chinese: 她脚底在光滑的瓷砖上滑了一下，但米奇闪电般作出反应，在她摔倒前抓住了她。

examples.english: The train was already travelling at speed when she tried to open the carriage door.
examples.chinese: 她试图打开车厢门时，火车已经在快速行驶了。

centers.english: fast cars/planes/trains

examples.english: Dean always loved fast cars and expensive clothes.
examples.chinese: 迪安总是喜欢高速的汽车和昂贵的衣服。

examples.english: Rosa caught the fast train to London.
examples.chinese: 罗莎赶上了去伦敦的高速列车。

examples.english: Boeing's new plane is faster and more luxurious than anything else they have ever produced.
examples.chinese: 波音公司的新式飞机比他们以前生产的任何飞机都更快、更豪华。

examples.english: The era of high-speed jet travel began after the end of World War II.
examples.chinese: 第二次世界大战结束后，高速喷气式飞机旅行的时代就开始了。

examples.english: Journey times have been reduced considerably since the introduction of high-speed trains.
examples.chinese: 自从启用了高速列车之后，旅程时间大大减少了。

examples.english: Concorde is capable of travelling at supersonic speeds.
examples.chinese: 协和式飞机能超音速飞行。!

examples.english: Thanks to supersonic travel, busy executives can attend meetings in New York and be back in London the same day.
examples.chinese: 全靠超音速旅行，事务繁忙的管理人员能去纽约参加会议，并于当天回到伦敦。

centers.english: doing things quickly or happening quickly
centers.chinese: 迅速地做事或迅速发生

examples.english: She undressed quickly and got into bed.
examples.chinese: 她很快脱了衣服上床了。

examples.english: It's important to realize how quickly this disease can spread.
examples.chinese: 知道这种疾病的传播速度有多快是很重要的。

examples.english: Quickly fry the onions, then add the meat.
examples.chinese: 把洋葱迅速煎一下然后加肉。

examples.english: I'll just take a quick shower.
examples.chinese: 我很快洗个淋浴就好了。

examples.english: That was quick -- have you finished already?
examples.chinese: 那么快呀，你已经做好了？

examples.english: I had to make a quick decision.
examples.chinese: 我必须立即作出决定。

examples.english: You'll have to be quick - we don't have much time.
examples.chinese: 你得快点，我们时间不多。

examples.english: Don't talk so fast - I can't understand what you're saying.
examples.chinese: 别讲得这么快，我听不懂你在说些什么。

examples.english: We're working as fast as we can.
examples.chinese: 我们在尽快地工作。

examples.english: a rapid increase in the population
examples.chinese: 人口的迅速增长

examples.english: She made a rapid recovery after her operation.
examples.chinese: 手术后她恢复得很快。

examples.english: Adolescence is a period of great and rapid change.
examples.chinese: 青春期是变化很大也很快的一段时期。

examples.english: the rapidly changing world of computer technology
examples.chinese: 迅速变化的计算机技术领域

examples.english: Punishment of the protesters was swift and severe.
examples.chinese: 对抗议者的惩罚迅速而严厉。

examples.english: Swift fashion changes mean that the shop has to change its stock every six to eight weeks.
examples.chinese: 时装更新很快，这意味着店铺每六至八个星期就要更新库存。

examples.english: We are working to ensure the safe and speedy return of all the refugees to their homes.
examples.chinese: 我们正致力于确保所有难民安全而及时地返回家园。

examples.english: Thousands of letters and telegrams arrived wishing Nikolai a speedy recovery from his surgery.
examples.chinese: 成千上万封来信和电报都祝愿尼古拉手术后迅速康复。

examples.english: The mayor's proposal was speedily approved by the town council.
examples.chinese: 市长的提议立刻得到市议会的批准。

examples.english: A major disaster was prevented by the prompt action of the safety officer.
examples.chinese: 保安人员及时采取行动，阻止了一场重大的灾难。

examples.english: It is important to ensure prompt delivery of goods that customers order.
examples.chinese: 确保将顾客所订的货及时送到，这是很重要的。

examples.english: Store the shellfish in a covered container and refrigerate them promptly.
examples.chinese: 把这些贝类存放在有盖的容器内，然后迅速冷藏起来。

examples.english: The film tells the story of Lee's meteoric rise from North Dakota radio singer to jazz legend.
examples.chinese: 这部影片讲的是李从北达科他州的电台歌手一举成为爵士乐传奇人物的故事。

examples.english: The Amazonian rainforest is disappearing at an alarming rate.
examples.chinese: 亚马逊雨林正在以惊人的速度消失。

examples.english: The number of people dying from lung cancer has increased at an alarming rate.
examples.chinese: 死于肺癌的人数以惊人的速度增长。

examples.english: He was gone in a flash.
examples.chinese: 他瞬即离开了。

examples.english: In a flash Atticus was up and standing over him.
examples.chinese: 阿蒂克斯迅速起来站在他旁边。

examples.english: Joel slipped but was on his feet in a flash.
examples.chinese: 乔尔滑了一下，但又迅速站稳了。

centers.english: to move very fast in a particular direction

examples.english: Hearing the children's screams, she raced back to the house.
examples.chinese: 她听到孩子们的尖叫声，便飞快地跑回屋里。

examples.english: A police car came racing down the road.
examples.chinese: 一辆警车迅速从路上开来。

examples.english: She tore across the lobby, then up three flights of stairs.
examples.chinese: 她快速跑过大厅，再跑上三段楼梯。

examples.english: Just before the explosion, a man came tearing across the street.
examples.chinese: 爆炸前一刹，那个男人从街对面飞跑过来。

examples.english: Everyone rushed out into the street to see what was happening.
examples.chinese: 人人都冲到街上去看发生什么事。

examples.english: People were rushing past me on their way to work.
examples.chinese: 人们匆匆从我身边走过去上班。

examples.english: Pam dashed into the store just as it was closing.
examples.chinese: 商店刚要关门的那一刻帕姆冲了进去。

examples.english: The boys dashed across the road and disappeared into the woods.
examples.chinese: 男孩们急奔过马路，消失在树林里。

examples.english: As I slowed down another car flew past me and turned to the left.
examples.chinese: 我慢下来时另一辆汽车从我身边疾驰而过，向左转了弯。

examples.english: The bus was flying along when suddenly the driver slammed on the brakes.
examples.chinese: 那辆公共汽车正在疾驰，忽然驾驶员急踏刹车。

examples.english: I could see small silvery fish darting through the water.
examples.chinese: 我可以看到银闪闪的小鱼在水里箭也似地穿梭游动。

examples.english: As the rain began to fall harder, I darted into the first shop I could find.
examples.chinese: 雨越下越大，我冲进最近的一家商店内。

examples.english: Two aircraft streaked across the sky.
examples.chinese: 两架飞机在空中一闪而过。

examples.english: I caught a glimpse of a man streaking away into the shadows.
examples.chinese: 我看到一名男子闪进了幽暗处。

examples.english: A bullet whizzed past my ear.
examples.chinese: 一颗子弹从我耳边呼啸而过。

examples.english: I stared out the window, watching as kids on bicycles and skateboards whizzed by.
examples.chinese: 我从窗口望出去，看着骑自行车和溜滑板的小孩们飞驰而过。

examples.english: The plane zoomed low over the airfield.
examples.chinese: 这架飞机在机场的低空呼啸疾飞。

examples.english: A fire engine zoomed past us.
examples.chinese: 一辆消防车从我们身边呼啸而过。

examples.english: An ambulance sped by on its way to an accident.
examples.chinese: 一辆救护车疾驰而过，开往事故地点。

examples.english: Small patrol boats sped along the shore.
examples.chinese: 小型的巡逻艇在沿岸边疾驰。

examples.english: That car must have been really moving when it hit the van.
examples.chinese: 那辆汽车撞到货车上时肯定开得很快。

centers.english: to move or work faster

examples.english: Could you go a little faster? We don't want to miss our plane.
examples.chinese: 你能不能再开快点？我们不想误了航班。

examples.english: You'll have to work a lot faster than this.
examples.chinese: 你得比这个速度再多多加快地干才行。

examples.english: I could feel my heart beating faster and faster.
examples.chinese: 我能感觉到自己的心跳越来越快。

examples.english: Nancy, if you don't speed up we'll be here until midnight.
examples.chinese: 南希，如果你再不加快速度，我们都得留在这儿直到半夜才能回家了。

examples.english: I'll phone the manager and get them to speed things up.
examples.chinese: 我要给经理打电话让他们加快速度。

examples.english: The company announced they're speeding up plans to expand the site.
examples.chinese: 该公司宣布他们正在加快扩大场地的计划。

examples.english: Francis leaned back in his seat as the plane picked up speed.
examples.chinese: 飞机加速时，弗朗西斯向后靠在座位上。

examples.english: The truck was already moving, gathering speed with a deep-throated roar.
examples.chinese: 卡车已经开动了，低沉轰响着在加速。

examples.english: The Ferrari Mondial can accelerate from zero to 60 mph in 6.3 seconds.
examples.chinese: 这辆法拉利蒙代尔能在6.3秒内从静止加速至时速60英里。

examples.english: The truck's wheels skidded on the snow as the driver accelerated forward.
examples.chinese: 当司机往前加速时，卡车轮子在雪地上打滑了。

examples.english: Suddenly realizing he was late, he quickened his pace towards the hotel.
examples.chinese: 他忽然意识到已经迟了，便加快步子朝旅馆走去。

examples.english: ‘I have some questions to ask you, Mr Murray,’ said the reporter, quickening his pace to keep up with me.
examples.chinese: “我想问你几个问题，默里先生。”记者说着，加快脚步赶上我。

examples.english: Gradually the train gathered momentum, and within seconds it was moving at top speed.
examples.chinese: 火车渐渐地加速，几秒钟后就以最快速度行驶了。

examples.english: The slope was getting steeper and their sledge was gaining momentum all the time.
examples.chinese: 山坡越来越陡，他们的雪橇向下滑得越来越快。

centers.english: to move as fast as someone else
centers.chinese: 与别人的移动速度一样快

examples.english: We need to start cycling faster or we'll never catch up.
examples.chinese: 我们得骑得快些了，不然就再也追不上了。

examples.english: Go on ahead. I'll catch up with you later.
examples.chinese: 继续往前走，我稍后赶上来。

examples.english: The police car caught up with the stolen van after a long chase.
examples.chinese: 警车经过长距离追赶，终于追上了那辆被盗的小货车。

examples.english: Run faster -- they're gaining on us!
examples.chinese: 再跑快点，他们要追上我们啦！

examples.english: Seeing the van was gaining on him, he turned suddenly onto a dirt road.
examples.chinese: 看到小货车在追上来，他突然转弯开到一条烂泥路上去了。

examples.english: Maggie had a stone in her shoe and was finding it hard to keep up.
examples.chinese: 玛吉鞋子里有块小石子，她觉得自己很难跟得上。

examples.english: Slow down! I can't keep up with you.
examples.chinese: 慢点！我跟不上你了。

examples.english: She had difficulty keeping up with J.D.'s long, quick strides.
examples.chinese: 她很难跟上J.D.那又大又快的步伐。

centers.english: to move faster than someone or something else
centers.chinese: 移动得比别人或其他东西快

examples.english: I turned around and saw that, out of the dust and the crowd, another car was pulling ahead to join us.
examples.chinese: 我转过头看到，从飞扬的尘土和人堆里又有一辆车赶超上来加入我们的车阵。

examples.english: With the finish line in sight, Wallace pulled ahead of the pack and drove to victory.
examples.chinese: 终点线在望，华莱士一车当先，赢得胜利。

examples.english: We were all running to catch the bus, but the others had longer legs and gradually left me behind.
examples.chinese: 我们都跑着去赶公共汽车，但其他人腿长，慢慢地我落后了。

examples.english: Dinah urged her horse on until she had left all the other riders way behind.
examples.chinese: 黛娜策马前进，直到远远地把其他骑手甩在了后面。

examples.english: Collins accelerated around the final turn and left the other runners standing.
examples.chinese: 柯林斯在最后一个弯道上加速，把其他赛跑选手远远甩在后面。

centers.english: a course of study that you do quickly

examples.english: The college offers a rapid programme of training for librarians.
examples.chinese: 学院为图书管理员提供速成培训课程。

examples.english: Rapid learning: Learn to speak a new language in 12 weeks!
examples.chinese: 速成学习：12个星期学会说一种新的语言！

examples.english: Before moving to Paris, Michael went on an intensive course to improve his French.
examples.chinese: 迈克尔移居巴黎前，参加了一门强化课程以提高他的法语水平。

examples.english: After a brief period of intensive training, I was allowed to make my first parachute jump.
examples.chinese: 经过短期强化培训，我被允许作第一次跳伞。

examples.english: Students lacking math and science skills take a seven-week crash course during the summer.
examples.chinese: 缺乏数学和科学技能的学生在暑期参加为期七周的速成课程。

centers.english: moving slowly or doing something slowly
centers.chinese: 慢慢地移动或慢慢地做某事

examples.english: The train was slow, noisy, and uncomfortable.
examples.chinese: 火车开得很慢，又吵又不舒服。

examples.english: I was always one of the slowest runners in my class.
examples.chinese: 我总是班上跑得最慢的人之一。

examples.english: My computer's really slow compared to the ones at school.
examples.chinese: 我的电脑和学校里的电脑相比实在是很慢。

examples.english: Farmers in the region have been slow to adopt modern agricultural methods.
examples.chinese: 这个地区的农民在采用现代农耕方法方面步伐很慢。

examples.english: The CIA has been slow in turning over the documents that Congress requested.
examples.chinese: 美国中央情报局迟迟没有交上国会所要求的文件。

examples.english: He got up slowly out of his chair and came towards me.
examples.chinese: 他慢慢地从椅子上站起，向我走来。

examples.english: Can you speak more slowly? I can't understand what you're saying.
examples.chinese: 你说得慢一点好吗？我听不懂你在说什么。

examples.english: Large white clouds drifted slowly across the deep blue sky.
examples.chinese: 大朵大朵的白云在蔚蓝色的天空中悠悠飘过。

examples.english: Lynne slowly began to realize the job wasn't as easy as it seemed.
examples.chinese: 琳恩慢慢地意识到，那工作看似简单，其实不然。

examples.english: In the evenings the roads out of town are clogged with slow-moving traffic.
examples.chinese: 到了晚上，镇外的公路上就堵满了缓缓行驶的车辆。

examples.english: Lubbers are slow-moving flightless insects native to the southeastern U.S.
examples.chinese: 笨蝗是行动缓慢、不能飞行的昆虫，产于美国东南部。

examples.english: The canal's water was muddy and slow-moving.
examples.chinese: 这条运河的水浑浊不清，水流缓慢。

examples.english: Sunday mornings she gets up late and then has a leisurely breakfast with her family.
examples.chinese: 星期天早上她起得很晚，然后悠闲地和家人一起用早餐。

examples.english: We spent a leisurely afternoon talking about old times.
examples.chinese: 我们度过了一个悠闲的下午，聊起了过去的时光。

examples.english: The two set off walking down the beach at a leisurely pace.
examples.chinese: 那两人步履悠闲地出发到海滩散步。

examples.english: The car crash seemed to take place in slow-motion.
examples.chinese: 撞车的那一瞬间好像一个慢镜头。

examples.english: The documentary showed the hawk's dive in slow-motion.
examples.chinese: 纪录片中用慢镜头表现了鹰的俯冲。

examples.english: Fans who taped the show can replay it in slow-motion to read the messages that whizzed by on screen.
examples.chinese: 把这个节目录下来的忠实观众可以用慢动作重放，以解读在屏幕上一闪而过的信息。

examples.english: You'll save money on petrol if you drive your car at low speed.
examples.chinese: 汽车低速行驶可以节省油费。

examples.english: The car came towards them at low speed, and then someone leaned out the back window and started shooting.
examples.chinese: 汽车慢慢朝他们驶来，然后有人从后窗探出身子开始射击。

examples.english: Low-speed crashes can still prove deadly, especially to children.
examples.chinese: 低速行驶中发生的车祸证明是仍可致命的，尤其是对于儿童来说。

examples.english: Traffic was moving at a snail's pace.
examples.chinese: 车流缓慢，犹如蜗牛爬行。

examples.english: The development project seems to be moving at a snail's pace.
examples.chinese: 这个开发项目像是蜗行牛步，进展缓慢。

examples.english: The car felt sluggish as we drove up the hill.
examples.chinese: 我们上山的时候，汽车开得慢慢吞吞的。

examples.english: The children were tired and sluggish and didn't seem interested in any of the games.
examples.chinese: 孩子们疲惫乏力，对这些游戏似乎都毫无兴趣。

examples.english: ‘I should probably go,’ Mike said, rising sluggishly from the sofa.
examples.chinese: “也许我该走了。”迈克说着，有气无力地从沙发上爬起来。

examples.english: Come on, slowcoach! If you don't hurry up, we'll be late.
examples.chinese: 快点，磨蹭鬼！你如果还不赶快，我们就要迟到了。

examples.english: I hate getting stuck behind these slowpokes on the highway.
examples.chinese: 我讨厌在公路上塞在这些慢吞吞的家伙后面。

centers.english: happening or changing slowly

examples.english: She's making a slow recovery after her illness.
examples.chinese: 她患病后，正在慢慢康复。

examples.english: Rebuilding the country's economy is likely to be a long, slow process.
examples.chinese: 重建这个国家的经济可能是一个漫长而缓慢的过程。

examples.english: For the first few months that I was taking lessons, my progress was extremely slow.
examples.chinese: 我上课的头几个月里进步极慢。

examples.english: The situation is slowly improving.
examples.chinese: 形势在慢慢好转。

examples.english: Slowly prices began to fall.
examples.chinese: 物价慢慢地开始下降了。

examples.english: Their relationship has developed slowly, but they now consider each other close friends.
examples.chinese: 他们的关系发展得很慢，但是现在都当对方是密友了。

examples.english: The climate is gradually becoming drier and warmer.
examples.chinese: 气候逐渐变得干燥暖和了。

examples.english: As the weeks passed, I gradually accepted the idea of him leaving.
examples.chinese: 几个星期过去，我逐渐接受了他离去的事实。

examples.english: Most patients gradually develop a resistance to the drug.
examples.chinese: 大多数病人慢慢地对这种药产生抗药性。

examples.english: I had noticed a gradual improvement in her written work.
examples.chinese: 我注意到她的书面作业逐渐有了进步。

examples.english: The chart showed a gradual rise in his temperature over the previous eight hours.
examples.chinese: 图表显示在过去八小时里他的体温在逐渐上升。

examples.english: Because the cell destruction is gradual, a victim's pancreas can function normally for years.
examples.chinese: 因为细胞破坏是渐进的，所以患者的胰腺可以维持正常的生理机能长达数年。

examples.english: Little by little, Greg's health improved.
examples.chinese: 渐渐地，格雷格的身体好起来了。

examples.english: Bit by bit, the dogs got used to their new surroundings.
examples.chinese: 渐渐地，狗儿适应了它们的新环境。

examples.english: Then add the olive oil, little by little, beating continuously until the sauce thickens.
examples.chinese: 然后加入橄榄油，一点一点地加，不停地搅打，直至酱汁变稠厚起来。

examples.english: Slowly but surely, the company is becoming successful again.
examples.chinese: 公司在缓慢而平稳地再次获得成功。

examples.english: She's getting better, slowly but surely.
examples.chinese: 她的身体在好转，很慢，但是很稳定。

examples.english: By degrees, little children grow less dependent on their parents.
examples.chinese: 渐渐地，小孩子就不怎么依赖父母了。

examples.english: The storm intensified by degrees until the rain was pouring down.
examples.chinese: 暴风雨逐渐增强，最后大雨倾盆而下。

centers.english: to do something slowly

examples.english: Just take your time. You don't have to decide immediately.
examples.chinese: 慢慢来吧，你不需要立即作出决定。

examples.english: Not wanting to seem too eager, Susan took her time about replying to the invitation.
examples.chinese: 苏珊不想显得太急切，于是慢慢才回复请柬。

examples.english: Alice took her time telling the story, making sure to include every detail.
examples.chinese: 艾丽丝从容不迫地讲着故事，她想把每一个细节都讲出来。

examples.english: ‘Do you mind if we look around a little more?’ ‘Not at all. I'm in no hurry.’
examples.chinese: “我再多转一下行吗？”“行，我不急。”

expressions.text: be in no hurry to do something

examples.english: The sun was shining and I was in no hurry to get back to the office.
examples.chinese: 阳光灿烂，我并不急着回到办公室去。

examples.english: Let's take it slowly, one step at a time, okay?
examples.chinese: 我们慢慢来吧，一步一步地，好吗？

examples.english: You've just had a serious operation - you'll have to take things slowly for the next few weeks.
examples.chinese: 你刚刚动了个大手术，接着几个星期你做事得慢慢来。

examples.english: We'll never get all the shopping done today if you dawdle like this.
examples.chinese: 如果你这样磨磨蹭蹭，我们今天就不可能把东西买齐。

examples.english: I can't see why those guys in the office are dawdling over this.
examples.chinese: 我不明白公司里的那些人为什么对这件事拖拖拉拉的。

centers.english: to move more slowly or do something more slowly

examples.english: Slow down! You're driving too fast!
examples.chinese: 慢一点！你开得太快了！

examples.english: Everyone's always rushing around trying to get things done - we all need to slow down and take it easy.
examples.chinese: 大家总是急急忙忙地想把事情做好，我们都需要放松一点，慢慢来。

examples.english: We could have been here hours ago, but the rain slowed us down.
examples.chinese: 我们本来老早就到了，可是下雨令我们慢了。

examples.english: Reduce speed now - roadworks ahead.
examples.chinese: 现在减速—前方道路施工。

examples.english: There is heavy fog on all roads tonight so drivers are advised to reduce speed and drive with extra care.
examples.chinese: 今晚所有道路都降大雾，所以建议各位司机减速行驶，多加小心。

centers.english: to move more slowly than the other people

examples.english: A half an hour into the hike, two of the boys had already fallen behind.
examples.chinese: 徒步半个小时就已经有两个男孩落在了后面。

examples.english: The ship was so slow it dropped far behind the rest of the convoy.
examples.chinese: 该轮船开得很慢，远远地落在船队其他船只的后面。

examples.english: He deliberately lagged behind so he could have a cigarette.
examples.chinese: 他故意落在后面，那样就可以抽支烟。

examples.english: We started walking faster, not wanting to lag behind the rest of the group.
examples.chinese: 我们不想掉队，于是开始加快了步速。

centers.english: to move slowly towards or away from something or someone
centers.chinese: 慢慢地移向或离开某物或某人

examples.english: The old Buick barely managed to crawl up the hill.
examples.chinese: 这辆旧别克车好不容易爬上了山。

examples.english: We sat in the car with the radio on, crawling along behind a long line of other cars.
examples.chinese: 我们坐在汽车里开着收音机，跟在一条汽车长龙后面徐徐而行。

examples.english: Due to a major road accident, southbound traffic is moving at a crawl.
examples.chinese: 由于严重交通事故，南行车辆行驶缓慢。

examples.english: The car inched forward into the narrow parking space.
examples.chinese: 汽车慢慢地驶进狭窄的车位。

examples.english: We watched the cat inching along the ground, not taking its eyes off the bird for a second.
examples.chinese: 我们看着猫两眼紧紧地盯着那只鸟，一步一步地往前挪。

examples.english: I inched my way across the crowded room to where Lou was standing.
examples.chinese: 我一步一步地挤过拥挤的房间，米到卢站立的地方。

examples.english: I started edging towards the door, hoping to slip away unnoticed.
examples.chinese: 我开始慢慢地朝门口挪去，希望能悄悄地溜走。

examples.english: He kept an eye on me as he edged across the room.
examples.chinese: 他一边留心着我，一边慢慢挪向房间另一边。

examples.english: Billy edged along the ledge, trying not to look down.
examples.chinese: 比利在岩架上缓缓挪动，尽量不往下看。

examples.english: Clouds crept across the horizon, just above the line of trees.
examples.chinese: 云团就在树梢上端，慢慢飘过地平线。

examples.english: Armed men in ski masks crept up on the van and raked it with gunfire.
examples.chinese: 几个头戴滑雪面罩的持枪男子悄悄地靠近小型货车，用枪对着它扫射。

examples.english: As the sun began to set, long shadows seemed to creep out of the corners.
examples.chinese: 太阳开始下山的时候，长长的影子好像慢慢爬出了角落。

examples.english: We crept down to the deserted library at the other end of the hall, so that we could talk.
examples.chinese: 我们悄悄地溜到大厅另一头无人的图书室里去以便说话。

centers.english: how fast something moves or is done
centers.chinese: 某物移动或某事完成的速度

examples.english: The train's designers claim it is capable of attaining speeds in excess of 350 kph.
examples.chinese: 该火车的设计人员称车速能达到每小时350公里以上。

examples.english: sensors which monitor speed and body movement
examples.chinese: 监测步速和人体运动的感应器

examples.english: What was the speed of the car at the time of the accident?
examples.chinese: 那汽车出事的时候车速是多少？

examples.english: the internal processing speed of a computer
examples.chinese: 电脑的内部处理速度

expressions.text: at a speed of 50 mph/10 metres per second etc

examples.english: The Earth moves round the Sun at a speed of 30 km per second.
examples.chinese: 地球以每秒30公里的速度绕太阳运行。

examples.english: Keep driving at a constant speed until I tell you differently.
examples.chinese: 保持恒速行驶，直到我另有吩咐。

examples.english: The Ferrari Testarossa has a top speed of 188 mph.
examples.chinese: 这辆法拉利“红头”最高时速达188英里。

examples.english: Individual children develop physically and emotionally at different rates.
examples.chinese: 每一个儿童在生理上和情感上的发展都速度不一。

examples.english: Our money was running out at an alarming rate.
examples.chinese: 我们的钱花起来速度惊人。

examples.english: The amount of light available will determine the plant's rate of growth.
examples.chinese: 光照决定植物的生长速度。

examples.english: equipment that can load ships at a rate of 5000 tonnes a day
examples.chinese: 以一天5,000吨的速度装船的设备

examples.english: The soldiers were marching at a steady pace.
examples.chinese: 士兵们正在稳步行进。

examples.english: We climbed at a leisurely pace, stopping occasionally to enjoy the view.
examples.chinese: 我们悠闲地爬着山，不时停下来欣赏风景。

examples.english: The pace of political change has been rapid.
examples.chinese: 政治改革步伐迅速。

examples.english: I'm enjoying the relaxed pace of life of Jamaica.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢牙买加这种休闲的生活节奏。

examples.english: The Kumon method involves students learning at their own pace.
examples.chinese: 公文教育法让学生以自己的步伐学习。

examples.english: This instrument is used for measuring wind velocity.
examples.chinese: 这个仪器用于测风速。

examples.english: an experiment to try to predict the velocity of a moving object
examples.chinese: 测算运动物体速度的一个实验

examples.english: a beam of high velocity electrons
examples.chinese: 高速电子束

examples.english: We are trying to measure the position and momentum of an electron as accurately as possible.
examples.chinese: 我们在想办法尽可能精确地测出电子的位置和动力。

examples.english: As the slope got steeper, the sled gathered momentum.
examples.chinese: 坡道越来越陡，雪橇的速度也越来越快。

examples.english: The ball was moving along, slowly losing momentum on the bumpy ground.
examples.chinese: 球向前滚动着，在凹凸不平的地面上慢慢减速。

expressions.text: miles per hour/metres per second etc

examples.english: The maximum speed on British motorways is 70 miles per hour.
examples.chinese: 英国高速公路上的最高车速为每小时70英里。

examples.english: Sound travels through the air at about 340 metres per second.
examples.chinese: 声音以大约每秒340米的速度在空气中传播。

examples.english: a propeller that revolves at a rate of 150 revolutions per minute
examples.chinese: 每分钟转速为150圈的螺旋桨

centers.english: to go around in circles

examples.english: Slowly the wheels of the train began to turn.
examples.chinese: 火车轮子慢慢地转动起来。

examples.english: I heard the door knob turning, and then Frank opened the door and tiptoed in.
examples.chinese: 我听到门把手转动的声音，然后弗兰克打开门，蹑手蹑脚地走了进来。

examples.english: As the propeller stopped turning, Grady ran up to the plane.
examples.chinese: 螺旋桨停止转动以后，格雷迪向飞机跑去。

examples.english: When the fan goes around it forces the warm air back down.
examples.chinese: 风扇旋转起来时使得热空气后退了。

examples.english: The gear was going round, but it didn't seem to be catching on anything.
examples.chinese: 齿轮在转动，但似乎什么也没卡住。

examples.english: The ice skater began to spin faster and faster.
examples.chinese: 溜冰者开始转得越来越快。

examples.english: The wheels were spinning in the mud, but the car wouldn't move.
examples.chinese: 轮子在泥地里飞速旋转，但汽车还是不动。

examples.english: The boy was spinning around in his father's desk chair.
examples.chinese: 小男孩在他爸爸的写字椅上转圈圈。

examples.english: The Earth rotates on its axis once every twenty-four hours.
examples.chinese: 地球每24小时绕地轴旋转一周。

examples.english: A disco ball revolved slowly over the empty dance floor.
examples.chinese: 在空荡荡的舞池上方，一个迪斯科转球在慢慢地旋转。

examples.english: The stage rotates giving the audience a constantly changing view.
examples.chinese: 舞台旋转能给观众带来不断变换的视角。

expressions.text: go/run/drive etc around in circles/ go/run/drive etc round in circles

examples.english: The children went round in circles till the music stopped.
examples.chinese: 孩子们绕着圈，直到音乐停止。

examples.english: We were driving around in circles, weaving through the parking lot.
examples.chinese: 我们开着车转圈，在停车场里绕来绕去。

expressions.text: go/run/drive etc around and around/ go/run/drive etc round and round

examples.english: Billy will sit and watch his train going round and round on its little track for hours.
examples.chinese: 比利会坐着看他的玩具火车在小轨道上一圈一圈地转，一看就是几个小时。

examples.english: The steers walked restlessly around and around their pen.
examples.chinese: 小公牛在围栏里不耐烦地转圈子走。

examples.english: The blades of the helicopter whirled powerfully overhead.
examples.chinese: 直升机的螺旋桨叶在头顶上强有力地旋转着。

examples.english: Flies whirled round the piles of sticky sweets.
examples.chinese: 苍蝇在黏黏的糖堆上盘旋。

examples.english: Dust and sand were whirling around in the air, as the desert wind began to get stronger.
examples.chinese: 沙漠里的风开始越来越大，尘土和沙砾在空中飞旋着。

examples.english: Smoke spiralled upward from the chimney.
examples.chinese: 烟从烟囱里袅袅上升。

examples.english: We watched the leaves spiral down from the trees in the cold autumn wind.
examples.chinese: 我们看着树叶在寒冷的秋风中盘旋落下。

examples.english: Dust swirled like smoke in the evening sunshine.
examples.chinese: 晚霞中，尘土像烟雾般打旋。

examples.english: Jessie's pale dress swirled round her slender ankles.
examples.chinese: 杰茜的淡色连衣裙在她瘦削的脚踝旁摆动。

examples.english: The wind swirling around the tree had blown all the snow away from its trunk.
examples.chinese: 绕着树盘旋的风把树干上的雪全都吹走了。

examples.english: We all looked towards the sky where the vultures were circling.
examples.chinese: 我们都向天空看去，上面有些鹫在盘旋。

examples.english: The plane circled the runway several times before landing.
examples.chinese: 飞机在跑道上方盘旋了几个圈后才降落。

examples.english: Helicopters circled overhead, trying to get pictures of the crime scene.
examples.chinese: 直升机在头顶上盘旋，拍下犯罪现场的照片。

examples.english: Rachel took her father's hand and twirled in and out under his arm.
examples.chinese: 雷切尔拉着父亲的手，在他的臂下转进转出。

examples.english: Half a dozen couples were twirling about to a waltz.
examples.chinese: 有六对舞伴随着华尔兹舞曲在快速旋转。

centers.english: to make something turn around

examples.english: Tim turned the handle slowly and pushed open the door.
examples.chinese: 蒂姆慢慢地转动把手，然后推开了门。

examples.english: She put the key in the ignition and turned it, but nothing happened.
examples.chinese: 她把钥匙插进点火开关转了一下，但没有反应。

examples.english: If we turn the table around we can fit more chairs in the room.
examples.chinese: 如果我们把桌子转个向，就可以在房间里放下更多椅子了。

examples.english: ‘I can't get the top off.’ ‘Try twisting it the other way.’
examples.chinese: “我打不开盖子。”“朝反方向旋一下试试看。”

examples.english: We twisted and tugged to get the mussels loose from their shells.
examples.chinese: 我们一转一拉，蚌肉就与壳松开了。

examples.english: You wind the handle on the side to make the music play.
examples.chinese: 你摇动旁边的手柄来放音乐。

examples.english: She wound the car window down to speak to the police officer.
examples.chinese: 她把汽车窗玻璃摇下来和警察说话。

examples.english: My watch has stopped - I must have forgotten to wind it.
examples.chinese: 我的手表停了，我肯定是忘记上发条了。

examples.english: He swivelled the camera on the tripod to follow her as she crossed the yard.
examples.chinese: 她穿过庭院时，他转动脚架上的摄影机跟着她。

expressions.text: swivel something around/ swivel something round

examples.english: She swivelled the computer screen around so that I could see it too.
examples.chinese: 她把电脑屏幕转过来让我也能看到。

examples.english: She sat in the bar twirling the stem of her wine glass, wishing she were somewhere else.
examples.chinese: 她坐在酒吧里，捻弄着酒杯的柄脚，心想但愿自己是在别的什么地方。

examples.english: He kept twiddling the knobs on the radio trying to get a signal.
examples.chinese: 他不停地转动收音机的旋钮想收到信号。

centers.english: to make something turn in circles very quickly

examples.english: Spin the wheel of the bicycle to make sure that it is fastened correctly.
examples.chinese: 快速转动自行车的车轮，以确定它是否固定妥当。

examples.english: He spun the dial on the padlock right, then left, then right again.
examples.chinese: 他把挂锁上的转盘右转，然后左转，然后再右转。

examples.english: I've never been able to twirl a baton.
examples.chinese: 我从来不会转动指挥杖。

examples.english: He picked up a pen and twirled it between his fingers.
examples.chinese: 他拿起一支笔，在手指间转动着。

examples.english: The man, without answering, began to twirl the ends of his moustache.
examples.chinese: 那个男人没有回答，开始拈胡子。

examples.english: Whirl the ingredients in a blender at high speed for a minute.
examples.chinese: 把原料放在搅拌机里高速旋转一分钟。

examples.english: The warriors approached, whirling their swords and spears in the air.
examples.chinese: 武士们跑上来，手里挥舞着剑和长矛。

centers.english: a turning movement

examples.english: She can do a 360-degree turn on water skis.
examples.chinese: 她能在滑水板上做360度的旋转。

examples.english: Loosen the screw one complete turn in order to release the valve.
examples.chinese: 把螺丝拧松整整一圈以松开阀门。

examples.english: With three swift turns of the wheel, he steered the boat away from the rocks.
examples.chinese: 他快速地把舵轮转动了三圈，把船驶离了礁石。

examples.english: The shaft spins at 950 revolutions per minute.
examples.chinese: 这根轴每分钟转950圈。

examples.english: The planet rotates in the same direction as its revolution around the sun.
examples.chinese: 这颗行星沿着绕太阳旋转的相同方向自转。

examples.english: He made a quick spin to avoid the oncoming player.
examples.chinese: 他快速转身躲避冲过来的球员。

examples.english: the spin of a propeller
examples.chinese: 螺旋桨的飞速旋转

centers.english: turning with a circular movement

examples.english: A revolving door led into the hotel lobby.
examples.chinese: 一扇旋转门通向旅馆门厅。

examples.english: The 28-story building is topped by a revolving restaurant that offers ever-changing views of the city.
examples.chinese: 这座28层的建筑顶上是一家旋转餐厅，那里可以看到不断变化的城市景色。

examples.english: The plastic is cut by a rotating disc.
examples.chinese: 这种塑料是用旋转盘切割的。

examples.english: The stones are polished in a rotating drum full of abrasive powder.
examples.chinese: 宝石被放在装满研磨粉的转轮内抛光。

centers.english: to turn your head or your body

examples.english: Campbell turned and walked out of the room.
examples.chinese: 坎贝尔转身走出了房间。

examples.english: ‘What do you think we should do?’ she said, turning to her husband.
examples.chinese: “你觉得我们该怎么办？”她转身对丈夫说道。

examples.english: She heard the door opening and turned to see who was coming in.
examples.chinese: 她听见开门声，就转身去看是谁进来了。

examples.english: Ian, turn round and face the front!
examples.chinese: 伊恩，转过身来脸朝前面！

examples.english: I turned around quickly to see if anyone was following me.
examples.chinese: 我迅速转过身来，看看是否有人在跟踪我。

examples.english: ‘Does my dress look OK?’ ‘Turn around and let me see the back.’
examples.chinese: “我的连衣裙好看吗？”“转过身来让我看看后面。

examples.english: Alice turned away with tears in her eyes.
examples.chinese: 爱丽斯含着眼泪转过脸去。

examples.english: The scene was so sickening I had to turn away.
examples.chinese: 那场面太恶心，我不得不转过脸去。

examples.english: She frowned and turned away from him without speaking.
examples.chinese: 她皱着眉，默默地转过脸去不看他。

examples.english: As soon as I turn my back, the children start to misbehave.
examples.chinese: 我刚一转身，那帮孩子就开始调皮捣蛋了。

examples.english: Don't turn your back on me - I'm talking to you!
examples.chinese: 别转过身背对着我—我在跟你说话！

examples.english: She turned her back on him and began to walk away.
examples.chinese: 她转过身背对着他，然后走开了。

examples.english: She turned her head to avoid making eye contact with the beggar.
examples.chinese: 她转过头去免得和那乞丐有目光接触。

examples.english: Whenever Suzie walks into a room all the men immediately turn their heads.
examples.chinese: 苏茜无论什么时候走进屋子，所有的男人都会立即回过头来看她。

examples.english: David angrily turned on his heel and marched towards the door.
examples.chinese: 戴维生气地转过身去，大步向门口走去。
