examples.english: When her ex-husband entered the room, she turned on her heel and left.
examples.chinese: 当她的前夫进入房间时，她迅速转身离开了。

examples.english: He swung around at the sound of her voice and smiled at her.
examples.chinese: 他一听到她的声音便转过身来向她微笑。

examples.english: I felt a hand on my shoulder and swung round quickly to find Chris standing behind me.
examples.chinese: 我觉得有一只手搭在我的肩上，便立即转过身来，发现克里斯正站在我身后。

examples.english: The sudden crash made her spin round and look back down the passage.
examples.chinese: 突然传来哗啦一声，她立刻转过身往过道那头看去。

examples.english: Dobbs yelled back and spun around with clenched fists, ready to fight.
examples.chinese: 多布斯也大吼着回应，然后急转过身来握紧了拳头准备打斗。

examples.english: Ralph swivelled in his chair and looked directly at Meg.
examples.chinese: 拉尔夫坐在椅子上一下子转过身来直视梅格。

examples.english: Mr Tench swivelled round in astonishment as the men burst through his office door.
examples.chinese: 那些男子闯进坦奇先生的办公室时，他吃惊地马上转过身来。

centers.english: to turn your body when you are lying down

examples.english: The bed squeaks every time I turn over.
examples.chinese: 我每次翻身，这床就嘎吱作响。

examples.english: Turn over and I'll give you a massage.
examples.chinese: 翻过身来，我给你按摩。

examples.english: He opened his eyes and turned over on his side, facing her.
examples.chinese: 他睁开眼睛，翻过身侧对着她。

examples.english: The cat purred, rolled over on its back, and stretched.
examples.chinese: 猫发出呼噜声，翻了个身仰面朝天，伸了伸懒腰。

examples.english: I heard the alarm clock, but I rolled over and went back to sleep.
examples.chinese: 我听见闹钟响，但我翻了个身又睡了。

centers.english: to change your direction when you are walking or driving
centers.chinese: 步行或驾车时改变方向

examples.english: He saw a police car up ahead, so he turned and went down a side street.
examples.chinese: 他看到前方有辆警车，就转了个弯从一条小路走了。

examples.english: Soon after leaving harbour, the ships turned and headed north.
examples.chinese: 船一离开港口就改变了方向朝北驶去。

examples.english: Turn left at the next intersection.
examples.chinese: 在下一个十字路口向左转。

examples.english: It's getting late - I think we should turn back before it gets dark.
examples.chinese: 天色已晚，我想在天黑以前我们应该回去了。

examples.english: Turn off Delaney Road just after the church.
examples.chinese: 一过教堂就离开德莱尼公路。

examples.english: Turn onto Lowell Street, then go straight for three blocks.
examples.chinese: 转到洛厄尔街，然后直行三个街区。

examples.english: I turned a corner and nearly ran into Caroline.
examples.chinese: 我转过街角，差一点撞上卡罗琳。

examples.english: The horse abruptly changed direction, nearly throwing the rider off.
examples.chinese: 这匹马突然转了向，差点把骑师甩下来。

examples.english: Changing direction on skis isn't difficult once you've learned the technique.
examples.chinese: 一旦掌握了技术，滑雪时转向并不难。

examples.english: The yacht changed course and approached the island.
examples.chinese: 帆船改变了航向，向小岛驶去。

examples.english: The plane must change course to avoid flying through the storm.
examples.chinese: 飞机必须改变航向以避免穿越那个风暴。

examples.english: The car suddenly veered across the road into oncoming traffic.
examples.chinese: 这辆汽车突然转向，横越马路冲向迎面驶来的车辆。

examples.english: The boat was heading for the rocks but at the last minute veered off in another direction.
examples.chinese: 那艘船冲向岩石，但在最后一刻猛然转向了。

examples.english: The driver swerved to avoid a child, and crashed into a signpost.
examples.chinese: 司机急转弯避开一个小孩，结果撞到了路标上。

examples.english: Dozens were injured when a passenger bus swerved into the wrong lane and slammed into another bus.
examples.chinese: 一辆客车突然转向驶上了逆向车道，结果和另一辆公共汽车相撞，数十人受伤。

centers.english: when a road or river changes direction

examples.english: The taxi went around the bend at an alarming speed.
examples.chinese: 这辆出租车以惊人的速度拐过弯道。

examples.english: The balcony overlooked a wide bend in the river.
examples.chinese: 从阳台可以俯视河流宽阔的弯道。

examples.english: As we rounded the bend, I could see the town up ahead.
examples.chinese: 我们转弯时，我看到了前面的市镇。

examples.english: He lost control of the car on a sharp curve.
examples.chinese: 他的车在一个急转弯处失控。

examples.english: I rounded the curve looking for a place to pull over.
examples.chinese: 我转过拐弯处寻找停靠的地方。

examples.english: The road curves to the left as you go up the hill.
examples.chinese: 随着往山上行走，小路弯弯曲曲转向左侧。

examples.english: Take the first turning after the traffic lights.
examples.chinese: 过了交通信号灯以后，在第一个拐弯处转弯。

examples.english: We were supposed to take Highway 12, but I think we missed the turn.
examples.chinese: 我们应该上12号公路，但我想我们错过了岔路口。

examples.english: A narrow road wound up the hillside towards the little house.
examples.chinese: 一条窄路曲曲折折地往山坡上延伸，通向那幢小房子。

examples.english: The path wound through the orchard and between small beds of flowers.
examples.chinese: 这条小路在果园中和小花床之间蜿蜒。

examples.english: The staircase appears almost to be floating on air, as it winds its way up three stories.
examples.chinese: 楼梯盘旋直上三层楼，看起来好像漂浮在空中一般。

examples.english: She hated driving on the winding mountain roads at night.
examples.chinese: 她讨厌夜里在弯弯曲曲的山路上开车。

examples.english: A dry stream bed twisted through thick tree roots.
examples.chinese: 一条干涸的溪床曲曲折折地流过粗树根。

examples.english: We approached Assisi via the dusty road that twists its way up Monte Subasio.
examples.chinese: 我们沿着蜿蜒通向苏巴西奥山上的一条尘土路走近了阿西西。

examples.english: Wild flowers grew on either side of the twisting path.
examples.chinese: 野花长在那条曲折小径的两旁。

examples.english: The river weaved across the plain, towards the sea.
examples.chinese: 这条河流迂回曲折地穿过平原，流向大海。

examples.english: The old highway weaved its way through Tucson.
examples.chinese: 这条旧公路曲折穿过图森。

centers.english: when a vehicle, ship etc turns over in an accident
centers.chinese: 车辆、船只等在事故中翻倒、倾覆

examples.english: The car smashed into the post, turned over, and burst into flames.
examples.chinese: 汽车撞到柱子上，翻倒后燃烧起来。

examples.english: The train was travelling so fast that when it came off the rails it turned over onto its roof.
examples.chinese: 火车开得太快，以致于驶离轨道后翻了个底朝天。

examples.english: The truck jack-knifed and then rolled over.
examples.chinese: 那辆铰接式卡车弯折，随后就倾翻了。

examples.english: Ships have stabilizers to prevent them from rolling over in rough seas.
examples.chinese: 船只上有减摇装置，使船身在波涛汹涌的大海上不会倾覆。

examples.english: The ship capsized in rough waters with the loss of 208 lives.
examples.chinese: 船在汹涌的水面上倾覆了，有208人丧命。

examples.english: People were fighting for places in the lifeboat, and there was a real danger of it capsizing.
examples.chinese: 人们为了坐上救生船打斗起来，救生船有倾覆的危险。

examples.english: A huge wave struck the side of our boat, almost capsizing it.
examples.chinese: 一个大浪打在我们的船舷上，差点把船击翻。

examples.english: The truck had overturned, but the driver was not injured.
examples.chinese: 卡车翻了，但司机没受伤。

examples.english: The whole crew was drowned when their boat overturned in a storm.
examples.chinese: 船在暴风雨中翻了，所有船员溺水而亡。

examples.english: During the riots several cars were overturned and set on fire.
examples.chinese: 暴乱期间有几辆汽车被掀翻后点燃。

centers.english: to turn something upside down

examples.english: Do not turn your exam papers over until I tell you to.
examples.chinese: 未得我允许不要把试卷翻过来。

examples.english: She turned the envelope over and began making notes on the back.
examples.chinese: 她把信封翻过来，在背面写起笔记来。

examples.english: The children were turning over the rocks to see what was underneath.
examples.chinese: 孩子们把石头一块块翻转过来，想看看下面是什么。

examples.english: Continue to turn the fritter until both sides are golden brown.
examples.chinese: 继续翻动油炸馅饼，直到它两面都呈金黄色。

examples.english: I'll read the story and you turn the pages. OK, honey?
examples.chinese: 我讲故事，你来翻页。好不好，宝贝？

examples.english: Take out the spark plugs and turn the cylinder block upside down.
examples.chinese: 拔下火花塞，把汽缸翻转过来。

examples.english: Come help me flip this mattress.
examples.chinese: 来帮我把这床垫翻过来。

examples.english: She flipped the pancakes over with one smooth movement.
examples.chinese: 她以一个流畅的动作把薄煎饼翻过来。

examples.english: I flipped over the card to see what was written on the other side.
examples.chinese: 我把贺卡翻过来看看另一面写了些什么。

examples.english: A camera inverts the image it receives.
examples.chinese: 照相机把获得的影像倒置过来。

examples.english: Invert the cake and pan on a wire rack and remove the pan.
examples.chinese: 把蛋糕和平底锅在金属架上翻过来，再把平底锅拿走。

centers.english: to walk

examples.english: Anna missed the bus, so she decided to walk.
examples.chinese: 安娜没赶上公共汽车，于是她决定走着去。

examples.english: How old was Daisy when she first started walking?
examples.chinese: 黛茜刚开始走路的时候是多大?

examples.english: Jed walked out of the station and got into a taxi.
examples.chinese: 杰德走出车站，上了一辆出租车。

examples.english: I was walking along Main Street when I met Pierre.
examples.chinese: 遇见皮埃尔时，我正在大街上走着。

examples.english: She hates walking home alone at night.
examples.chinese: 她不喜欢晚上独自走回家。

examples.english: We must have walked about five miles today.
examples.chinese: 今天我们肯定走了大约有五英里的路。

examples.english: It's not far. It'll take you about ten minutes on foot.
examples.chinese: 不远，步行去大约十分钟。

examples.english: The bus left us at the bottom of the hill, and we went the rest of the way on foot.
examples.chinese: 公共汽车把我们留在山脚下，我们徒步走剩下的路。

centers.english: to walk quickly

examples.english: Brian strode out of the room without speaking.
examples.chinese: 布赖恩一句话也没说，大步走出房间。

examples.english: The Principal came striding towards me, and shook my hand.
examples.chinese: 校长大步向我走来，同我握手。

examples.english: Sheila marched straight into the office and demanded an apology.
examples.chinese: 希拉直接冲进办公室，要求道歉。

examples.english: ‘I'll never forgive you for this,’ Marge said, and she marched off without a backward glance.
examples.chinese: “这件事我永远不会原谅你，”玛吉说，然后头也不回地快步走了。

expressions.text: pace back and forth/up and down

examples.english: ‘We're going to be late,’ Jordan said irritably, pacing up and down the room.
examples.chinese: “我们要迟到了，”乔丹在房间里踱来踱去，烦躁地说。

examples.english: Sarah paced back and forth along the corridor, waiting for the doctor to come back.
examples.chinese: 萨拉在走廊里踱来踱去，等医生回来。

examples.english: A lion paced up and down the cage, growling.
examples.chinese: 一头狮子咆哮着在笼子里走来走去。

examples.english: Meryl was also awake, pacing the floor in her dressing-gown.
examples.chinese: 梅里尔也醒了，穿着睡袍来回踱步。

centers.english: to walk slowly in a relaxed way

examples.english: They strolled along the riverbank, enjoying the evening sun.
examples.chinese: 他们沿着河堤散步，欣赏落日。

examples.english: We drove to Penrhyn Castle, and strolled around the beautiful gardens there.
examples.chinese: 我们驱车前往彭林堡，在那里美丽的花园中漫步。

examples.english: An old man appeared from behind the house and ambled across the yard.
examples.chinese: 一位老人从房子后面出来，缓缓穿过院子。

examples.english: One of the horses, a white one, slowly ambled towards me.
examples.chinese: 其中有一匹白马缓缓地向我走来。

examples.english: Cecil was ambling along as usual without a care in the world.
examples.chinese: 塞西尔像平常一样无牵无挂地漫步。

examples.english: ‘Shouldn't you be in class?’ Mr Harris asked the girls who were sauntering down the corridor.
examples.chinese: “你们不是应该在上课的吗？”哈里斯先生问在走廊里闲逛的女孩子们。

examples.english: I sauntered into the garden, where some friends were chatting near the barbecue.
examples.chinese: 我溜达着走进花园，那里有一些朋友正在烤架旁聊天。

examples.english: As usual, Ron sauntered into the office twenty minutes late.
examples.chinese: 跟平常一样，罗恩迟了20分钟才慢悠悠地走进办公室。

centers.english: to walk slowly because you are tired

examples.english: She trudged back up the hill, loaded down with heavy bags of groceries.
examples.chinese: 她提着一袋袋沉重的食品杂货，费力地返回山上。

examples.english: We trudged to school through the snow, wishing we could be playing in it.
examples.chinese: 我们拖着脚步走过雪地去上学，很希望能在雪地里玩耍。

examples.english: The donkey was plodding slowly along under its heavy load.
examples.chinese: 驴子驮着沉甸甸的东西，艰难缓慢地往前走。

examples.english: We traipsed around every store in town, trying to find the right color paint.
examples.chinese: 我们拖着疲惫的身躯逛了镇上每一家商店，想要找到颜色合适的油漆。

examples.english: Traipsing round museums all day is not my idea of fun.
examples.chinese: 在博物馆逛一整天，我可不认为是好玩的事。

centers.english: to walk slowly because you are in pain or weak
centers.chinese: 因为疼痛或身体虚弱而缓慢行走

examples.english: My knee was stiff and painful, and I could only hobble.
examples.chinese: 我的膝盖又僵又疼，我只能一瘸一拐地走。

examples.english: Aunt Sophy hobbled slowly across the room on her crutches.
examples.chinese: 索菲姑妈撑着一副拐杖在房间里蹒跚而行。

examples.english: I noticed that one of the horses was limping, and called for the vet.
examples.chinese: 我注意到其中一匹马跛了，于是叫来兽医。

examples.english: She limped painfully over to a chair and sat down.
examples.chinese: 她一瘸一拐痛苦地走到椅子边坐下。

examples.english: Josie walked with a slight limp.
examples.chinese: 乔西走路有点跛。

examples.english: Supporting herself on Ali's arm, the old woman shuffled towards the door.
examples.chinese: 老太太靠在阿里的胳膊上，拖着脚步向门口走去。

examples.english: I heard Bob shuffling around the kitchen in his slippers.
examples.chinese: 我听见鲍勃穿着拖鞋拖着脚步在厨房里踱来踱去。

examples.english: Looking tired and fat, Parker shambled onto the stage and started playing.
examples.chinese: 帕克看上去又疲倦又肥胖，蹒跚着走上舞台开始演奏。

examples.english: An old tramp shambled along, looking for money or cigarette ends on the floor.
examples.chinese: 一个老流浪汉蹒跚着往前走，在地上找钱或者烟蒂。

centers.english: to walk in an unsteady way

examples.english: Something hit me on the head, and I staggered across the room.
examples.chinese: 有东西打在我头上，我跟踉跄跄走到房间另一头去。

examples.english: My father was staggering under the weight of a huge parcel.
examples.chinese: 我父亲背着一只很大的包裹摇摇晃晃地走着。

examples.english: The room was dark, and Stan nearly fell over a chair as he stumbled to the phone.
examples.chinese: 房间里很黑，斯坦跌跌撞撞地走向电话，差点被一把椅子绊倒。

examples.english: I finished the whiskey, then stumbled upstairs and into bed.
examples.chinese: 我喝完威士忌，然后跌跌撞撞地爬上楼倒在床上。

examples.english: Paul lurched sideways as the boat rolled suddenly.
examples.chinese: 船突然摇晃起来，保罗踉跄地歪向一边。

examples.english: Harriet lurched towards the bathroom, clutching her stomach in pain.
examples.chinese: 哈丽雅特东歪西倒地走向浴室，痛苦地捂住胃部。

centers.english: to walk with heavy, noisy steps

examples.english: My sister stomped out of the house, slamming the door behind her.
examples.chinese: 我姐姐噔噔地走出房子，随手砰地关上了门。

examples.english: Kevin looked furious as he stomped into his office. ‘What the hell is going on here?’ he yelled.
examples.chinese: 凯文跺着脚走进自己的办公室，看上去怒气冲冲的。“这里他妈的怎么回事？”他叫嚷道。

examples.english: The three of us clumped up the steps in our heavy ski boots.
examples.chinese: 我们三个穿着笨重的滑雪靴，迈着沉重的脚步走上楼梯。

examples.english: The walls are so thin we can hear the man next door clumping about all day.
examples.chinese: 墙很薄，我们都能听见隔壁那个男人整天在走来走去，脚步很重。

centers.english: to walk quietly

examples.english: He tiptoed out of the room, trying not to wake the baby.
examples.chinese: 他踮着脚走出房间，尽量不吵醒小宝宝。

examples.english: I tiptoed along the passage to Claire's door and peeped in.
examples.chinese: 我踮起脚走过通道，来到克莱尔的门口，朝里面偷看。

examples.english: Ron unlocked the back door and crept out into the yard.
examples.chinese: 罗恩打开后门的锁，蹑手蹑脚地出去，来到院子里。

examples.english: No-one noticed that the little boy had crept into the room and was sitting there, listening.
examples.chinese: 没有人注意到这个小男孩溜进了房间，坐在那里听。

examples.english: She crept up behind him and put her hands over his eyes.
examples.chinese: 她蹑手蹑脚地走到他身后，用手捂住他的双眼。

examples.english: The thieves sneaked in while the guard had his back turned.
examples.chinese: 警卫转过身去时，小偷们就溜了进来。

examples.english: Molly snuck around the house.
examples.chinese: 莫莉鬼鬼祟崇地在房子里转来转去。

examples.english: We tried to sneak off from work early.
examples.chinese: 我们试图偷偷溜走提早下班。

examples.english: Michelle got out of bed, and padded across to the window.
examples.chinese: 米歇尔下了床，轻轻地走到窗边。

examples.english: The cat came padding softly across the kitchen floor, and jumped onto my lap.
examples.chinese: 猫儿轻轻穿过厨房跳到我腿上。

centers.english: to walk proudly

examples.english: Ali swaggered arrogantly into the boxing ring, as if he had already won the fight.
examples.chinese: 阿里趾高气扬地走进拳击台，那样子就好像他已经打赢了比赛。

examples.english: Sally's boyfriend came swaggering down the steps with his hands in his pockets.
examples.chinese: 萨利的男朋友双手插在口袋里，大摇大摆地走下台阶。

examples.english: Bernard left the room with a swagger, clearly pleased with himself.
examples.chinese: 伯纳德大摇大摆地走出房间，显然自鸣得意。

examples.english: He strutted across the stage like Mick Jagger.
examples.chinese: 他像米克·贾格尔一样神气活现地走到舞台的另一边。

examples.english: The hotel was full of rich people strutting around in fur coats and Rolex watches.
examples.chinese: 酒店里到处都是有钱人，他们穿着毛皮大衣、戴着劳力士手表，神气活现地走来走去。

examples.english: On Sunday mornings, fashionable young couples parade up and down the Boulevard St Michel.
examples.chinese: 每个星期天早上，时髦的年轻夫妇都在圣米歇尔大道上招摇过市。

centers.english: to walk without going in one particular direction

examples.english: I keep seeing these two strange men walking around. I'm sure they're up to something.
examples.chinese: 我老是看见这两个陌生男人在闲逛，我肯定他们是在密谋什么。

examples.english: We walked round the market for a while, and then we went to the beach.
examples.chinese: 我们在市场逛了一会儿，然后去海滩。

examples.english: For an hour and a half we wandered around the old city, totally lost.
examples.chinese: 我们在这座古城闲逛了一个半小时，完全迷路了。

examples.english: We wandered along the river bank, looking for a place to cross.
examples.chinese: 我们沿着河堤转，寻找过河的地方。

examples.english: After dinner Carol left the hotel to wander the crowded streets.
examples.chinese: 晚饭后，卡罗尔离开旅馆去拥挤的街上逛逛。

examples.english: Tourists love roaming about the old town.
examples.chinese: 游客们喜欢在这座古镇上到处逛逛。

examples.english: Great herds of wild deer roam freely over the hills.
examples.chinese: 大群大群的野鹿自由自在地在山丘上漫步。

examples.english: Residents of the Red Hall estate claim to be living in fear of gangs who roam the streets after dark.
examples.chinese: 红厅小区的居民称他们生活在恐惧之中，怕天黑后在街上晃荡的罪犯。

examples.english: Several wolves prowled round the camp, but were kept at bay by the fire.
examples.chinese: 几匹狼在营地周围悄然出没，但因为有篝火而不敢靠近。

examples.english: The babysitter said she could hear someone prowling around in the garden.
examples.chinese: 照看孩子的人说她听见花园里有人在蹑足走动。

examples.english: Police have warned the public that the killer may still be prowling the streets.
examples.chinese: 警察已经警告公众，那个杀人犯可能还在街上徘徊。

centers.english: to walk for pleasure or exercise
centers.chinese: 出于玩乐或锻炼而行走

examples.english: Walking instead of driving is an excellent way of losing weight.
examples.chinese: 徒步取代开车是绝佳的减肥方式。

examples.english: Sheena's up at six every morning to walk along the beach.
examples.chinese: 希娜每天早上6点起床，沿海滩散步。

examples.english: I like to go walking in the woods, just to breathe the air.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢在树林里散步，只是为了呼吸那里的空气。

examples.english: The park was full of Sunday afternoon walkers.
examples.chinese: 公园里全是周日下午来散步的人。

expressions.text: go for a walk/take a walk

examples.english: It's a lovely evening. Let's go for a walk.
examples.chinese: 今晚天气很好，我们散步去吧。

expressions.text: go for a walk/take a walk along/through/across etc

examples.english: He said he was going for a walk around the block to clear his head.
examples.chinese: 他说要沿街区散步，让头脑清醒一下。

examples.english: We arrived in St Louis at noon, and took a walk down by the Mississippi River.
examples.chinese: 我们中午到达圣路易斯，然后沿着密西西比河散步。

expressions.text: go for a stroll along/through/across etc

examples.english: Dave strolled along the riverbank, enjoying the evening sun.
examples.chinese: 戴夫沿着河堤漫步，欣赏落日。

examples.english: They decided to go for a stroll along the beach.
examples.chinese: 他们决定去海滩溜达溜达。

examples.english: Patricia likes doing active things like canoeing, hiking, and horse-riding.
examples.chinese: 帕特里夏喜欢运动，比如划独木舟、远足和骑马。

examples.english: We're going to hike to the top of that hill over there.
examples.chinese: 我们想徒步到那边的那座小山顶上去。

examples.english: We're going hiking in Scotland this summer.
examples.chinese: 今年夏天我们会在苏格兰徒步旅行。

examples.english: We trekked up Mount Calahi for five days.
examples.chinese: 我们步行了五天登上卡拉希山。

examples.english: The scouts will have to trek 40km back to the base camp.
examples.chinese: 童子军将必须步行40公里回到大本营。

examples.english: Maria's going trekking in the Himalayas this year.
examples.chinese: 玛丽亚今年会在喜马拉雅山地区徒步旅行。

examples.english: After so long on the train, we couldn't wait to get out and stretch our legs.
examples.chinese: 在火车上坐了那么长时间，我们迫不及待地要出去散散步。

examples.english: Do you feel like stopping here and stretching your legs?
examples.chinese: 你想在这里停下伸伸胳膊腿吗？

examples.english: I get quite a lot of exercise myself by walking the dog.
examples.chinese: 遛狗的同时，我自己也得到不少锻炼。

examples.english: Geoff wants a Walkman for when he walks the dogs.
examples.chinese: 杰夫想要一部随身听在遛狗的时候听。

centers.english: to walk through water

examples.english: They waded across the river.
examples.chinese: 他们蹚过了河。

examples.english: One of my earliest memories is paddling in the sea with my parents.
examples.chinese: 我最早的记忆之一是与父母在海里戏水。

examples.english: Ryan took off his shoes and socks to wade in the stream.
examples.chinese: 瑞安脱了鞋袜，在小溪中蹬水。

centers.english: to walk with regular steps with other people

examples.english: Thousands of US soldiers marched through the streets of Paris.
examples.chinese: 数以千计的美国士兵齐步走过巴黎的街道。

examples.english: The regiment had finally learned to march in step.
examples.chinese: 这个军团终于学会了齐步行进。

examples.english: Steve wasn't walking in step with the rest of the class.
examples.chinese: 史蒂夫跟班里其他同学的脚步不一致。

centers.english: to walk slowly and carefully

examples.english: The boy began to pick his way over the rocks towards the ocean.
examples.chinese: 男孩开始小心翼翼地越过岩石走向大海。

examples.english: I picked my way across the crowded field, towards the main stage.
examples.chinese: 我小心翼翼地穿过拥挤的场地，走向主舞台。

examples.english: Mervyn edged sideways through the front door, which seemed to be stuck.
examples.chinese: 默文侧身挤过前门，那门好像卡住了。

examples.english: Edging my way through the crowd, I eventually managed to get to the bar.
examples.chinese: 我慢慢地穿过人群，终于来到了吧台前。

centers.english: a trip that you make by walking

examples.english: It was a pleasant walk, under cool, shady trees.
examples.chinese: 在阴凉蔽日的树下散步真愉快。

examples.english: Emily enjoyed her walks in the park with her father.
examples.chinese: 埃米莉喜欢和父亲一起在公园里散步。

examples.english: I love going for walks in the countryside.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢在乡间散步。

examples.english: They set off on a 10 mile hike across the island.
examples.chinese: 他们开始了穿越该岛的10英里徒步旅行。

examples.english: The hike back was through the first real jungle I had ever been in.
examples.chinese: 回来时，我第一次徒步穿越了真正的丛林。

examples.english: We went on lots of great hikes.
examples.chinese: 我们有过多次愉快的徒步旅行。

examples.english: You'll need a guide for your treks in the mountains.
examples.chinese: 你需要一位向导带你在山上艰苦跋涉。

examples.english: I took my backpack and joined some Egyptian friends for a trek in the Sahara.
examples.chinese: 我带上背包，和几个埃及朋友一起徒步撒哈拉沙漠。

examples.english: The trek to the campsite was along bush tracks and down cliffs.
examples.chinese: 徒步去营地要顺着灌木小径并爬下峭壁一直走。

centers.english: a single movement when you are walking

examples.english: I was so breathless, I could only manage a few steps.
examples.chinese: 我气喘得厉害，只能勉强走几步。

examples.english: Zoe took a step forward to let the man pass.
examples.chinese: 佐薇向前走一步，让那男子过去。

examples.english: She walked briskly, with quick, short steps.
examples.chinese: 她走路轻快，脚步急促。

examples.english: Suddenly Rachel heard footsteps behind her in the dark street.
examples.chinese: 突然，在黑漆漆的街上，雷切尔听到身后有脚步声。

examples.english: I could always recognise my mother's footsteps as she scurried up the stairs.
examples.chinese: 我总能听得出母亲匆匆跑上楼的脚步声。

examples.english: He took a couple of paces forward, then stopped.
examples.chinese: 他向前走了几步，然后停下来。

examples.english: Eddie walked a few paces behind his mother, his head hung low.
examples.chinese: 埃迪跟在母亲身后数步，头垂得低低的。

examples.english: I'd gone about ten paces, when I heard a strange sound behind me.
examples.chinese: 我走了大约十步时，听见后面有个奇怪的声音。

examples.english: In four angry strides, Stuart was through the front gate.
examples.chinese: 斯图尔特气得四大步便从前门走了出去。

examples.english: Doctor Jameson hurried past us, taking long strides.
examples.chinese: 詹姆森医生迈着大步从我们身边匆匆走过。

centers.english: the way someone walks

examples.english: The woman's stiff, agitated walk showed how nervous she was.
examples.chinese: 那个女人僵直、局促不安的步态说明她有多紧张。

examples.english: Janet had the slow, leaning walk of an expectant mother.
examples.chinese: 珍妮特走路慢腾腾的，身子有点倾斜，就是孕妇的样子。

examples.english: Melanie walked with the slightly awkward gait of a very tall person.
examples.chinese: 梅拉妮个子非常高，脚步稍微有点笨拙。

examples.english: The old man approached the counter with a stooped, shuffling gait.
examples.chinese: 老人弯腰曲背，拖着脚步走向柜台。

centers.english: someone who is walking

examples.english: Banning traffic from the shopping areas has made life much more pleasant for pedestrians.
examples.chinese: 购物区禁止车辆通行使行人活动舒适多了。

examples.english: The man lost control of his car, killing a pedestrian.
examples.chinese: 那个男子驾车失控，撞死一名行人。

examples.english: There's a rough track through the woods for riders and walkers.
examples.chinese: 树林中有一条崎岖小径，供骑车人和远足者使用。

examples.english: They ran a hostel for hikers in the White Mountains.
examples.chinese: 他们在怀特山经营一家为徒步旅行者服务的旅馆。

centers.english: to run

examples.english: You'll have to run or you'll miss the bus.
examples.chinese: 你得跑了，否则就赶不上公共汽车。

examples.english: He kept on running until he was out in the open country.
examples.chinese: 他不停地跑，一直跑到野外。

examples.english: A dog ran straight out in front of my car.
examples.chinese: 一条狗直往外跑，跑到我的车前面。

examples.english: Run to the bathroom and get a towel.
examples.chinese: 跑到浴室去拿条毛巾。

examples.english: Just running for the bus leaves me out of breath.
examples.chinese: 光是跑一下赶公共汽车就让我上气不接下气了。

examples.english: They grabbed her purse and then ran off towards the subway.
examples.chinese: 他们抢了她的钱包，然后往地下人行道跑去。

examples.english: Neil tried to catch the frightened animal, but it ran away from him.
examples.chinese: 尼尔想抓住那只受了惊的动物，可是它逃脱了。

examples.english: The kids were running around and being silly.
examples.chinese: 孩子们到处跑来跑去在胡闹。

examples.english: Her dog was running after a rabbit and did not hear her calling.
examples.chinese: 她的狗在追兔子，没听见她的叫喊。

examples.english: Gillian saw two men dash past, but they didn't notice her.
examples.chinese: 吉莉恩看见两个男人飞奔过去，但他们没有注意到她。

examples.english: I eventually found the place, and dashed up the stairs.
examples.chinese: 我终于找到了那地方，于是冲上了楼梯。

examples.english: We only have a few moments, because Heidi's got to dash off soon.
examples.chinese: 我们只有一点点时间，因为海迪马上就得离开。

examples.english: It was raining, and we made a run for the car.
examples.chinese: 下雨了，我们朝汽车的方向飞奔。

examples.english: When the lecture was finally ovr, the students made a break for the exit.
examples.chinese: 讲课终于结束，学生都朝出口处奔去。

examples.english: As soon as the guard turns around, we'll make a run for it.
examples.chinese: 警卫一转身，我们就拼命逃跑。

expressions.text: make a made dash for something

examples.english: She heard the whistle and made a mad dash for the departing train.
examples.chinese: 她听到汽笛声，便朝着正离站的火车全力奔昌去。

examples.english: Margaret sprinted down the street, almost collapsing when she reached us.
examples.chinese: 玛格丽特在街上拼命地跑，追上我们时她已几乎瘫倒了。

examples.english: The bus driver must have seen me sprinting for the bus, but he drove off.
examples.chinese: 那名公共汽车司机肯定看到我朝着公共汽车全速跑去的，可是他把车开走了。

examples.english: I made a quick sprint to the local shop for some coffee.
examples.chinese: 我全速跑向当地一家商店去买咖啡。

examples.english: She trotted softly through the passageway to the gate.
examples.chinese: 她穿过走廊，轻轻地慢慢跑向大门。

examples.english: I looked up, and saw a dog trotting along the sidewalk toward me.
examples.chinese: 我抬头一看，看见一条狗在人行道上向我小跑过来。

examples.english: Dorothy arrived, with a little dog trotting along behind her.
examples.chinese: 多萝西到了，身后有一条小狗小跑着跟着她。

examples.english: Bobby tore past, shouting something about being late for work.
examples.chinese: 博比飞跑而过，嘴里叫着上班要迟到了。

examples.english: A masked man came tearing out of the bank and jumped into a waiting car.
examples.chinese: 一名蒙面男子从银行里冲出来，跳进了一辆等候着的汽车。

examples.english: Mary tore off downstairs, determined to see the visitors for herself.
examples.chinese: 玛丽飞跑下楼，打定主意要自己去见来客。

examples.english: The doors flew open, and Pascoe charged across the foyer, scattering people in all directions.
examples.chinese: 门忽然打开了，帕斯科猛冲过前厅，使得大家四散逃开。

examples.english: Riot police with batons charged at soccer fans twice during last night's international with Spain.
examples.chinese: 在昨晚与西班牙队的一场国际比赛中，防暴警察手握警棍两次向球迷冲去。

examples.english: Don't charge off, I want a word with you.
examples.chinese: 别急着走，我有话要和你说。

examples.english: Suddenly two of the prisoners broke into a run, heading as fast as they could for the fence.
examples.chinese: 突然有两名囚犯跑了起来，拼命冲向围栏。

examples.english: He walked swiftly, resisting the urge to break into a run.
examples.chinese: 他快步走着，忍住想要突然奔跑的冲动。

examples.english: A big black Alsatian dog came bounding up to her.
examples.chinese: 一条大而黑的德国牧羊犬朝她蹦跳着跑过去。

examples.english: There was a shout, and suddenly Adrian bounded into the room.
examples.chinese: 传来一声叫喊，阿德里安突然蹦蹦跳跳地跑进了房间。

centers.english: to run as a sport or for exercise
centers.chinese: 作为运动或锻炼而跑步

examples.english: I think I'll probably run for about 40 minutes, then come back for a shower.
examples.chinese: 我想我也许会跑大约40分钟，然后回来洗个淋浴。

examples.english: She runs a couple of miles twice a week.
examples.chinese: 她每周两次跑上几英里。

examples.english: I ran four miles Saturday, and I can tell you I was exhausted after it.
examples.chinese: 星期六我跑了四英里，我可以告诉你，跑完后我精疲力尽了。

examples.english: Omar's running the marathon this year.
examples.chinese: 奥马尔今年会跑马拉松。

examples.english: After his run, he took a long shower.
examples.chinese: 他跑完后好好地冲了个淋浴。

examples.english: After my first baby I took up running the sport of running to try to lose some weight.
examples.chinese: 我生下第一个孩子以后开始跑步，想减轻一点体重。

examples.english: Long distance runners follow a different training programme from other athletes.
examples.chinese: 长跑运动员的训练计划跟其他运动员的不一样。

examples.english: Have you been jogging this morning?
examples.chinese: 今天早晨你去慢跑了吗？

examples.english: When I lived in Washington, I jogged along the river every morning.
examples.chinese: 我住在华盛顿时每天早晨都沿着河边慢跑。

examples.english: There was a lady jogging down by the water with her dog.
examples.chinese: 有位女士带着狗在河边慢跑。

examples.english: I always feel better after a jog around the park.
examples.chinese: 我每次绕着公园慢跑之后总会感觉好些。

examples.english: Jogging is the only sport both Dave and I enjoy.
examples.chinese: 慢跑是戴夫和我都喜欢的唯一一项运动。

examples.english: It's surprising how many joggers you see in the park in the mornings.
examples.chinese: 早上在公园里竟能看到这么多人在慢跑，真让人吃惊。

examples.english: Kari and I are going for a run -- would you like to come?
examples.chinese: 卡里和我正要去跑步，你也一起来吗?

examples.english: Do you fancy coming jogging with me?
examples.chinese: 你想不想和我一起去慢跑？

examples.english: Athletes who have been trained to sprint aren't usually very good at running long distances.
examples.chinese: 受过短跑训练的运动员一般不是很善于长跑。

examples.english: They chose Alex for the last leg of the relay race because he was an excellent sprinter.
examples.chinese: 他们选了亚历克斯在接力赛中跑最后一段，因为他是名优秀的短跑运动员。

examples.english: I beat my personal best for the 25-metre sprint.
examples.chinese: 我打破了自己25米短跑的最佳成绩。

centers.english: to run with short quick steps

examples.english: We used to hear rats and mice scrurrying around in the attic at night.
examples.chinese: 过去我们晚上常常听到大大小小的老鼠在阁楼上到处跑。

examples.english: His aides scurried about, murmuring to each other in Russian.
examples.chinese: 他的助手们急匆匆地跑来跑去，低声地用俄语相互交谈着。

examples.english: The monkeys scampered down the tree, anxious to investigate what was happening on the ground.
examples.chinese: 那群猴子一跳一蹦地从树上跑下来，急着想知道地上发生了什么事。

examples.english: Children were scampering and wrestling in the playground.
examples.chinese: 儿童在操场上蹦蹦跳跳地跑着，并扭打成一团。

examples.english: Jenny scampered off in excitement to set up the game.
examples.chinese: 珍妮兴奋得蹦蹦跳跳地去设置游戏。

examples.english: A loud bang sent all the crabs scuttling across the sand.
examples.chinese: “砰”的一声巨响吓得螃蟹都在沙地上乱窜起来。

examples.english: He spotted a cockroach as it scuttled out from under a bin bag.
examples.chinese: 他看见一只蟑螂从垃圾袋下面窜出来。

centers.english: to move smoothly across a surface

examples.english: We slipped and slid, losing our balance on the ice
examples.chinese: 我们在冰上脚下一滑，失去了平衡。

examples.english: The children were having a great time, sliding around on the polished floor.
examples.chinese: 孩子们开心极了，在光滑的地板上滑来滑去。

examples.english: Several glasses slid off the tray and crashed to the floor.
examples.chinese: 有几个杯子从托盘上滑下来，落到地板上摔碎了。

examples.english: Harry slid across the bench so he was sitting next to me.
examples.chinese: 哈里从长凳那头移过来，坐在我身边。

examples.english: ‘Your money,’ said White, as he slid a roll of banknotes across the table.
examples.chinese: “你的钱。”怀特说着，把一卷钞票从桌子那边推过来。

examples.english: Fold the omelette over, slide it onto a plate, and serve immediately.
examples.chinese: 把煎蛋卷折过来，倒进一个盘子，然后立即端上桌。

examples.english: Your skis should glide naturally as you move across the snow.
examples.chinese: 你在雪上滑动的时候，滑雪板应该自然滑行。

examples.english: A swan glided across the surface of the lake.
examples.chinese: 一只天鹅在湖面上滑行而过。

examples.english: The rattlesnake can see in the dark and makes no noise as it glides along.
examples.chinese: 响尾蛇在黑暗中也能看见，而且移动的时候没有声音。

examples.english: The snake slithered away through the grass.
examples.chinese: 蛇从草丛中蜿蜒爬走了。

examples.english: Tom slithered down the muddy bank into the water.
examples.chinese: 汤姆连晃带滑地走下泥泞的河岸进入水中。

examples.english: As Katie fell asleep her book fell from her hands and slithered off the bed.
examples.chinese: 凯蒂睡着了，书从她手中掉下，从床上滑了下来。

examples.english: He slipped and fell. I think he's broken his arm.
examples.chinese: 他滑了一跤，跌倒了，我想他手臂跌断了。

examples.english: As the boys went down the path they slipped on the wet leaves.
examples.chinese: 男孩们走在小路上，一滑一滑地踩着湿湿的树叶。

examples.english: These glasses keep slipping down my nose.
examples.chinese: 这副眼镜老是从我鼻梁上滑下来。

examples.english: The car in front of me skidded and I slammed the brakes on to avoid it.
examples.chinese: 前面的汽车打滑了，我猛力踩刹车以免撞到它。

examples.english: Nineteen people were injured today when a bus skidded off the road into a ditch.
examples.chinese: 今天一辆公共汽车侧滑翻入一条沟里，造成19人受伤。

examples.english: They set off down the road, the dogs skidding on the hard packed snow.
examples.chinese: 他们开始沿着大路走，那些狗在硬实的雪上直打滑。

examples.english: She skidded to a halt, jumped off her scooter, and ran into the house.
examples.chinese: 她滑着停了下来，跳下踏板车，跑进了屋里。

examples.english: Turn the steering wheel in the direction of, and not against, a skid.
examples.chinese: 把方向盘顺着打滑的方向转，不要反着转。

examples.english: In wet weather cars sometimes aquaplane when you brake heavily.
examples.chinese: 下雨天如果猛踩刹车，汽车有时会打滑。

examples.english: Porous asphalt tyres were developed to cut the risk of hydroplaning.
examples.chinese: 研制多孔沥青轮胎是为了减少湿路打滑的危险。

centers.english: a surface that makes you slide

examples.english: Be careful - the floor is very slippery.
examples.chinese: 小心——地板很滑。

examples.english: The pavements are a bit slippy so we have to walk carefully.
examples.chinese: 人行道有点滑，所以我们得小心行走。

examples.english: These boots give a good grip, even on slippery rocks.
examples.chinese: 这靴子防滑力强，在湿滑的岩石上也是如此。

examples.english: In places, the towpath is slippery with mud.
examples.chinese: 曳船道有些地方满是烂泥，很滑。

examples.english: They couldn't climb up the slick lime walls of the pit.
examples.chinese: 矿井里的石灰墙滑溜溜的，他们爬不上去。

centers.english: to drive a car, train, or other vehicle
centers.chinese: 驾驶汽车、火车或其他交通工具

examples.english: Drive carefully - the roads are very icy.
examples.chinese: 小心驾车，路上结满了冰。

examples.english: They drive on the left in the UK.
examples.chinese: 在英国车是沿左边行驶的。

examples.english: I'm learning to drive. In fact, I take my test next week.
examples.chinese: 我在学驾驶，事实上我下周就要考驾驶执照了。

examples.english: We need someone to drive the school bus.
examples.chinese: 我们需要有人来开校车。

examples.english: ‘What car do you drive?’ ‘A Fiat Brava.’
examples.chinese: “你开的是什么品牌的车？”“菲亚特博悦。”

examples.english: Driving a Rolls Royce into a swimming pool was one of the most dangerous stunts Crawford had to perform.
examples.chinese: 驾驶一辆劳斯莱斯汽车冲进游泳池是克劳福德表演过的最惊险的绝技之一。

examples.english: Driving in central London is pretty unpleasant.
examples.chinese: 在伦敦市中心驾车是很讨厌的事。

examples.english: She was arrested for dangerous driving.
examples.chinese: 她因危险驾车而被捕。

examples.english: I got driving lessons for my 18th birthday.
examples.chinese: 有人送我去学驾驶作为我18岁的生日礼物。

examples.english: The man was stopped by police for an alleged driving offence.
examples.chinese: 那人因为涉嫌违章驾驶被警察拦截。

examples.english: I ride a bicycle to work every day.
examples.chinese: 我每天骑自行车上班。

examples.english: Riding a motorcycle is safer than riding a scooter.
examples.chinese: 开摩托车要比开小型摩托车安全。

examples.english: After you've been riding a bike all day, you're really glad to reach your campsite.
examples.chinese: 骑了一整天的车，到达营地时你会非常高兴。

examples.english: Even the children had a go at steering the boat.
examples.chinese: 甚至孩子们也尝试操纵船舵。

examples.english: Steer slightly to the right as you enter the bend.
examples.chinese: 进入弯道时把方向盘稍稍向右转。

examples.english: You can adjust the height of the steering wheel.
examples.chinese: 你可以调整方向盘的高低。

expressions.text: be behind the wheel/at the wheel

examples.english: An old Pontiac pulled up, with a young Mexican man at the wheel.
examples.chinese: 一辆旧的庞蒂亚克牌汽车停了下来，驾车的是一个墨西哥青年。

examples.english: Seymour was glad to be behind the wheel again after his two-year ban.
examples.chinese: 两年的禁驶令解除后，西穆很高兴又能重握方向盘。

examples.english: The King himself was at the controls when his helicopter landed.
examples.chinese: 直升机降落时是国王自己操纵的。

examples.english: The power boat, with Don at the controls, swept around the bay.
examples.chinese: 汽艇由唐驾驶着在海湾里疾驰。

examples.english: When I was a child I used to imagine myself at the controls of a fighter plane.
examples.chinese: 我小时候经常想象自己在驾驶一架战斗机。

examples.english: Joyriding is the most common type of crime among boys under 18.
examples.chinese: 偷别人的汽车兜风是18岁以下男孩中最普遍的犯罪活动。

examples.english: A group of youths went joyriding in stolen cars, causing three accidents before the police caught them.
examples.chinese: 一伙年轻人开着偷来的车到处乱撞，等警察抓住他们时已导致了三起车祸。

examples.english: Joyriders are becoming a serious problem in the inner cities.
examples.chinese: 偷车乱兜风的人成了旧城区内一个很严重的问题。

centers.english: to go somewhere in a car or other vehicle
centers.chinese: 坐汽车或别的交通工具去某地

examples.english: ‘How do you get to work?’ ‘I drive.’
examples.chinese: “你怎么去上班的？”“我自己开车去。”

examples.english: We drove to the airport, but couldn't find anywhere to park.
examples.chinese: 我们开车去机场，但找不到地方停车。

examples.english: They drove home in silence.
examples.chinese: 他们一声不吭地开车回家。

examples.english: She drove off without saying goodbye.
examples.chinese: 她再见也没说就开车走了。

examples.english: Was the restaurant worth driving 50 miles for?
examples.chinese: 开车50英里去那家饭店吃饭值不值得？

examples.english: It's a two-hour drive to Hamilton from here.
examples.chinese: 从这里开车到哈密尔顿是两小时的路程。

examples.english: One group went by car and the others took a taxi.
examples.chinese: 一组人乘汽车去，其他的人乘出租车。

examples.english: I can get to work in about 20 minutes by bike.
examples.chinese: 我骑自行车上班要大约20分钟。

examples.english: I went by boat the first time I went to Tahiti.
examples.chinese: 我第一次去塔希提岛是坐小船去的。

examples.english: Cycling isn't only good for the environment - it's a great form of exercise too.
examples.chinese: 骑自行车不仅对环境有益，而且也是一种极佳的锻炼方式。

examples.english: I usually cycle through the park to get to school.
examples.chinese: 我经常骑自行车穿过公园去学校。

examples.english: It took about 20 minutes for her to cycle the 5 miles to her home.
examples.chinese: 她回家5英里的路程骑车大约要20分钟。

expressions.text: go for a drive/ take a drive

examples.english: We'll go for a drive after lunch and see if we can find this park.
examples.chinese: 午餐后我们开车去兜兜风，看看能否找到这个公园。

examples.english: We took a drive down to the ocean.
examples.chinese: 我们开车去海边。

expressions.text: go for a ride/ take a ride

examples.english: When you've finished your work, shall we take a ride?
examples.chinese: 你完成工作后，我们开车去兜兜怎样?

examples.english: It was unusual that someone should be taking a ride on a stormy night like this.
examples.chinese: 这样风雨交加的夜里竟然有人在骑车兜风，真稀奇。

centers.english: when you take someone somewhere in a car etc

examples.english: Could you take me to the station, please?
examples.chinese: 请你开车送我去车站行吗？

examples.english: The President was driven away in a big black limousine.
examples.chinese: 总统乘坐一辆黑色豪华大轿车走了。

examples.english: He always expected his girlfriend to drive him home at the end of the night.
examples.chinese: 他夜里回家时总要女友开车送他。

examples.english: Never accept lifts from strangers.
examples.chinese: 千万别搭乘陌生人的车。

examples.english: Pedro stopped to give me a lift.
examples.chinese: 佩德罗停下来让我搭便车。

examples.english: Do you need a ride to school?
examples.chinese: 你要搭便车去上学吗?

examples.english: I accepted her offer of a lift home.
examples.chinese: 我接受了她请我搭便车回家的邀请。

examples.english: I've been in a carpool with the same three women for ten years.
examples.chinese: 我和那三位妇女拼车已有十年了。

examples.english: a proposal to open up a new network of carpool lanes
examples.chinese: 开辟合伙用车专用道新网络的提议

examples.english: We should encourage more people to carpool.
examples.chinese: 我们应该鼓励更多的人拼车。

examples.english: Despite efforts to promote carpooling, 70% of all commuters drive to work alone.
examples.chinese: 尽管努力提倡合伙用车，但上下班的人中有70%仍然在单独驾车。

centers.english: someone who drives a car, train etc

examples.english: Many drivers suffer from backache.
examples.chinese: 许多开车的人都患有背痛。

examples.english: The car was almost torn in half in the crash but amazingly the driver was unhurt.
examples.chinese: 汽车在撞车事故中几乎被撞成两截，但不可思议的是，驾驶者却没有受伤。

examples.english: Car drivers now pay more than ever for fuel.
examples.chinese: 如今的驾车者在燃料上的开销比过去任何时候都要多。

examples.english: Some women drivers are concerned about their personal safety.
examples.chinese: 一些女性驾车者担心自己的个人安全。

examples.english: He got into the driver's seat and started the engine.
examples.chinese: 他坐上驾驶座发动了引擎。

examples.english: Motorists are developing the habit of buying a new car every other year.
examples.chinese: 汽车驾驶者正形成每两年买一辆新车的习惯。

examples.english: Safety needs to be improved, not only for motorists but also for pedestrians.
examples.chinese: 需要提高行车交通安全。这不仅是为驾车者，也是为行人考虑。

examples.english: The countryside is being destroyed for the benefit of the motorist.
examples.chinese: 为了方便开车者，乡村却正在遭受破坏。

examples.english: Three motorcyclists dressed in black rode past.
examples.chinese: 三个身穿黑衣的人骑着摩托车驶过。

examples.english: The President's car arrived with its escort of police motorcyclists.
examples.chinese: 总统的汽车在警方摩托车队的护送下到达了。

examples.english: The rider wasn't badly hurt, but his bicycle was all smashed- up.
examples.chinese: 骑车的人伤得不重，但他的自行车却全撞毁了。

examples.english: The leading rider in this year's motorcycle championship is Wayne Rainey.
examples.chinese: 本年度摩托车冠军赛的领先选手是韦恩·雷尼。

examples.english: Most cyclists in London have their bikes stolen eventually.
examples.chinese: 伦敦大多数骑自行车的人车子最后都被偷走了。

examples.english: Cyclists are demanding more and safer cycle paths in the city.
examples.chinese: 骑车人要求在市内辟出更多更为安全的自行车道。

examples.english: Heather, a keen cyclist, is hoping to raise £10,000 by riding her bike across Europe.
examples.chinese: 希思，一位热心的骑车者，希望通过骑车横越欧洲筹款一万英镑。

centers.english: someone whose job it is to drive a car, train, etc
centers.chinese: 以驾驶汽车、火车等为工作的人

examples.english: The limousine pulled up outside the church and the driver got out.
examples.chinese: 豪华轿车停在教堂外，司机下了车。

examples.english: A lot of truck drivers stop at this restaurant because it's open all night.
examples.chinese: 许多卡车司机都在这家饭店歇脚，因为这里通宵营业。

examples.english: Harry asked the cab driver to stop outside the store for a couple of minutes while he bought a paper.
examples.chinese: 哈里叫出租车司机在店外面等几分钟，他去买一份报纸。

examples.english: I'll learn to drive and be some film star's chauffeur.
examples.chinese: 我要学会开车去为某个电影明星当司机。

examples.english: a chauffeur-driven Rolls Royce
examples.chinese: 一辆雇有司机驾驶的劳斯莱斯牌汽车

centers.english: to go somewhere or do something very quickly
centers.chinese: 很快地去某地或做某事

examples.english: Please hurry - this is an emergency.
examples.chinese: 请快一点，这事很急。

examples.english: We have plenty of time, there's no need to hurry.
examples.chinese: 我们有足够的时间，不必匆忙。

examples.english: Brewing beer is a long process and should not be hurried.
examples.chinese: 酿造啤酒是个很长的过程，急不得。

examples.english: The day was cold, and students hurried across campus to warm classrooms.
examples.chinese: 那天天气很冷，校园里学生都急匆匆地赶往温暖的教室。

examples.english: Elizabeth disappeared into the crowd and Donald had to hurry after her.
examples.chinese: 伊丽莎白消失在人群中，唐纳德只得赶紧追上去。

examples.english: In the kitchen Paul was hurrying to get the dinner ready before six o'clock.
examples.chinese: 厨房里，保罗在赶着在6点以前把晚餐准备好。

examples.english: Try to do your work calmly and carefully, without rushing.
examples.chinese: 尽量安安静静、仔仔细细地做作业，不要匆忙。

examples.english: The book was rushed into print, and there are a lot of mistakes in it.
examples.chinese: 这本书仓促付印，所以里面错误很多。

examples.english: If you rush your meals, you'll get indigestion.
examples.chinese: 吃饭太急会消化不良的。

examples.english: Everyone rushed out into the street to see what was happening.
examples.chinese: 大家都赶到大街上看发生了什么事。

examples.english: We rushed around trying to get all the information we needed before the end of the week.
examples.chinese: 我们忙得团团转，想赶在周末之前找到我们所需要的全部资料。

examples.english: Zack rushed to tell her what had happened.
examples.chinese: 扎克赶紧把所发生的事情告诉她。

examples.english: I rushed over to meet him.
examples.chinese: 我赶紧上前去迎接他。

examples.english: Don't try to rush things in a new relationship.
examples.chinese: 刚刚建立一段新的关系时不能仓促行事。

examples.english: Kids were dashing across the playground chasing a ball.
examples.chinese: 孩子们在操场上奔跑着抢一个球。

examples.english: He just dashed into the office and then dashed out again without speaking to anyone.
examples.chinese: 他冲进办公室，一会儿又冲了出去，和谁也没有说话。

examples.english: She dashed off to the airport and just managed to catch her plane.
examples.chinese: 她匆匆赶去机场，正好赶上飞机。

examples.english: I dashed downstairs to answer the phone.
examples.chinese: 我冲到楼下去接电话。

examples.english: She had left in a hurry, and had forgotten her driver's license.
examples.chinese: 她走得很急，忘了带驾驶证。

expressions.text: be in a hurry/rush to do something

examples.english: We were in a hurry to get back to the office.
examples.chinese: 我们急着要赶回办公室。

examples.english: Why are they in such a rush to sell the house?
examples.chinese: 他们为什么这样急着要卖房子？

examples.english: She hurried through her breakfast with her eye on the clock.
examples.chinese: 她眼睛看着时钟，急匆匆地吃完了早餐。

examples.english: Don't rush through the chapters; take notes as you read.
examples.chinese: 不要匆匆翻阅章节，要边看边做笔记。

examples.english: People hurried through their morning chores, wanting to get to the fair.
examples.chinese: 人们急急地做完早上的家务，想到集市去。

examples.english: He dressed hurriedly and went to answer the door.
examples.chinese: 他急忙穿好衣服去开门。

examples.english: The official hurriedly glanced through my papers and stamped my passport.
examples.chinese: 那官员匆匆扫视了一下我的文件，就在我的护照上盖了章。

examples.english: ‘Sorry,’ Alice said, as she hurriedly put out her cigarette.
examples.chinese: “对不起。”艾丽斯说着，赶紧熄灭了香烟。

examples.english: She had married in haste and regretted it ever since.
examples.chinese: 她草率地结了婚，从此一直懊悔不已。

expressions.text: in your haste to do something

examples.english: In his haste to leave, he almost forgot his coat.
examples.chinese: 他急于离去，差点忘了拿外套。

centers.english: what you say to tell someone to hurry

expressions.gram: intransitive phrasal verb not in progressive or past

examples.english: Hurry up or you'll be late for school.
examples.chinese: 快点，否则你上学就迟到了。

examples.english: If you want tickets, you'd better hurry up. There's only a few left.
examples.chinese: 如果你要购票就得赶快，只剩最后几张了。

examples.english: Hurry up and get your things, it's time to go.
examples.chinese: 快点，拿好你的东西，该走了。

examples.english: Come on, you two, we're going to be late.
examples.chinese: 你们两个快点吧，我们要迟到了。

examples.english: Oh come on! We don't have all day!
examples.chinese: 哦！快点吧！我们没多少时间了！

expressions.text: get a move on/ get moving

examples.english: Come on Sally, get a move on!
examples.chinese: 好了萨利，快点！

examples.english: I think we'd better get moving, it's only five minutes to boarding time.
examples.chinese: 我想我们得赶紧了，离登机时间只有五分钟了。

examples.english: Step on it. We have a plane to catch.
examples.chinese: 开快一点，我们要赶飞机。

examples.english: You'll have to step on it if you're going to be there by eleven thirty.
examples.chinese: 如果你想在11点半以前到达那儿就得开快点。

examples.english: Get cracking you people! I want the whole house cleaned by four o'clock.
examples.chinese: 你们这帮人赶紧动手干！4点前必须把整幢房子打扫干净！

examples.english: When Alf arrives we'll get cracking moving the furniture.
examples.chinese: 阿尔夫到达以后，我们将赶紧动手搬家具。

examples.english: It's time you got cracking with your homework.
examples.chinese: 现在你该快点做作业了。

examples.english: I need to have that job done before lunch. Come on, jump to it!
examples.chinese: 那工作午饭前必须完成。来吧，赶快动手！

centers.english: to make someone hurry

examples.english: They're taking a long time to finish. Can you hurry them up a little?
examples.chinese: 他们要花很长时间才能完成。你能否催催他们？

examples.english: I think she's nearly finished packing. I'll go hurry her up.
examples.chinese: 我想她差不多收拾好行李了，我去催她一下。

examples.english: I wish you wouldn't hurry me. I'm being as quick as I can.
examples.chinese: 希望你不要再催我，我已经在尽快了。

examples.english: Don't rush me! You'll only make me get it wrong.
examples.chinese: 不要催我！你这样反而会让我弄错。

examples.english: I'm sorry to rush you, but we don't have much time left.
examples.chinese: 对不起，我要来催你了，我们剩下的时间不多了。

examples.english: He'll get the job done, but you need to chivvy him along a bit.
examples.chinese: 他会把这工作做完的，但你需要稍微多催催他。

centers.english: done quickly because you are hurrying
centers.chinese: 因为赶时间所以很快做成的

examples.english: After a hurried dinner, the boys do their homework or watch TV.
examples.chinese: 匆匆吃完晚饭，男孩子们或做功课，或看电视。

examples.english: They made a hurried search for the missing letters, but they couldn't find them.
examples.chinese: 他们匆匆找了一下那些遗失的信件，但没找到。

examples.english: Her handwriting looked shaky and hurried.
examples.chinese: 她的字看上去歪歪扭扭，写得很草。

examples.english: Do I have time for a quick shower?
examples.chinese: 我来得及很快洗个澡吗？

examples.english: Could I just make a quick phone call?
examples.chinese: 我就很快打个电话可以吗？

examples.english: The house is priced for a quick sale.
examples.chinese: 这房子标这个价格是想尽快卖出去。

examples.english: People have complained that the doctors seem rushed, with too many patients to see in a day.
examples.chinese: 人们抱怨说医生看病似乎很草率，他们一天要看太多的病人了。

examples.english: The actors’ performances were needlessly rushed.
examples.chinese: 这些演员的表演太仓促了。

examples.english: He only had time for a hasty glance at the papers.
examples.chinese: 他时间很紧，只能迅速浏览了一下报纸。

examples.english: ‘I have to go now,’ said Alex, bidding them a hasty goodbye.
examples.chinese: “现在我得走了，”亚历克斯说着，急急忙忙地向他们告别。

examples.english: I managed to leave a hastily scribbled note for John.
examples.chinese: 我匆匆忙忙地给约翰留了一个潦草的字条。

examples.english: Before the game there was a frantic rush to get the last few remaining tickets.
examples.chinese: 比赛开始前，人们纷纷争抢剩下的最后几张门票。

examples.english: The staff spent three frantic days trying to get everything ready.
examples.chinese: 员工们紧张地忙乱了三天，想把一切准备就绪。

examples.english: Throughout the night, everyone mopped floors and washed walls in a frantic effort to clean the place up for the inspectors.
examples.chinese: 大家拖地的拖地，擦墙的擦墙，整整忙了一个晚上，把地方打扫干净，等待检查人员的到来。

examples.english: Mel was frantically trying to finish all her work before the deadline.
examples.chinese: 梅尔正在紧张地赶她的工作，想在最后期限之前做完。

examples.english: The show was about to begin and behind the stage there were scenes of feverish activity.
examples.chinese: 演出就要开始了，舞台后面一片闹哄哄的景象。

examples.english: Feverish preparations were being made for the arrival of the President.
examples.chinese: 大家都在紧张忙乱地做准备工作，等待总统的到访。

examples.english: Negotiators worked feverishly late into the night to hammer out an agreement.
examples.chinese: 谈判人员紧张地工作到深夜，以敲定协议。

examples.english: I don't want a rush job. I want this work done right.
examples.chinese: 我不想要仓促赶出来的工作，我要它做得妥当。

examples.english: Getting back into condition after a serious injury can't be a rush job.
examples.chinese: 受了重伤之后要让身体康复是急不得的事。

centers.english: to be in a situation in which you must hurry
centers.chinese: 处于必须赶紧的情况下

examples.english: I'm sorry, I'm in a hurry. I can't stop now.
examples.chinese: 对不起，我在赶时间，现在不能停下。

examples.english: Some people always seem to be in a hurry.
examples.chinese: 某些人好像总是匆匆忙忙的。

expressions.text: be in a hurry to do something

examples.english: Would you like to stay for a coffee, or are you in a hurry to leave?
examples.chinese: 你想留下来喝杯咖啡，还是急着要走？

examples.english: I don't want to seem rude but I'm very pressed for time. Could I call you back later?
examples.chinese: 我并不想显得这么无礼，但是现在时间很紧，我过会儿给你回电话好吗?

examples.english: I can't stop right now, I'm a little pushed for time.
examples.chinese: 我现在不能停下，时间有点紧。

examples.english: In advertising you're always working against the clock, trying to meet deadlines.
examples.chinese: 干广告这一行总是要赶时间，赶限期。

expressions.text: work/race against the clock to do something

examples.english: We really had to work against the clock to finish the report on time.
examples.chinese: 为了按时完成报告，我们不得不加紧工作。

examples.english: Battleship repair crews swung into action in a race against time
examples.chinese: 战列舰修理人员赶紧投入行动。

examples.english: In an urgent race against time, the Coast Guard and marine biologists struggled to rescue a whale that had beached itself on the shore.
examples.chinese: 海岸警卫队和海洋生物学家争分夺秒，奋力营救一条搁浅在海岸上的鲸鱼。

examples.english: I had forgotten my wallet in the usual Monday morning rush.
examples.chinese: 星期一早晨像往常一样忙乱，我忘记带钱包了。

examples.english: There was a furious rush to have everything ready for the opening night.
examples.chinese: 大家紧张地忙乱着为首演之夜做好一切准备工作。不必匆忙

centers.english: what you say to tell someone not to hurry
centers.chinese: 告诉某人不必匆忙时所说的话

examples.english: There's no hurry. The train never leaves on time.
examples.chinese: 不要急，火车从不准点发车。

examples.english: You can hand in your report any time next week. There's no rush.
examples.chinese: 下周任何时候你都可以交报告，不用急。

examples.english: Take your time, think the matter over carefully, and then tell me what you've decided.
examples.chinese: 不要着急，仔细考虑考虑这件事，然后把你的决定告诉我。

examples.english: Just take your time, and speak slowly and clearly.
examples.chinese: 不要急，慢慢说，说清楚点。

examples.english: What's the hurry? The plane doesn't leave for another two hours.
examples.chinese: 急什么？飞机还要两个小时才起飞呢。

examples.english: ‘I'd better go.’ ‘Why? What's the rush?’
examples.chinese: “我要走了。”“为什么？干什么这么急？”

centers.english: not hurrying

expressions.text: be in no hurry/not be in any hurry

examples.english: I'm in no hurry. I can wait.
examples.chinese: 我不急，我可以等。

examples.english: He wasn't in any hurry, so he decided to take a look around town.
examples.chinese: 他并不赶时间，所以决定在镇上四处看看。

expressions.text: be in no hurry/not be in any hurry to do something

examples.english: Danny seemed in no hurry to pay me the money he owed me.
examples.chinese: 丹尼好像不着急归还他欠我的钱。

examples.english: He swam gracefully, with easy, unhurried strokes.
examples.chinese: 他游泳很优雅，划水动作轻松自如，不慌不忙。

examples.english: It's a community of old trees, big houses, and an unhurried life.
examples.chinese: 那是一个古树环抱的社区，大大的房子，闲适的生活。

examples.english: She walked along, calm and unhurried.
examples.chinese: 她往前走着，神色从容，不慌不忙。

examples.english: She enjoyed a leisurely breakfast and had time to read the newspaper.
examples.chinese: 她悠闲地用完早餐，还有时间看报纸。

examples.english: The horse walked at a leisurely pace through the flat Fenland countryside.
examples.chinese: 那匹马迈着悠闲的脚步走在芬兰区平坦的乡野上。

centers.english: to follow someone

examples.english: Follow me and I'll show you where the library is.
examples.chinese: 跟我来，我指给你看图书馆在哪儿。

examples.english: You drive on ahead and I'll follow.
examples.chinese: 你在前面开车，我来跟着。

examples.english: My little brother's been following me around all day.
examples.chinese: 我的弟弟整天到处跟着我。

examples.english: The woman entered the room, followed by three young children.
examples.chinese: 那妇女进入房间，后面跟着三个小孩。

examples.english: She didn't notice that Jack had followed her into the kitchen.
examples.chinese: 她未注意到杰克跟着她进了厨房。

examples.english: You go ahead. I'll follow on later.
examples.chinese: 你先走，我稍后就来。

examples.english: The bus set off first and we followed on behind in the car.
examples.chinese: 公共汽车先走，我们开着汽车跟在后面。

centers.english: to closely follow a person or animal in order to watch them
centers.chinese: 为监视而紧紧跟踪某人或某动物

examples.english: He followed her home to find out where she lived.
examples.chinese: 他跟踪她回家，想知道她住哪儿。

examples.english: Did you make sure you weren't followed on the way over here?
examples.chinese: 你确定来这里的路上没被人跟踪吗？

examples.english: She complained to the police officer that she was being followed by two strangers.
examples.chinese: 她向警察投诉说有两个陌生人在跟踪她。

examples.english: That police car has been tailing us for the last 5 miles.
examples.chinese: 在过去5英里路程中，那辆警车一直在跟踪我们。

examples.english: A group of photographers tailed the couple all over London.
examples.chinese: 一群摄影师尾随着这对夫妇在伦敦到处跑。

examples.english: A police car was on their tail within seconds of the alarm going off.
examples.chinese: 警报响后几秒钟，一辆警车就跟上他们了。

examples.english: We sat on their tail for about an hour until we lost them in traffic.
examples.chinese: 我们尾随了他们约一小时，直到他们在来往车辆中消失为止。

examples.english: I want you to shadow him for the next three days and find out who he hangs out with.
examples.chinese: 我要你随后的三天里紧紧跟踪他并查出他和谁混在一起。

examples.english: Consumed with jealousy, he shadowed her for three days, hoping to catch her with her lover.
examples.chinese: 他妒火中烧，跟踪了她三天，希望捉住她和她情夫在一起。

examples.english: Undercover agents have been tracking him for weeks.
examples.chinese: 密探已跟踪他几星期了。

examples.english: It would be impossible to trail anyone across this type of ground.
examples.chinese: 在这种地面上跟踪人是不可能的。

examples.english: The police trailed the gang to their hideout.
examples.chinese: 警察追踪匪徒到他们的藏身地。

examples.english: Dogs are used to track the wolves to their lair in the forest.
examples.chinese: 用狗来追踪狼在林中的巢穴。

examples.english: After the court case she was hounded relentlessly by the press.
examples.chinese: 那场官司之后，她不断受到新闻界的追逼。

examples.english: The couple found themselves hounded by photographers as they left the church.
examples.chinese: 这对夫妇离开教堂时受到了摄影师们的纠缠。

examples.english: Polar bears stalk seals that are resting on the ice.
examples.chinese: 北极熊悄悄地逼近在冰上休息着的海豹。

examples.english: The killer would stalk his victim, overpower her and then brutally murder her.
examples.chinese: 这个杀手会悄悄地跟着他的受害者，把她制伏，然后残忍地杀了她。

examples.english: Women's groups are demanding that Congress toughen the law against stalkers.
examples.chinese: 妇女团体要求国会针对跟踪者制定更为严厉的法律。

examples.english: The stalker's reappearance has led to increased security around the actress.
examples.chinese: 这名跟踪者的再次出现使女演员加强了身边的保安措施。

centers.english: to follow a person or animal quickly in order to catch them
centers.chinese: 迅速跟踪人或动物以抓获他[它]

examples.english: A group of little boys ran after him to ask for his autograph.
examples.chinese: 一群小男孩跟在他身后要求他签名。

examples.english: She's left her briefcase. Quick! Run after her!
examples.chinese: 她忘记拿公文包了。快！追上她！

examples.english: I didn't have the energy to chase him any more.
examples.chinese: 我再也没有力气追赶他了。

examples.english: The farmer chased the children across the field.
examples.chinese: 那个农民追赶着孩子们跑过田地。

examples.english: We chased after him for about five blocks but then we lost him and had to turn back.
examples.chinese: 我们追了他大约有五个街区，后来就看不见他了，只得往回走。

examples.english: Police pursued the gunman into an abandoned building.
examples.chinese: 警察追捕持枪歹徒进入一幢废弃的建筑内。

examples.english: The ship was being pursued by enemy submarines.
examples.chinese: 这艘船正被敌人的潜水艇紧追不舍。

examples.english: The robbers sped off in a stolen car with three police vehicles in pursuit.
examples.chinese: 盗贼开着一辆偷来的汽车疾速离去，三辆警车在后面追赶。

examples.english: Cheng raced through a crowded shopping mall in pursuit of the man who had grabbed her purse.
examples.chinese: 程穿过拥挤的购物中心快速追赶那名抢了她钱包的男子。

examples.english: A deer suddenly sprang across the road, with a pack of hunting dogs in hot pursuit.
examples.chinese: 一头鹿突然蹿过马路，后面一群猎狗紧追不舍。

examples.english: The rebels headed for the border but government troops were still at their heels.
examples.chinese: 叛军开往边境，可政府军仍紧追不舍。

examples.english: Just 15 minutes into the race Lawson was already hot on the champion's heels.
examples.chinese: 比赛开始才15分钟，洛森已紧紧跟上了冠军。

examples.english: He rushed out of the theatre with a pack of reporters at his heels.
examples.chinese: 他冲出剧院，一群记者紧跟其后。

examples.english: Don't go after him now. Let him calm down first.
examples.chinese: 现在别去跟着他，先让他平静下来。

examples.english: Police spotted the car speeding on Dumbarton Bridge and quickly gave chase.
examples.chinese: 警方发现那辆汽车在敦巴顿大桥上快速行驶，便迅速开始追赶。

centers.english: to look for someone or something when you do not know where they are
centers.chinese: 寻找不知道在何处的某人或某物

examples.english: I'm looking for Simon -- have you seen him?
examples.chinese: 我正在找西蒙—你看见过他吗?

examples.english: I've been looking everywhere for that key! Where did you find it?
examples.chinese: 我到处找那把钥匙！你在哪里找到的？

examples.english: Jill was up in the attic trying to find her old school books.
examples.chinese: 吉尔在阁楼上找她以前的课本。

examples.english: I tried to find Jack to tell him the news, but he wasn't anywhere around.
examples.chinese: 我想找到杰克告诉他这个消息，可是他不见踪影。

examples.english: ‘I can't find my shoes.’ ‘Have a look in your bedroom.’
examples.chinese: “我找不到我的鞋子。”“到卧室里看看。”

examples.english: I had a look for Clive but he wasn't in his office.
examples.chinese: 我找了一下克莱夫，可他不在办公室里。

examples.english: I'll just have a quick look for that book before we go.
examples.chinese: 我们走之前，我想很快找一下那本书。

examples.english: We don't have time to find it now - we'll have a good look in the morning.
examples.chinese: 现在我们没有时间去找—明天早上再仔细找吧。

examples.english: Coastguards are still searching for survivors from the ship.
examples.chinese: 海岸警卫队仍在搜寻船上的幸存者。

examples.english: Friends and neighbours joined police officers to search for clues.
examples.chinese: 朋友和邻居也一起协助警察查找线索。

examples.english: She stayed and talked for a while, then went off in search of Flynn.
examples.chinese: 她留下来说了一会儿话，然后去找弗林了。

examples.english: Friends and neighbors hunted everywhere, but no-one could find the child.
examples.chinese: 朋友和邻居到处都找遍了，但没有人能找到那孩子。

examples.english: I hunted all morning for the book of photos, but couldn't find it.
examples.chinese: 我整个早上都在找那本影集，还是没有找到。

centers.english: to look for something or someone that you need or want
centers.chinese: 寻找你需要或想要的某物或某人

examples.english: Can you help me? I'm looking for a place to stay.
examples.chinese: 能帮个忙吗？我在找住的地方。

examples.english: The company is looking for young, enthusiastic graduates to work in its sales department.
examples.chinese: 该公司诚聘年轻、有热情的大学毕业生加入销售部工作。

examples.english: I spent months looking for a job, with no luck.
examples.chinese: 我找了好几个月的工作，都没有运气。

examples.english: I spent half an hour trying to find a parking space.
examples.chinese: 我花了半小时找一个停车的地方。

examples.english: Drug companies are trying to find an alternative drug, which will not have such serious side-effects.
examples.chinese: 制药公司想找一种不会产生这么大的副作用的替代药品。

examples.english: Many young people move to the cities in search of a better future.
examples.chinese: 许多年轻人都搬到城里寻找更美好的前途。

examples.english: I searched everywhere for a birthday present for Kim, but I found nothing I liked.
examples.chinese: 我四处寻找可以送给金的生日礼物，但没找到我所喜欢的东西。

examples.english: I was made redundant last year, and am still searching for a new job.
examples.chinese: 我去年被裁员，现在还在找工作。

examples.english: The school is currently searching for ways to save money.
examples.chinese: 学校目前正在想办法省钱。

examples.english: He looked around for somewhere to hang the wet towel.
examples.chinese: 他四处寻找能挂湿毛巾的地方。

examples.english: We're always looking around for new products to add to our list.
examples.chinese: 我们总是在寻找新产品加入我们的目录中。

examples.english: They're looking around for a decent apartment, not too far from the city.
examples.chinese: 他们在四处寻找一套体面的公寓，离市中心不要太远。

examples.english: Economics graduate, aged 25, with business experience, seeks interesting part-time work in the West London area.
examples.chinese: 经济学的毕业生，25岁，有商贸工作经验，寻找伦敦西区有趣味的兼职工作。

examples.english: If you are at all worried about your symptoms, you should seek medical advice.
examples.chinese: 如果你很担心自己的这些症状，就应该求医。

examples.english: The government is seeking support from teachers for its latest education reforms.
examples.chinese: 政府正在为它最近推行的教育改革寻求教师的支持。

examples.english: My mother's always on the lookout for a good bargain.
examples.chinese: 我母亲总是留意着有什么便宜货。

examples.english: The club is always on the lookout for new young players.
examples.chinese: 这家俱乐部总是在寻找年轻的新球员。

examples.english: The design department is constantly on the lookout for original ideas.
examples.chinese: 设计部在不断地寻找有创意的构思。

examples.english: We rented a car and went house-hunting as soon as we arrived.
examples.chinese: 我们一到此地便租了辆汽车，开始找房子。

examples.english: Job hunting takes a lot of effort and can be a long, depressing process.
examples.chinese: 找工作很费心，有时是一个漫长、令人沮丧的过程。

examples.english: If a solution can be found, Mr Danby, I shall leave no stone unturned until I have found it.
examples.chinese: 丹比先生，如果能够找到解决途径，我会想尽一切办法，一定要找到。

examples.english: Union leaders have promised to leave no stone unturned in their search for a way to keep the factory open.
examples.chinese: 工会领导人承诺要想方设法让这家工厂运作下去。

centers.english: to look for information in a book, on a computer etc
centers.chinese: 在书中、电脑上等寻找信息

examples.english: You can search on the Internet for the names of dealers in your area.
examples.chinese: 你可以在互联网上查找你所在地区经销商的名字。

examples.english: I searched a few websites, but couldn't find the information I was looking for.
examples.chinese: 我找了几个网站，但是没有找到我所要的信息。

examples.english: I did a quick search on the Internet and found three airlines with tickets available on that date.
examples.chinese: 我在互联网上快速查找了一下，发现那天有三家航空公司有机票。

examples.english: Many youngsters spend hours surfing the net.
examples.chinese: 许多年轻人花很长时间上网浏览信息。

examples.english: If you look up the title of the book, you should then find the author.
examples.chinese: 你如果查找书名，就会找到作者。

examples.english: If you don't know what it means, look it up in the dictionary.
examples.chinese: 如果不知道是什么意思，就查词典。

examples.english: He went through the contract quite carefully, checking all the details.
examples.chinese: 他仔仔细细地审阅了一遍这份合同，检查了所有的细节。

examples.english: I've finished my essay, but I just need to go through it to check for spelling mistakes.
examples.chinese: 我已写完论文，但还需看一遍检查拼写错误。

examples.english: She spent hours trawling through patients’ medical records looking for evidence of negligence by doctors.
examples.chinese: 她花了很长时间翻看病人的医疗记录，想找出证明医生失职的证据。

examples.english: A trawl through the jobs pages of his local paper produced only one job that he wanted to apply for.
examples.chinese: 他翻了一下本地报纸上的求职版，只有一份他想去应聘的工作。

examples.english: He spent half an hour scouring the newspaper for any mention of the fire.
examples.chinese: 他花了半个小时在报纸上寻找有关这场火灾的报道。

examples.english: Browsing the net one afternoon, I came across Tom's homepage.
examples.chinese: 一天下午，我在上网浏览的时候找到了汤姆的个人网页。

examples.english: One company said that up to half of their employees spend over an hour's work time a day browsing the web.
examples.chinese: 一家公司说，他们有一半的员工每天花超过一小时的工作时间浏览网页。

centers.english: when the police are looking for a criminal

examples.english: Police are still looking for three men who stole £55,000 from a post office in York.
examples.chinese: 警方仍在追捕从约克市一家邮局窃取了55,000英镑的三名男子。

examples.english: Police searching for the killer of a nine-year-old girl have appealed to the public for help.
examples.chinese: 警方在搜捕杀害一名9岁女孩的凶手，他们已向公众发出了呼吁，请求协查。

examples.english: She said she was frightened because the police were after her.
examples.chinese: 她说她很害怕，因为警方在找她。

examples.english: The man we're after is one of the biggest drug-dealers in South America.
examples.chinese: 我们在追捕的那名男子是南美最大的毒贩之一。

examples.english: Police are still hunting for the girl's killer.
examples.chinese: 警方仍在搜捕杀害小女孩的凶手。

examples.english: Many opponents of the regime who escaped abroad were later hunted down and killed.
examples.chinese: 许多反对这个政权并逃往国外的人后来都遭到了追杀。

centers.english: to search a place to try to find someone or something
centers.chinese: 搜查某地试图找到某人或某物

examples.english: At the site, rescue workers have been searching systematically in the hope of finding more survivors.
examples.chinese: 救援人员正在现场进行彻底的搜索，希望能发现更多的幸存者。

examples.english: Police have searched the area near his home, but have so far found nothing.
examples.chinese: 警方已经搜查了他家附近的地区，但到目前为止没有找到什么东西。

examples.english: Local people are still searching woods near the victim's home for any clues to help find her killer.
examples.chinese: 当地的人仍在被害人住所附近的林子里寻找线索，帮助找到杀害她的凶手。

examples.english: A team of detectives is scouring the area for the murder weapon.
examples.chinese: 一队警探正在该地区搜查杀人的凶器。

examples.english: Developers are scouring the country for possible sites for these new superstores.
examples.chinese: 开发商正在乡间择址建造这几家新的超级市场。

examples.english: Police and volunteers are combing the countryside in the hope of finding the missing boy.
examples.chinese: 警察和志愿人员正在乡间仔细搜寻，希望能找到失踪的男孩。

examples.english: Rescuers combed the hillsides but found no trace of the missing climbers.
examples.chinese: 救援人员彻底搜查了山腰，但是找不到失踪登山者的任何踪迹。

examples.english: The children combed the shoreline for shells.
examples.chinese: 孩子们在岸边寻找贝壳。

examples.english: The murder weapon, a kitchen knife, was found when police dragged a nearby pond.
examples.chinese: 警方用拖网在附近的一个池塘里打捞起了凶器，是一把菜刀。

examples.english: Thieves broke in and ransacked the house.
examples.chinese: 小偷破门而入，把屋子洗劫一空。

examples.english: The newspaper's offices were ransacked by members of the secret police.
examples.chinese: 报社的办公室被秘密警察搜了个遍。

examples.english: The Casino nightclub has been closed since it was raided last month.
examples.chinese: 这家赌场夜总会自上月遭突击搜查以后就关门了。

examples.english: Police officers raided a house in North London last night, and found substantial quantities of illegal drugs.
examples.chinese: 昨晚警方突击搜查伦敦北部的一所住宅，发现大量的违禁药品。

examples.english: We turned all the cupboards inside out but couldn't find the letters.
examples.chinese: 我们翻箱倒柜地找，但是没有找到那些信件。

examples.english: I turned the house upside down looking for my birth certificate.
examples.chinese: 我把房子翻了个底朝天，寻找我的出生证。

centers.english: to look for something in a bag, box, pocket etc
centers.chinese: 在包、箱子、口袋等里面寻找

examples.english: Louise rummaged in her bag and pulled out a small envelope.
examples.chinese: 路易丝在手提包里翻找，扯出一个小小的信封。

examples.english: He unzipped his school bag and rummaged about for a pencil.
examples.chinese: 他拉开书包的拉链，在里面翻找铅笔。

examples.english: Sam fished about in his pockets and produced a few coins.
examples.chinese: 萨姆在口袋里摸摸，找出几枚硬币来。

examples.english: He fished around in his bag for the letter.
examples.chinese: 他在手提包里摸着找那封信。

examples.english: She put her hand under the seat and felt around, but couldn't find anything.
examples.chinese: 她伸手到座位底下摸了摸，但什么也没有找到。

examples.english: She felt around for the light switch.
examples.chinese: 她摸着找电灯的开关。

examples.english: The phone rang and, half-asleep, Winston fumbled about to find the receiver.
examples.chinese: 电话铃响了，温斯顿于半梦半醒之间笨手笨脚地摸索着找话筒。

examples.english: He fumbled around in the cupboard, desperately searching for his tablets.
examples.chinese: 他在柜子里乱摸一通，急着找他的药片。

examples.english: He fumbled around on the ground for his glasses.
examples.chinese: 他在地上乱摸找他的眼镜。

examples.english: Customs officials went through his luggage but found nothing.
examples.chinese: 海关官员仔细地检查了他的行李，但没有发现什么东西。

examples.english: You have no right to go through my personal possessions!
examples.chinese: 你没有权利搜查我的私人物品！

examples.english: All visitors to the prison are thoroughly searched.
examples.chinese: 进监狱探访的人全都经过彻底搜身。

examples.english: The men were all searched for weapons and then allowed to enter.
examples.chinese: 所有男性都被搜查有没有带武器，然后才允许进入。

examples.english: A guard frisked him expertly, then led him into a large room.
examples.chinese: 一名卫兵熟练地搜了搜他的身，然后把他领进一个很大的房间。

examples.english: We were stopped by the police and frisked before being allowed into the building.
examples.chinese: 我们被警察拦住搜了身，然后才获准进入大楼。

examples.english: They pulled me aside and frisked me for weapons.
examples.chinese: 他们把我拉到一边，查我身上有没有武器。

centers.english: an attempt to find something or someone
centers.chinese: 想要找到某人或某物的尝试

examples.english: Rescuers are continuing their search for survivors of the crash.
examples.chinese: 救援人员继续在搜救事故的生还者。

examples.english: Their search for gold took them west to Washington State.
examples.chinese: 他们为了寻找金子来到了西部的华盛顿州。

examples.english: FBI agents carried out a search of all the nightclubs in the area.
examples.chinese: 联邦调查局的探员对该地区所有的夜总会都进行了一次搜查。

examples.english: When the men did not return, the commanding officer sent out a search party.
examples.chinese: 那些人没有回来，指挥官便派出了一支搜索队。

examples.english: Police are carrying out house-to-house searches in villages near the scene of the murder.
examples.chinese: 警方对凶杀案现场附近的村子进行挨家挨户的搜查。

examples.english: Police have launched a nationwide hunt for the killer.
examples.chinese: 警方在全国范围内发起了一场追捕凶手的行动。

examples.english: Raids are almost a nightly occurrence at this club.
examples.chinese: 这家俱乐部几乎每天晚上都要被突击检查。

examples.english: Seven people were injured in last night's police raid on a house in Brixton, South London.
examples.chinese: 昨天夜里，警方对位于南伦敦布里克斯顿的一所住宅进行突袭搜查，有七人受伤。

examples.english: I had a feeling that the trip up to Edinburgh might turn out to be a wild goose chase.
examples.chinese: 我有种感觉，这次去爱丁堡说不定是白跑一趟。

expressions.text: send/take somebody on a wild goose chase

examples.english: He lied and took us on a wild goose chase to North Yorkshire.
examples.chinese: 他说了谎，带着我们白白地跑了一趟北约克郡。

centers.english: to escape from somewhere where you are in danger

examples.english: Only four people managed to escape before the roof collapsed.
examples.chinese: 只有四人得以在屋顶坍塌之前逃了出来。!

examples.english: The refugees have crossed miles of desert to escape civil war and famine.
examples.chinese: 难民们千里迢迢穿过沙漠，就为躲避内战和饥荒。

examples.english: Josie managed to escape from her attacker and call the police.
examples.chinese: 乔茜设法摆脱袭击者并报了警。

examples.english: Some people were able to escape over the border into Tanzania.
examples.chinese: 有些人越过边境逃到了坦桑尼亚。

examples.english: When the army began killing civilians in the town, he was able to escape through the jungle.
examples.chinese: 军队开始屠杀镇上平民时，他得以从丛林逃脱了。

examples.english: Eventually we realized there was no way of getting out.
examples.chinese: 我们终于意识到已经无路可逃了。

examples.english: A few people managed to get out before the government crackdown.
examples.chinese: 有些人设法在政府垮台之前逃了出去。

examples.english: All US tourists and journalists are being advised to get out of the country as soon as possible.
examples.chinese: 所有的美国游客和记者都接到忠告，应尽快离开那个国家。

examples.english: The whole building was on fire - we were lucky to get out alive!
examples.chinese: 整栋大楼都着火了—我们能活着出来真是幸运啊！

examples.english: Don't run away - I'm not going to hurt you.
examples.chinese: 别逃，我不会伤害你的。

examples.english: Delia managed to get away from the man and ran off screaming.
examples.chinese: 迪莉娅设法挣脱了那人，尖叫着逃走了。

examples.english: If you run away from the bull, it's almost certain to attack you.
examples.chinese: 如果你从公牛身边逃走，那它几乎是肯定要攻击你的。

examples.english: He jumped out of the car and ran off into the woods.
examples.chinese: 他跳下汽车跑进了树林。

examples.english: One of the horses got into a panic and bolted.
examples.chinese: 其中一匹马受了惊，突然狂奔起来。

examples.english: When police approached him to ask him some questions, he bolted.
examples.chinese: 警察上前想问他几个问题，他拔腿就跑。

examples.english: Before I could say a word, she turned and bolted out the front door.
examples.chinese: 我还没来得及开口，她转身就从前门跑了。

expressions.text: run for it/make a run for it

examples.english: Somebody's coming. Quick, run for it.
examples.chinese: 有人来了，快跑。

examples.english: There's no way we can beat them - we're going to have to make a run for it.
examples.chinese: 我们是打不过他们的——只有快点逃走了。

examples.english: When police arrived, the two men fled.
examples.chinese: 警察一来，那两个人就逃跑了。

examples.english: Most of the women there were Somalis fleeing from the civil war.
examples.chinese: 那里大部分妇女都是躲避内战的索马里人。

examples.english: Up to five million political refugees have fled to other countries.
examples.chinese: 有近500万名政治难民逃到了其他国家。

examples.english: Rollins tried to flee the country but was stopped at the airport.
examples.chinese: 罗林斯企图逃出国境，但是在机场被截住了。

examples.english: He made his escape by climbing through the window and down the fire escape.
examples.chinese: 他是爬窗顺着太平梯逃走的。

examples.english: The hostages spent days waiting for the opportunity to make their escape.
examples.chinese: 人质天天都在等待逃跑的机会。

examples.english: The kids immediately took to their heels as Mrs Brewster appeared around the corner.
examples.chinese: 布鲁斯特太太一出现在拐角处，孩子们立刻撒腿就跑。

examples.english: He jumped off the train, took to his heels, and was quickly out of sight.
examples.chinese: 他跳下火车就飞跑起来，一转眼就不见了身影。

examples.english: She wanted to break away, but his grip was too strong.
examples.chinese: 她想挣脱，可是他抓得太紧了。

examples.english: With a violent twist he broke free and ran out of the room.
examples.chinese: 他身体用力一扭，挣脱开来跑出了房间。

examples.english: Then Tammy broke free from Judd and ran for the door screaming.
examples.chinese: 后来塔米挣脱了贾德，尖叫着冲向门口。

examples.english: The pilot bailed out of the aircraft just in time and was only slightly injured.
examples.chinese: 飞行员及时跳伞逃出了飞机，只是受了一点轻伤。

centers.english: to escape from a prison or from where someone is keeping you
centers.chinese: 从监狱逃出或从被关押的地方逃走

examples.english: Guards have been ordered to shoot anyone trying to escape.
examples.chinese: 卫兵接到命令，逃跑者格杀勿论。

examples.english: He escaped from prison in June, but was rearrested by police a month later.
examples.chinese: 他在6月越狱逃跑，但是一个月之后又被警方抓捕。

examples.english: Grant had escaped through a bathroom window while in police custody.
examples.chinese: 格兰特被警方拘留时从卫生间的窗户逃掉了。

examples.english: Some of the men were planning to break out.
examples.chinese: 其中有些人在计划出逃。

examples.english: The doors and windows are all firmly locked - I don't know how we're going to get out.
examples.chinese: 门和窗都牢牢地锁住了—我不知道我们如何逃得出去。

examples.english: In 1998 the two men broke out of jail and murdered a police officer.
examples.chinese: 1998年，那两名男子越狱出逃，杀害了一名警察。

examples.english: No one has ever managed to get out of this prison alive.
examples.chinese: 还没有人能活着逃出这座监狱。

centers.english: to succeed in escaping from someone who is chasing you

examples.english: It looks as if they've escaped. They're probably over the border by now.
examples.chinese: 看来他们已经逃掉了，现在很可能已过了边境。

examples.english: So far the terrorists have managed to escape the police.
examples.chinese: 到目前为止，恐怖分子仍未被警方抓获。

examples.english: He ducked down an alley to escape from the mob that was chasing him.
examples.chinese: 他闪进一条巷子，逃脱了正在追捕他的那群暴徒。

examples.english: Criminals generally know their neighborhood well, so it's not difficult for them to escape into the back streets.
examples.chinese: 犯罪分子一般都对邻近地区很熟悉，所以逃进一些穷街陋巷并不难。

examples.english: How could you let him get away!
examples.chinese: 你怎么能让他逃掉呢！

examples.english: Police believe the gunmen got away in a white Ford pickup.
examples.chinese: 警方认为这些持枪歹徒是开着一辆白色的福特皮卡逃走的。

examples.english: Follow that car and don't let it get away from you.
examples.chinese: 跟上那辆车，别让它跑了。

examples.english: Detectives followed the man as far as the harbour, but then he jumped into a speedboat and got clean away.
examples.chinese: 侦探们跟踪那个男子到了港口，而他却突然跳上一艘快艇，逃得无影无踪。

examples.english: Watch him very carefully - he might try and give us the slip.
examples.chinese: 要密切注意他—他可能想甩掉我们。
