examples.english: All exhibits are listed in the catalogue.
examples.chinese: 所有展品都列在目录上。

examples.english: The Supermac Computer was on display at the Umax booth.
examples.chinese: 超级苹果电脑在力捷展区展出。

examples.english: As expected, there were dozens of cellular phones on display.
examples.chinese: 一如所料，陈列着许许多多手机。

examples.english: Several famous paintings by Leonardo da Vinci are on show at the National Gallery.
examples.chinese: 莱奥纳多·达·芬奇的几幅名画在国家美术馆里展出。

examples.english: Schiele's watercolours go on show here for two months, starting August 24.
examples.chinese: 席勒的水彩画从8月24日起在这里展出两个月。

examples.english: GM's solar-powered car was unveiled at last month's Geneva auto show.
examples.chinese: 通用汽车公司的太阳能汽车在上月的日内瓦车展上首次展出。

examples.english: The government has unveiled its plans for the future of Britain's armed forces.
examples.chinese: 政府首次公开英国武装力量的未来计划。

centers.english: when paintings, art, products etc are shown publicly
centers.chinese: 画作、艺术品、产品等的公开展出

examples.english: All the photographs in the exhibition are for sale.
examples.chinese: 展览会上的所有照片都供出售。

examples.english: Milan is hosting an international trade exhibition this month.
examples.chinese: 米兰本月将主办一个国际性的交易展览会。

examples.english: The exhibit, entitled "Search and Destroy', proved to be very popular.
examples.chinese: 这次展览主题为“探索与毁灭”非常受欢迎。

examples.english: Davis is organizing an exhibition of paintings by contemporary black artists.
examples.chinese: 戴维斯正在筹办一个当代黑人画家作品展。

examples.english: They held a spectacular firework display to mark the new millennium.
examples.chinese: 他们举办了一个壮观的烟花表演庆祝新千年。

examples.english: The festival of Lucia is a dazzling display of the art, music and dance of an ancient culture.
examples.chinese: 露西娅艺术节内容丰富，令人眼花缭乱，展示了一个古代文化的艺术、音乐和舞蹈等各种项目。

examples.english: a display of Shona sculpture from Zimbabwe
examples.chinese: 从津巴布韦来的绍纳人雕塑展品

centers.english: to show someone a house, building, or place
centers.chinese: 带领某人看房子、大楼或某个地方

examples.english: Let me show you the garden.
examples.chinese: 我带你看看花园吧。

examples.english: It's worth asking a local guide to show you the sights.
examples.chinese: 请一个当地的导游带你游览景点是值得的。

examples.english: The real estate agent showed them house after house, but they couldn't find one they liked.
examples.chinese: 房地产经纪人带他们看了一处又一处的房子，可他们就是找不到自己喜欢的。

expressions.text: show somebody around/ show somebody round

examples.english: She'd never been to Oxford before, so I offered to show her round.
examples.chinese: 她以前从没到过牛津，所以我提出带她去逛逛。

examples.english: Later, he showed Margaret all around his new house.
examples.chinese: 后来，他带玛格丽特到他的新房子里四处看看。

examples.english: In the afternoon, we were shown around the Kennedy Space Center.
examples.chinese: 下午，有人带我们参观了肯尼迪宇航中心。

examples.english: The visitors were conducted around the factory by senior managers.
examples.chinese: 来宾在高级经理的带领下参观了工厂。

examples.english: An officer was sent to conduct the journalists around the shattered building.
examples.chinese: 一位官员被派去带领记者看了倒塌的大楼。

examples.english: We went on a conducted tour of the castle.
examples.chinese: 我们在导游的带领下参观了城堡。

examples.english: Gregory took us on a riverboat tour down the Volga.
examples.chinese: 格雷戈里带我们乘内河船游览了伏尔加河。

examples.english: Officials were taken on a tour of the one-time maximum security prison.
examples.chinese: 有人带领官员参观了这座曾经戒备最森严的监狱。

centers.english: to show someone something because you are proud of it

examples.english: She raised her wrist, showing off a sparkling diamond bracelet.
examples.chinese: 她抬起手腕，亮出一条亮闪闪的钻石手链。

examples.english: At last, this was a chance to show off her talents before a real audience.
examples.chinese: 终于机会来了，她可以在真正的观众面前展示自己的才华了。

examples.english: He wanted to show his daughter off to everybody.
examples.chinese: 他想向所有人夸耀他的女儿。

examples.english: Others have called him arrogant, for flaunting his millionaire lifestyle.
examples.chinese: 他喜欢摆阔，别人都说他自负。

examples.english: He's very rich, but he doesn't like to flaunt his wealth or waste his money.
examples.chinese: 他非常有钱，但不喜欢炫耀财富，也不挥霍。

examples.english: If you've got it, flaunt it!
examples.chinese: 你有的话展示给大家看呀！

examples.english: She came in excitedly, flourishing a letter with her exam results.
examples.chinese: 她兴奋地走进来，手里挥舞着她的考试成绩通知书。

examples.english: The painting showed two gates guarded by imposing military figures flourishing swords.
examples.chinese: 画上有两扇大门，由手挥宝剑的威武的军人把守。

examples.english: The captured soldiers were paraded through the streets of the city.
examples.chinese: 被俘的士兵在城里游街示众。

examples.english: The senator loves parading his beautiful new wife before the nation.
examples.chinese: 参议员喜欢在国人面前炫耀他那位美丽的新太太。

centers.english: when something shows that something else is true
centers.chinese: 某事物表明另一事物是真的

examples.english: Her nervousness is shown by a tendency to laugh a lot in public.
examples.chinese: 她在众人面前经常发笑，这说明她紧张。

examples.english: It cost $5000 to repair the car -- that shows how bad the damage was!
examples.chinese: 修这车花了5,000美元，可见损毁有多严重！！

examples.english: It shows you what they think of their customers, if they can't even be bothered to answer your letter!
examples.chinese: 如果他们甚至连信都不愿意给你回，这就说明顾客在他们心中是什么地位！

examples.english: Caffeine has been shown to have a good effect on mental performance.
examples.chinese: 咖啡因被证明有助于大脑的功能。

examples.english: The evidence shows that this area was probably the site of a Roman settlement.
examples.chinese: 有证据显示，这个地区可能是一个古罗马村落的遗址。

examples.english: The polls clearly show that the voters are dissatisfied with the present government.
examples.chinese: 民意调查清楚地表明选民对现政府不满。

examples.english: It just goes to show that we can all learn from experience, whether we're 8 or 80.
examples.chinese: 这件事恰好证明，我们大家，不管是8岁还是80岁，都可以从经验中学习。

examples.english: The public opposition to the airport is a sign of how much people's attitudes have changed.
examples.chinese: 民众反对建机场，这是人们态度转变的迹象。

examples.english: A cluttered desk is a sign of a creative mind.
examples.chinese: 桌子凌乱是创造力的表现。

examples.english: When a dog wags his tail, it's a sign that he's happy.
examples.chinese: 狗摇尾巴表明它是高兴的。

examples.english: My mother pursed her lips, a sure sign of displeasure.
examples.chinese: 我母亲撅起嘴，明显是不高兴的表示。

expressions.text: take/view/see something as a sign that

examples.english: When she didn't return my calls, I took it as a sign that she just wasn't interested.
examples.chinese: 她没有回电话，我当她是不感兴趣了。

examples.english: His commitment to the company is demonstrated by his work on the project.
examples.chinese: 他对公司的忠诚，从他为这个项目所做的工作中得到了证明。

examples.english: Here are some examples that demonstrate how badly some students write their resumés.
examples.chinese: 这里有几个例子说明有些学生的简历写得多么不好。

examples.english: The assessment center gives each applicant the opportunity to demonstrate whether they are suited to the work.
examples.chinese: 评估中心给每一位应聘者一个机会证明自己是否合适做这个工作。

examples.english: I'm afraid this whole episode demonstrates that we have become less compassionate as a society.
examples.chinese: 我认为，这整个事件表明我们这个社会同情心减弱了。

examples.english: You've got to be able to demonstrate to people that the union can help in these cases.
examples.chinese: 你得向人们证明这些事情工会可以提供帮助。

expressions.gram: transitive verb not in progressive or passive

examples.english: Cloudy water from the taps usually means problems with your storage tank.
examples.chinese: 自来水浑浊一般表示水箱有问题。

examples.english: A free economy does not mean the absence of any economic control.
examples.chinese: 自由经济并不是说没有任何经济调控。

examples.english: This sort of behaviour means that the child is definitely unhappy.
examples.chinese: 这种行为表明孩子肯定是不快乐的。

examples.english: If A is false, does that also mean proposition B is false?
examples.chinese: 如果A是错误的，那么是否就意味着命题B也是错误的呢?

examples.english: Her car's not there, so that must mean she's gone to pick him up.
examples.chinese: 她的车不在那里，那表示她肯定是去接他了。

examples.english: This new evidence makes it clear that Rourke was acting independently of the others.
examples.chinese: 这个新的证据清楚地表明，鲁尔克是独立于他人而行动的。

examples.english: The look in her eyes made it clear what she meant.
examples.chinese: 她的眼神清楚地表明了她的意思。

examples.english: The Unabomber's diary makes clear his loathing and contempt for society.
examples.chinese: “隐形炸弹人”的日记明确表示了他对社会的痛恶和鄙视。

examples.english: The three-year guarantee reflects the company's confidence in the quality of its products.
examples.chinese: 三年的保修期反映了该公司对自己产品质量的信心。

examples.english: His fair, freckled skin and blue eyes reflect his Irish heritage.
examples.chinese: 他那白皙、长着雀斑的皮肤和蓝眼睛显示出了他的爱尔兰血统。

examples.english: This poll reflects what the Republicans of California are sensing.
examples.chinese: 这次民意调查反映出加州的共和党意识到了什么。

examples.english: The culture of a nation is always reflected in its language.
examples.chinese: 一个民族的文化往往反映在它的语言里。

examples.english: As this story illustrates, some stars have become as bored as audiences by Hollywood extravagance.
examples.chinese: 这个故事说明，有些明星和观众一样厌倦了好莱坞的奢华。

examples.english: This point can be illustrated by two brief examples.
examples.chinese: 这一点可以用两个简单的例子来说明。

examples.english: The experiment illustrates how careful you have to be when interpreting results.
examples.chinese: 这个实验说明，分析结果的时候要多么小心。

expressions.gram: transitive verb not in progressive or passive

examples.english: What does this tell us about the experience of young immigrants to this country?
examples.chinese: 对于年轻移民来这个国家的经历，这个故事告诉了我们什么？

examples.english: Unfortunately, the photographs can tell us very little about the potential for life on Mars.
examples.chinese: 遗憾的是，关于火星上是否有生命存在，这些照片能告诉我们的非常少。

examples.english: The research told us that many drug addicts were returning to heroin up to three years after the treatment.
examples.chinese: 调查告诉我们，许多有毒瘾的人接受治疗以后，最多过三年就会再次复吸海洛因。

examples.english: Changes in sexual laws in recent years are evidence of a gradual movement towards greater tolerance.
examples.chinese: 近年来涉及性方面的法律的修改，证明人们的态度逐渐变得更加宽容。

examples.english: Journalists argue that being attacked by both sides is evidence that their coverage is fair.
examples.chinese: 新闻记者争辩说，双方都来攻击他们就说明他们的报道是公正的。

examples.english: This is clear evidence that the crime figures do not represent the true situation.
examples.chinese: 这明确表明，犯罪数字并不能反映真实的情况。

examples.english: The way he spoke in the bar afterwards revealed prejudice and bitterness that I had never suspected.
examples.chinese: 后来他在酒吧里说的那些话道出了他的偏见和怨愤，这是我以前未曾怀疑过的。

examples.english: Separate holidays and weekends apart reveal more clearly than any words the state of their marriage.
examples.chinese: 假期和周末都分开各自过，这毋庸多言就清楚地揭示了他们的婚姻状况。

examples.english: The President's refusal to meet the press reveals just how serious the crisis is.
examples.chinese: 总统拒绝与媒体见面，这正揭示了危机的严重性。

examples.english: Positive tests have revealed why some athletes were so reluctant to co-operate.
examples.chinese: 检验结果呈阳性，这就揭露了为什么有些运动员那么不愿意合作。

examples.english: The fact that there are no black officers in the entire regiment reveals that the army is not serious about its anti-discrimination policies.
examples.chinese: 整个军团里没有一个黑人军官，这个事实揭示出军中的反歧视政策并不是认真的。

examples.english: a very revealing statement
examples.chinese: 一个使真相大白的声明

centers.english: when something shows that something else is likely to be true
centers.chinese: 某事物表明另一事物有可能是真实的

examples.english: In fact, the situation is far worse than these figures suggest.
examples.chinese: 事实上，情况远比这些数据所反映的要来得严重。

examples.english: There was nothing in his letter to suggest that he might have been unhappy or depressed.
examples.chinese: 他的信中没有片言只字显示他当时是不开心或沮丧的。

examples.english: The drop in trading suggests the Asian economies may be headed for recession.
examples.chinese: 贸易下滑显示亚洲的经济可能正在走向衰退。

examples.english: A child's behavior might suggest to others that there are problems at home.
examples.chinese: 孩子的行为有可能向别人暗示家里出现了问题。

examples.english: The door had not been forced open, which strongly suggests that the victim was known to her killer.
examples.chinese: 门不是强行破开的，这清楚说明被害人是认识凶手的。

examples.english: How badly reform is needed was indicated recently by the rising youth crime figures.
examples.chinese: 最近青年人犯罪数字在提高，这说明了改革的迫切需要。

examples.english: Research indicates that the drug can be harmful to pregnant women.
examples.chinese: 研究显示，这种药物可对孕妇造成伤害。

examples.english: Campaign finance records indicate many of the guests were donors to his own party.
examples.chinese: 竞选活动的财务记录表明，许多来宾都是他这个党派的捐助人。

expressions.gram: verb phrase not usually in progressive

examples.english: She wasn't stupid, though at times she gave that impression.
examples.chinese: 她并不笨，只是有时候会给人这种印象。

examples.english: The luxurious offices gave the impression that the company had plenty of money to spare.
examples.chinese: 豪华的办公室给人一种这样的印象—这家公司有的是钱。

examples.english: All this splendour and ceremony may at first give the impression of high culture and sophistication.
examples.chinese: 这种排场和礼数起初可能给人一种文化修养深、不同凡俗的印象。

examples.english: He gave me the impression that he didn't really rate my work.
examples.chinese: 他给我的印象是，他对我的工作并没有很好的评价。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in progressive or passive

examples.english: All the evidence points to a fatty diet being the main cause of heart disease in the West.
examples.chinese: 所有证据都表明高脂肪的饮食是西方国家引发心脏病的主要原因。

examples.english: The poor economic climate and the attitude of leading executives both point to a grim future.
examples.chinese: 不利的经济气候，以及主要管理人员的态度，都表明未来形势严峻。

examples.english: Among the ruins there are inscriptions, implying some degree of literacy even in the 9th century BC.
examples.chinese: 废墟中有碑文，这意味着早在公元前9世纪的时候就已经有了一定程度的文化水平。

examples.english: As the examples imply, some markets are local while others are national or international in scope.
examples.chinese: 这些例子表明，从范围来看，有些市场是地方性的，有些则是全国性乃至国际性的。

examples.english: The results imply that the disease originated in West Africa.
examples.chinese: 结果显示这种疾病最早可能源自西非。

examples.english: According to the prosecution, DNA tests ‘irrefutably’ implicate Henson.
examples.chinese: 根据控方所说，脱氧核糖核酸检测“无可辩驳”地表明亨森与事件有关。

examples.english: Sexually transmitted diseases have long been implicated in infertility.
examples.chinese: 性传播疾病很早就被认为是不育的一个原因。

examples.english: Seafood is increasingly implicated as the source of the hepatitis A virus.
examples.chinese: 海产食品被越来越多地认为是甲型肝炎病毒的元凶。

examples.english: Simon knew he couldn't possibly provide a blood sample without implicating himself.
examples.chinese: 西蒙知道，如果提供了血样，就不可能不把自己牵涉进去。

examples.english: Persistent abdominal pain may be indicative of appendicitis.
examples.chinese: 肚子持续疼痛可能是阑尾炎的征兆。

examples.english: The absence of any famous female composers is more indicative of male dominance than male genius.
examples.chinese: 没有著名的女作曲家，这反映出的是男人的主导地位，而不是男人的天赋。

centers.english: to show that someone or something is good

examples.english: His performance shows great talent.
examples.chinese: 他的表演显示出他极高的天赋。

examples.english: Lieutenant Marche's actions showed great courage and leadership.
examples.chinese: 马奇中尉的行为显出他极大的勇气和领导才能。

examples.english: This latest album certainly shows why Pine is rated a first-class jazz musician among his peers.
examples.chinese: 这张最新的专辑绝对能说明派因为什么能超越同辈，被誉为一流的爵士音乐家。

expressions.text: say a lot for/about/say a great deal for/about

examples.english: The decision says a great deal for Chang's courage and convictions.
examples.chinese: 这个决定充分说明了常的勇气和信心。

examples.english: To have those sort of setbacks and still keep everything together says a lot about Richard.
examples.chinese: 遭遇那样的挫折还能坚强不屈，这充分说明了理查德的性格。

examples.english: It says a lot for Banks that he decided to finish the race even though he had no hope of winning.
examples.chinese: 班克斯虽然获胜无望，但还是决定完成赛事，这足以说明他的毅力。

examples.english: It was a tribute to her teaching methods that most of the children passed the tests.
examples.chinese: 大部分孩子都通过了这些考试，这就说明她的教学方法很优秀。

examples.english: That the book was ever finished was a tribute to the patience and dedication of all concerned.
examples.chinese: 本书得以完成体现了所有相关人员的耐心和奉献精神。

expressions.gram: verb phrase not usually in progressive

examples.english: Military success always reflects well on the government in power at the time.
examples.chinese: 军事上的成就总是充分反映了当权政府的能力。

examples.english: This win reflects well on the growing strength of our young players.
examples.chinese: 这次获胜充分体现了我们年轻球员的力量在不断壮大。

examples.english: The television coverage of the trial reflected well on NBC.
examples.chinese: 电视上对这个案件审理的报道充分体现了美国全国广播公司的能力。

examples.english: The incredible precision of the equipment is testament to the mechanical skill of the engineers who built it.
examples.chinese: 这个设备令人难以置信的精确度显示了制造这个设备的工程师的技术水平。

examples.english: It is a testament to the greatness of Rodgers & Hart that their music still sounds as fresh and vital as the day it was written.
examples.chinese: “罗杰斯和哈特”组合的音乐现在听来依然清新、充满活力，一如当初，这充分证明了他们的伟大。

centers.english: to show that someone or something is bad

examples.english: His approach to the problem showed a complete lack of understanding.
examples.chinese: 他所用的方法表明他完全没有理解这一问题。

examples.english: This just shows how the standard of reporting has declined over the past ten years.
examples.chinese: 这正说明了过去十年里新闻报道的水准在下降。

examples.english: Maddie's remarks showed why she is disliked and feared by her fellow workers.
examples.chinese: 马迪的话说明了她的同事为什么不喜欢她、怕她。

expressions.text: not say much for/not say a lot for somebody/something

examples.english: Only a quarter of the class passed the exam, which doesn't say much for the quality of the teaching.
examples.chinese: 班上只有1/4的学生通过这次考试，这说明教学质量有问题。

examples.english: It doesn't say much for the media that they are criticizing something they haven't even seen yet.
examples.chinese: 媒体对于某件事情甚至没有亲自去了解就横加批评，这说明了很大的问题。

examples.english: ‘It's no reflection on you,’ Fred explained kindly. ‘My mum's always like that with my girlfriends.’
examples.chinese: “这并不说明是你不好，”弗雷德和气地解释道。“我妈妈对待我的女朋友总是这样。”

examples.english: When children are criticized, mothers often see it as a reflection on themselves.
examples.chinese: 孩子受到批评时，做母亲的常常会觉得那是自己不对。

expressions.text: reflect badly on/not reflect well on somebody/something
expressions.gram: verb phrase not usually in progressive

examples.english: The way that the refugees have been treated reflects very badly on the government.
examples.chinese: 难民们所受到的待遇有损政府的形象。

examples.english: Both women knew that squabbling in public would reflect badly on both of them.
examples.chinese: 两个女人都知道，当众争执会使两人都丢脸。

examples.english: The increasing focus on sex and violence in most movies is a comment on the changing tastes of the movie-going public.
examples.chinese: 多数电影里都越来越着力地渲染性和暴力的场面，这反映了电影观众口味的变化。

examples.english: The food queues were a sad comment on the quality of life in the new republic.
examples.chinese: 排队购买食物可悲地反映出新共和国的生活质量。

examples.english: To suggest that people are only honest when it can benefit them, is a damning comment on the human character.
examples.chinese: 说人只是在有利可图的时候才会诚实，这是人性可悲的写照。

expressions.text: be symptomatic of/be a symptom of

examples.english: The whole episode was symptomatic of the US determination to avoid another Cuba.
examples.chinese: 整件事表现了美国避免出现另一个古巴的决心。

examples.english: Noisy classrooms are a symptom of a breakdown in authority.
examples.chinese: 课堂喧哗吵闹是教师丧失威信的一个征兆。

examples.english: The movie is neither an indictment nor an endorsement of capital punishment.
examples.chinese: 这部电影对死刑既没有提出批评，也不表示赞同。

expressions.text: be a glaring/sad/serious etc indictment of something

examples.english: Numbers of casualties among refugees represent an appalling indictment of Western policy.
examples.chinese: 难民中伤亡人数之多是对西方政策的有力控诉。

examples.english: It is a serious indictment of a medical profession so arrogant that it dismisses out of hand any ‘alternative’ forms of therapy.
examples.chinese: 这是对狂妄自大的医学界的严正指控，他们把所有的“替代性”治疗方法都拒之于门外。

examples.english: If that man gets released, it will make a mockery of our legal system.
examples.chinese: 如果那人获释，那就是对我们司法制度的嘲讽。

examples.english: Recent expenditure on defense has made a mockery of government promises to improve the lives of ordinary Russians.
examples.chinese: 最近的国防开支是对政府要改善俄罗斯民生这一承诺的嘲讽。

centers.english: to show that something is going to happen

expressions.gram: transitive verb not in progressive or passive

examples.english: Dark clouds usually mean rain.
examples.chinese: 乌云一般表示要下雨了。

examples.english: High interest rates and high inflation mean a recession is not far away.
examples.chinese: 高利率和高通货膨胀意味着经济衰退为期不远了。

examples.english: His new responsibilities at work mean Leroy will rarely see his children.
examples.chinese: 勒罗伊的新职务意味着他难得能见到自己的孩子了。

examples.english: Stein took off his glasses and rested his head on the back of the chair. I took this to mean that he wasn't going to say any more.
examples.chinese: 斯坦摘下眼镜，把头靠在椅背上。我觉得他这样子是不打算再说什么了。

expressions.gram: transitive verb not in progressive or passive

examples.english: No one thinks this could spell the closure of the firm, but things could be better.
examples.chinese: 没有人觉得这样做会使公司关门，反而可以做得更好。

examples.english: Out-of-town retail developments often spell the death of independent high street shops.
examples.chinese: 镇外的零售业开发区往往会导致大街上独立经营的商店消失。

expressions.gram: verb phrase not usually in progressive

examples.english: Gandalf's late arrival did not bode well.
examples.chinese: 甘道夫的迟到不是个好兆头。

examples.english: The drop in profits bodes ill for Japan's semiconductor industry.
examples.chinese: 利润下降对日本的半导体工业来说不是个好兆头。

examples.english: The high early viewing figures bode well for writers Lane and Harvey, who hope to achieve network success.
examples.chinese: 初期的浏览人数很多，这对莱恩和哈维两位作者来说是好兆头，他们希望在网络上取得成功。

examples.english: In February, the first storks arrive, heralding spring.
examples.chinese: 2月里第一批白鹳飞来，预告春天将临。

examples.english: What changes do the attacks herald for everyday life in the US?
examples.chinese: 这些袭击事件预示着美国人的日常生活将会发生什么样的变化?

examples.english: His prosecution perhaps heralds an end to the systematic corruption that has stained this government's reputation.
examples.chinese: 他这次被起诉也许预示着，玷污政府名声的体制腐败走到尽头了。

examples.english: Taxol has been heralded as a breakthrough in cancer treatment.
examples.chinese: 泰素被誉为是癌症治疗方面的一个突破。

centers.english: to show your thoughts or feelings

examples.english: Paul didn't show much interest in the idea.
examples.chinese: 保罗对这个想法没有表现出多大兴趣。

examples.english: I just want you to show some respect, just for once.
examples.chinese: 我只是希望你能表示一点尊重，就这一次。

examples.english: If you're pleased, you've got a funny way of showing it.
examples.chinese: 如果你开心了，就会用一种有趣的方法表现出来。

expressions.text: show how angry/upset/happy etc you are

examples.english: The hostages showed great courage in a very frightening situation.
examples.chinese: 人质在十分可怕的情况下表现出了极大的勇气。

examples.english: I was determined not to show how upset I felt.
examples.chinese: 我下定决心，不表现出自己有多不安。

examples.english: I'm displaying my ignorance here, but could you just tell us exactly what your job as a geologist involves?
examples.chinese: 恕我无知，可否确切地告诉我们作为地质学家你都干些什么呢?

examples.english: The previous government displayed a notable lack of enthusiasm for women's rights.
examples.chinese: 前届政府对妇女权利问题表现得明显缺乏热情。

examples.english: The contestants here today have displayed tremendous skill.
examples.chinese: 今天参赛者显示出了高超的技艺。

examples.english: a display of strength
examples.chinese: 力量的表现

examples.english: Ian never did like public displays of affection.
examples.chinese: 伊恩从来都不喜欢在公开场合表现得很亲热。

examples.english: Brenda wanted to demonstrate her sympathy in a practical way.
examples.chinese: 布伦达想以实际行动表示她的同情。

examples.english: The new law was intended to demonstrate the government's concern for the lowest paid workers.
examples.chinese: 这条新的法律旨在表示政府对最低工资工人的关心。

examples.english: The prisoner exhibited no emotion when the sentence was read out.
examples.chinese: 宣读判决书的时候，囚犯表现得无动于衷。

examples.english: Anyone who exhibits extreme anxiety in the face of potential danger is unlikely to become an effective military leader.
examples.chinese: 任何人如果在潜在的危险面前表现出极度的焦躁不安，都不可能成为一名卓越的军事首领。

examples.english: Caitlin watched his face, which registered a mixture of alarm and astonishment.
examples.chinese: 凯特琳注视着他的脸，那张脸流露出惊慌和诧异的交错神色。

examples.english: It was only when I mentioned the money that she registered a flicker of interest.
examples.chinese: 在我提到钱的时候，她脸上才闪现出一丝兴趣。

centers.english: to show your feelings, attitudes etc when you do not intend to
centers.chinese: 不经意地流露出感情、态度等

examples.english: Kris couldn't hide her delight at my situation.
examples.chinese: 对我的情况，克丽丝喜不自禁。！

examples.english: ‘It'll be okay,’ said Kang, unable to hide his disappointment.
examples.chinese: “没关系的。”康说道，失望之情难以掩饰。

examples.english: He couldn't hide his envy from her.
examples.chinese: 他在她面前掩饰不住自己的嫉妒。

expressions.gram: transitive verb not usually in progressive

examples.english: The look on my face must have revealed my embarrassment.
examples.chinese: 我脸上的表情肯定暴露了我的窘迫。

examples.english: Carter's face was a blank, revealing no emotion.
examples.chinese: 卡特一脸茫然，毫无表情。

examples.english: A slight trembling of his hands revealed his growing excitement.
examples.chinese: 他的双手微微颤抖，流露出越来越激动的情绪。

expressions.gram: transitive verb not in progressive or passive

examples.english: His words were calm, but his voice betrayed his very real concern and anxiety.
examples.chinese: 他的言语是平静的，但是他的声音却暴露出他确实非常担心和焦虑。

examples.english: Barker's comments on Germany betrayed a woeful ignorance of history and recent politics.
examples.chinese: 巴克评论德国的那番话暴露了他对历史和时政的极度无知。

centers.english: someone who usually shows what they feel and think
centers.chinese: 通常会把自己的感受和想法表露出来的人

examples.english: He had a very open nature.
examples.chinese: 他性格非常直率。

examples.english: My New Year resolution is to be more honest and open towards other people.
examples.chinese: 我的新年计划是待人要更加诚恳坦率。

examples.english: Julia was quite open about her disappointment.
examples.chinese: 朱莉娅很坦诚，并不掩饰她的失望。

examples.english: So I'll lose my job, just for being open about my beliefs.
examples.chinese: 所以我要失去工作了，就因为我对自己的信仰未加隐瞒。

examples.english: She spoke openly about her fears.
examples.chinese: 她坦率地说出自己的恐惧。

examples.english: You openly disobeyed your father.
examples.chinese: 你公然违抗你父亲的意愿。

examples.english: His parents were never very demonstrative towards him, so he finds it hard to show his own feelings.
examples.chinese: 他父母对他从来都不太表露感情，所以他也觉得很难表露自己的感情。

examples.english: She's not a very demonstrative person, but her friends are important to her.
examples.chinese: 她不是一个感情很外露的人，但朋友对她来说很重要。

centers.english: someone who does not usually show what they feel or think
centers.chinese: 通常都不表露自己的感受或想法的人

examples.english: Are you cautious and reserved, or adventurous and uninhibited?
examples.chinese: 你是谨慎寡言的人，还是敢作敢为、放任不羁的人?

examples.english: They are very reserved people, the English. It takes some time to get to know them.
examples.chinese: 英格兰人性格很内敛，了解他们要花点时间。

examples.english: He is described as an introverted teenager, with a love of horses.
examples.chinese: 据说他是一个性格内向的少年，很喜欢马。

examples.english: I was probably more introverted than Arthur when we were children.
examples.chinese: 小时候我可能比阿瑟更内向。

examples.english: I think everyone has a bit of the introvert in them.
examples.chinese: 我觉得每个人的性格中都有内向的一面。

examples.english: Neighbours say he was quiet, undemonstrative and always carefully dressed.
examples.chinese: 邻居说他是一个文静、感情不外露的人，总是穿得很讲究。

centers.english: to help someone or something show a particular feeling or quality
centers.chinese: 使某人表露出某种感情或某物显示某种品质

examples.english: Competitive games bring out my aggressive side.
examples.chinese: 竞技性的比赛显露了我好强的一面。

examples.english: Counsellors are specially trained to bring out people's innermost fears and emotions.
examples.chinese: 辅导人员都受过专门训练，能诱导人们将内心最深处的恐惧和情感诉说出来。

expressions.text: bring out the best/ worst in somebody

examples.english: For some reason, Christmas always seemed to bring out the worst in Dad.
examples.chinese: 由于某种原因，圣诞节好像总是暴露出爸爸最不好的一面。

centers.english: to pull something with your hands

examples.english: You need to pull this lever to start the machine.
examples.chinese: 你得拉一下这个控制杆来启动机器。

examples.english: He pulled her towards him and kissed her.
examples.chinese: 他将她拉到身旁并吻了她。

examples.english: Pull the chair nearer to the fire.
examples.chinese: 将椅子拉近火炉些。

examples.english: Everyone took hold of the rope and pulled hard.
examples.chinese: 所有人都拿起绳子用力拉。

examples.english: She gave a gentle pull on the reins, and the horse stopped.
examples.chinese: 她轻轻一拉缰绳，那匹马便停住了。

examples.english: That door sticks a bit - give it a good pull.
examples.chinese: 那扇门有点卡住——用力拉一下。

examples.english: The little girl was tugging her mother's sleeve, trying to get her attention.
examples.chinese: 那个小女孩在拉母亲的衣袖，想引起她注意。

examples.english: We tugged at the door but it still wouldn't open.
examples.chinese: 我们用力拉了一下门，但门还是没开。

examples.english: Tony gave Simon's T-shirt a tug, and they both left the room.
examples.chinese: 托尼拉了拉西蒙的T恤衫，两人离开了房间。

examples.english: She took my hand and drew me closer.
examples.chinese: 她抓起我的手，把我拉得更近些。

examples.english: He wound in the line, steadily drawing the fish towards the bank.
examples.chinese: 他把钓线收起来，慢慢地把鱼拖向岸边。

examples.english: Paula drew back the sheet and looked at the sleeping child.
examples.chinese: 葆拉拉开被单，看着熟睡的孩子。

examples.english: It was getting dark so I drew the curtains and switched on the light.
examples.chinese: 天渐渐黑了，于是我拉上窗帘，打开灯。

centers.english: to pull something suddenly

examples.english: He jerked the string and the light came on.
examples.chinese: 他蓦地拉了一下线，灯亮了。

examples.english: Isabel jerked her hand away from his and shoved it in her pocket.
examples.chinese: 伊莎贝尔将手猛地从他手中抽出来，插进自己的口袋里。

examples.english: She pulled the dog back with a sharp jerk of his leash.
examples.chinese: 她使劲拉动狗颈项上的皮带，硬是把它往后拖。

examples.english: The other girls surrounded her, calling her names and yanking her hair.
examples.chinese: 其他女孩子围住她，破口大骂并使劲拉她头发。

examples.english: Buddy yanked the drawer open, and took out the gun.
examples.chinese: 巴迪猛地拉开抽屉，拿出枪。

examples.english: His friends grabbed him and yanked him to his feet.
examples.chinese: 朋友们抓住他，把他拖起来。

examples.english: The child's mother caught him just in time, and yanked him away from the kerb.
examples.chinese: 那个孩子的母亲及时抓住他，把他从路边拖开。

examples.english: I gave the lever a yank, and the machine started up.
examples.chinese: 我猛地一拉控制杆，机器便开动起来。

centers.english: to pull something heavy

examples.english: The Queen's carriage was pulled by two white horses.
examples.chinese: 女王的马车由两匹白马拉着。

examples.english: a tractor pulling a plough
examples.chinese: 牵引着犁的拖拉机

examples.english: One of the firemen went back in and dragged my husband out through the flames.
examples.chinese: 一名消防员又冲了进去，拖着我的丈夫穿过重重烈焰。

examples.english: Pick up your chairs, children. Don't drag them along the floor.
examples.chinese: 抬起椅子，孩子们。别在地板上拖。

examples.english: Somehow we managed to haul the boat out of the water and onto the bank.
examples.chinese: 我们终于想办法把小船拖出水面弄上岸。

examples.english: At about five o'clock the beach was full of activity, with the fishermen hauling in their nets.
examples.chinese: 5点左右，渔夫拉起网来，沙滩上一阵热闹。

examples.english: What's the speed limit for cars towing trailers?
examples.chinese: 拖挂车的汽车限速多少？

examples.english: The damaged ship was towed to the nearest port.
examples.chinese: 那艘损坏的船被拖往最近的港口。

examples.english: The police had towed his car away because it was blocking the road.
examples.chinese: 因为阻塞马路，警方将他的车拖走了。

examples.english: Everyone pull together now. Are you ready? Heave!
examples.chinese: 现在大家一起拉。准备好了吗，拉！

examples.english: Rod bent down and heaved the sack onto his shoulder.
examples.chinese: 罗德弯下腰，将大口袋扛到肩上。

centers.english: to push something or someone

examples.english: We pushed as hard as we could, but we couldn't get the bus to move.
examples.chinese: 我们用尽力气推，可还是推不动那辆公共汽车。

examples.english: Mum, William pushed me!
examples.chinese: 妈，威廉推我！

examples.english: She pushed the table into a corner of the classroom.
examples.chinese: 她把桌子推到教室的角落里。

examples.english: Pushing his plate to one side he called for the waiter.
examples.chinese: 他把盘子推到一边，叫侍者过来。

examples.english: Witnesses had seen the man push Mrs Cooper off the bridge into the canal.
examples.chinese: 目击者看见那男子把库珀太太从桥上推下运河去。

examples.english: ‘Go away!’ she said, giving him a push.
examples.chinese: “走开！”她推了他一下说。

examples.english: He reached out and gave the door a gentle push.
examples.chinese: 他伸出手，轻轻推了一下门。

examples.english: The children were all pushing and shoving each other.
examples.chinese: 孩子们在相互推搡。

examples.english: One of the soldiers shoved her roughly against the wall.
examples.chinese: 其中一名士兵把她猛推到墙上。

examples.english: Tom shoved his suitcase under the bed.
examples.chinese: 汤姆把手提箱推到床底下去。

examples.english: Armed police shoved the protestors aside to make way for the president's car.
examples.chinese: 武装警察把抗议者猛推到一边，为总统的车子开道。

examples.english: If the door won't open just give it a shove.
examples.chinese: 如果门打不开，就猛推一下。

examples.english: ‘Mind your own business!’ said Graham, giving me a shove.
examples.chinese: “别管闲事！”格雷厄姆说道，猛地把我一推。

examples.english: Two policemen quickly appeared and hustled him out.
examples.chinese: 两名警察很快到来，把他推了出去。

examples.english: Martin seized her arm and hustled her away.
examples.chinese: 马丁捉住她的胳膊，把她推开。

examples.english: The two men were hustled into a police van and driven away.
examples.chinese: 两名男子被推入警车带走了。

examples.english: Her friends managed to get her out of the pub and bundled her home.
examples.chinese: 她的朋友设法把她拉出小酒馆，匆匆送她回家。

examples.english: He had been bundled into the back of a Volkswagen by three masked men.
examples.chinese: 他被三名蒙面男子迅速推入一辆大众汽车的后座。

examples.english: I collected up the dirty washing and bundled it into the washing machine.
examples.chinese: 我把脏衣服放在一起，塞进洗衣机。

examples.english: The soldiers were manhandling two men into the yard.
examples.chinese: 士兵们把两名男子硬推入院子。

examples.english: The gang manhandled the stolen trailer through a gap in the fence.
examples.chinese: 匪徒把偷来的拖车从篱笆的缺口处硬推过去。

centers.english: to push something that has wheels or rolls easily
centers.chinese: 推有轮子或容易滚动的东西

examples.english: Paul held the door open for a woman pushing a trolley of heavy books.
examples.chinese: 保罗拉着门不让它关上，让一名推着装有沉重书籍小车的妇女通过。

examples.english: Shoppers were pushing their carts around the supermarket.
examples.chinese: 顾客推着购物手推车在超级市场里转悠。

examples.english: The car had run out of gas so they pushed it into a side-street.
examples.chinese: 车子耗尽了汽油，于是他们把它推进小路。

examples.english: As I arrived she was just wheeling her bicycle out of the shed.
examples.chinese: 我到达时，她正好把自行车从车棚里推出来。

examples.english: I collected a trolley and wheeled it towards the frozen food section.
examples.chinese: 我取了一辆手推车，把它推向冷冻食品区。

examples.english: She hated being wheeled round in a wheelchair.
examples.chinese: 她讨厌坐在轮椅里被人推来推去。

examples.english: We had so much fun rolling stones down into the river.
examples.chinese: 我们把石头滚到河里，玩得很开心。

examples.english: The porters were trundling barrows loaded with vegetables into the market.
examples.chinese: 搬运工吃力地把满载蔬菜的两轮手推车推进市场。

examples.english: The soldiers trundled the massive gun carriage along the road.
examples.chinese: 士兵们费劲地推着庞大的炮车，沿着公路前进。

centers.english: to push someone or something with your finger, elbow, or with something pointed
centers.chinese: 用手指、肘部或尖的东西推某人或某物

examples.english: Toby nudged my arm. ‘That's the guy I told you about,’ he whispered.
examples.chinese: 托比轻轻碰了一下我的胳膊，小声说道：“那就是我跟你说过的家伙。”

examples.english: Christine nudged me and giggled.
examples.chinese: 克里斯蒂娜轻轻碰了我一下，咯咯地笑了起来。

examples.english: Mark gave me a nudge and indicated two men who had just walked in.
examples.chinese: 马克用肘轻轻推了推我，指向刚进来的两名男子。

examples.english: The boys poked the fish with sticks to see if it was still alive
examples.chinese: 那些男孩子用木棒戳那条鱼看它是否还活着。

examples.english: Careful with that stick! You nearly poked me in the eye.
examples.chinese: 拿着那根棍子小心点！你差点戳到我眼睛。

examples.english: I gave dad a poke to wake him up.
examples.chinese: 我捅了爸爸一下，把他叫醒。

examples.english: Sergeant Thompson raised his stick and prodded the soldier in the chest.
examples.chinese: 汤普森中士提起棍子，戳戳那士兵的胸脯。

examples.english: They walked around him, prodding and pinching him.
examples.chinese: 他们绕着他走来走去，对他又戳又掐。

examples.english: He gave the dog a quick prod with his foot.
examples.chinese: 他飞快地踢了那条狗一脚。

examples.english: Jenny dug me sharply in the ribs and told me to be quiet.
examples.chinese: 珍妮突然戳了我一下，叫我安静。

examples.english: Edward laughed loudly, digging me in the ribs, wanting me to share the joke.
examples.chinese: 爱德华大声笑着，碰了碰我，让我分享笑话。

centers.english: to push an object into an opening or into something soft
centers.chinese: 把物体推进一个开口或柔软的东西内

examples.english: They stuck pins into a map to show where the enemy's camps were.
examples.chinese: 他们把图钉按在一张地图上表示敌人营地的位置。

examples.english: The doctor had to stick a tube down my throat in order to examine my stomach.
examples.chinese: 医生必须把一根管子从我的咽喉往下插作胃部检查。

examples.english: She tried to force her feet into the shoes but they were too small.
examples.chinese: 她把双脚硬往鞋子里塞，但鞋子实在太小了。

examples.english: I finally managed to force the package through the small letterbox.
examples.chinese: 我终于把包裹塞进那个小信箱。

examples.english: She shoved two more sweaters into her bag.
examples.chinese: 她又把两件毛衣塞进包里。

examples.english: He quickly stuffed the letter down the side of the sofa.
examples.chinese: 他飞快地把那封信塞进沙发的一边。

examples.english: She rammed the papers into her briefcase.
examples.chinese: 她把文件用力塞进手提箱。

examples.english: Construction workers had to spend the night ramming iron girders into place to support the building.
examples.chinese: 建筑工人不得不花了一晚上用力把支撑大楼的铁梁安装好。

examples.english: Plunging both hands deep into the sack she rummaged among the parcels.
examples.chinese: 她把双手深深地插进盛有包裹的麻袋里胡乱翻找着。

examples.english: Then he plunged the knife into his victim's chest.
examples.chinese: 然后，他把刀猛地刺进被害者的胸膛。

examples.english: He thrust the knife deep into the animal's chest.
examples.chinese: 他把刀深深地刺进那只动物的胸口。

examples.english: Thrusting the gun back into its holster, the man grinned at the body lying on the floor.
examples.chinese: 那人把枪插入枪套，朝躺在地板上的尸体咧嘴狞笑。

examples.english: He thrust some money into my hand and told me to drive him to the airport.
examples.chinese: 他把一些钱塞入我手中，告诉我开车送他去机场。

examples.english: I don't think I can squeeze any more files into this drawer.
examples.chinese: 我觉得这只抽屉里再也塞不进文件了。

examples.english: It's no use trying to squeeze yourself into clothes that are too small for you.
examples.chinese: 太小的衣服你硬穿是穿不上的。

examples.english: Just hold the door open while I jam a wedge under it.
examples.chinese: 把门开着，让我把楔子塞在下面。

examples.english: Kelly poured himself another glass of wine and jammed the cork back into the bottle.
examples.chinese: 凯利又给自己倒了杯酒，然后把木塞塞回瓶口。

centers.english: to push people in order to move forward

examples.english: Can you tell the people at the back of the queue to stop pushing!
examples.chinese: 告诉排在队伍后面的人不要挤，好吗？

examples.english: He pushed his way through the crowd.
examples.chinese: 他从人群中挤过去。

examples.english: She pushed past me to the front of the line.
examples.chinese: 她推开我挤到队伍的前面。

examples.english: The people moved forward towards the food, pushing and shoving to get there first.
examples.chinese: 人群朝着食物的方向推推搡搡，争先恐后地往前挤。

examples.english: Peter shoved his way through the dense crowd in search of his son.
examples.chinese: 彼得在拥挤的人群中连推带挤地寻找他的儿子。

examples.english: Robert shoved past the others and made his way to the front of the room.
examples.chinese: 罗伯特挤过其他人身边，朝房间的前部走去。

examples.english: She just barged into me, without even apologizing.
examples.chinese: 她撞了我个满怀，连道歉话也不说。

examples.english: A woman carrying a large basket barged past me to the front of the line.
examples.chinese: 一个拎着大篮子的妇女从我旁边横冲直撞地挤到队伍的前面。

examples.english: He forced his way through the dense crowd.
examples.chinese: 他在拥挤的人群中强行挤出一条路来。

examples.english: Police forced their way into the flat and arrested two men.
examples.chinese: 警察破门进入公寓，逮捕了两名男子。

examples.english: Passengers were jostling each other at the news kiosk for the last remaining copies of the evening paper.
examples.chinese: 乘客们为了购得最后几份晚报，在售报亭前互相推搡。

examples.english: The children moved forward, jostling to get to the front and see the magician.
examples.chinese: 孩子们往前挤，想到前面看那位魔术师。

examples.english: She elbowed her way through the crowd.
examples.chinese: 她用肘推挤着穿过人群。

expressions.text: elbow somebody aside/elbow somebody to one side

examples.english: Craig elbowed me aside roughly.
examples.chinese: 克雷格粗鲁地用肘把我挤到旁边。

examples.english: Elbowing me to one side, he took hold of the microphone.
examples.chinese: 他用肘把我挤到一边，一把抓住麦克风。

examples.english: As the race started the crowd pressed forward towards the track.
examples.chinese: 比赛开始时，人们朝跑道的方向拥挤着。

examples.english: The security men tried to hold back crowds of reporters pressing round the President's car.
examples.chinese: 保安人员试图阻止记者围拥在总统的座车四周。

centers.english: to push something to operate a machine

examples.english: It's very simple - you put the paper in and push a button, and the computer does the rest for you.
examples.chinese: 这很简单—把纸放进去，按下按钮，余下的事就由电脑来做了。

examples.english: She pressed the button and the machine started to whirr.
examples.chinese: 她按下按钮，机器便开始呼呼转起来。

centers.english: to stick out

examples.english: The fridge door won't shut because there's something sticking out.
examples.chinese: 冰箱的门关不上，因为有东西伸了出来。

examples.english: His large ears stuck out almost at right angles.
examples.chinese: 他的一对大耳朵撑出来几乎成直角了。

examples.english: A neatly folded handkerchief was sticking out of his jacket pocket.
examples.chinese: 他的外套口袋里露出一块折得整整齐齐的手帕。

examples.english: A pair of skis stuck out through the car window.
examples.chinese: 一副滑雪板从车窗里伸出来。

examples.english: His hair was white, and stuck up in tufts on his head.
examples.chinese: 他的头发白了，一绺绺竖在头上。

examples.english: Can you see that branch that's sticking up?
examples.chinese: 你能看到伸出来的那根树枝吗?！

examples.english: He saw a hand sticking up through the snow.
examples.chinese: 他看见雪里有一只手伸出来。

examples.english: A church steeple stuck up above the roofs of the surrounding cottages.
examples.chinese: 教堂的尖塔耸立在周围小屋的屋顶之上。

examples.english: I noticed a metal pipe protruding from the wall.
examples.chinese: 我发现有一根金属管从墙上突出来。

examples.english: The largest stone can be seen protruding above the level of the river.
examples.chinese: 可以看到，最大的石头露出了河面。

examples.english: She injured herself on a screw that protruded 2 inches out of the bench.
examples.chinese: 她被长凳上凸出来两英寸长的一根螺丝钉割伤了。

examples.english: Our guide led us to where a flat rock juts out from the side of the cliff.
examples.chinese: 导游把我们带到一个地方，那里有一块平坦的岩石从悬崖边伸出去。

examples.english: a slim piece of land jutting out into the Gulf of Mexico
examples.chinese: 伸入墨西哥湾的一块狭长的陆地

examples.english: Our rafts floated downstream towards the icebergs, which jutted 30 feet out of the water.
examples.chinese: 我们的橡皮筏顺流而下，漂向浮在水面30英尺的冰山。

examples.english: I looked across the street and saw Mike's head poking out above the fence.
examples.chinese: 我向街对面望去，只见迈克从篱笆的上面探出头来。

examples.english: The first snowdrops poked out through the frozen ground.
examples.chinese: 那些早开的雪花莲露出了结冰的地面。

examples.english: His cheeks bulged, and his face turned purple with rage.
examples.chinese: 他气得鼓起了脸颊，脸色铁青。

examples.english: Father's face was flushed, and his eyes bulged out.
examples.chinese: 父亲满脸通红，双眼凸出。

examples.english: Her purse bulged with keys, cigarettes, scraps of paper, and old receipts.
examples.chinese: 她的手提包鼓鼓囊囊，有钥匙、香烟、碎纸片和旧收据。

examples.english: Two walkways projected over the gorge on both sides of the river.
examples.chinese: 峡谷上有两条凌空的栈道，分别位于河的两边。

examples.english: The pier would be 1000 metres long and project about 400 metres into the sea.
examples.chinese: 码头全长将有1,000米，有400米左右伸入海里。

centers.english: words for describing something that sticks out
centers.chinese: 描述某物突出来的词语

examples.english: His face was tanned, the cheekbones high and prominent.
examples.chinese: 他的脸晒得黑黑的，两边的颧骨高高地凸起。

examples.english: Her nose was quite prominent, and she had small, even teeth.
examples.chinese: 她的鼻子高高的，牙齿小而整齐。

examples.english: a heart-shaped face, slightly prominent teeth and small eyes
examples.chinese: 一张瓜子脸，牙齿微微突出，眼睛很小

examples.english: Dentists have developed a new device to correct protruding teeth.
examples.chinese: 牙医研制了一种新的器具矫正凸出的牙齿。

examples.english: Roy examined the letter carefully for protruding wires.
examples.chinese: 罗伊仔细地检查这封信，看有没有突起的线。

examples.english: He had bulging blue eyes, a large nose and a long chin.
examples.chinese: 他蓝色的眼睛鼓鼓的，鼻子很大，下巴很长。

examples.english: She pushed her bulging suitcase under the chair.
examples.chinese: 她推了一下放在椅子下面那个鼓鼓的行李箱。

centers.english: something that sticks out

examples.english: Her tailored suit fitted neatly, hiding the slight bulges of middle-age.
examples.chinese: 她这身订做的衣服非常合身，把人到中年微微发福的体态掩盖了起来。

examples.english: At five months pregnant, the bulge was beginning to show.
examples.chinese: 怀孕五个月的时候，鼓起的肚子就开始显出来了。

examples.english: She saw a lump under the bedclothes.
examples.chinese: 她看到床单下面有一个隆起的地方。

examples.english: He put the gun in his pocket, where it made a slight lump.
examples.chinese: 他把枪放在口袋里，使口袋微微地鼓了起来。

examples.english: He had a lump on his forehead the size of a golf ball.
examples.chinese: 他额头上有一个高尔夫球大小的肿块。

examples.english: The car rattled every time it went over a bump.
examples.chinese: 汽车每次开过路上隆起的地方就格格作响。

examples.english: A small bump had started to develop over Irene's eye.
examples.chinese: 艾琳的眼睛上面开始长出一个小小的肿块。

examples.english: I nearly stumbled over a bump in the ground.
examples.chinese: 我差点儿在地上一块高起的地方绊倒。

centers.english: to make part of your body stick out

examples.english: A woman stuck her head out of the window and told us to come upstairs.
examples.chinese: 一个女人把头探出窗外，叫我们上楼。

examples.english: He stuck his lower lip out and frowned.
examples.chinese: 他噘起嘴，皱着眉头。

examples.english: He stuck out a hand. ‘Hi, I'm Melvyn.’
examples.chinese: 他伸出手，“嗨，我叫梅尔文。”

examples.english: I stuck out my thumb and caught a ride to Tay Ninh.
examples.chinese: 我伸出大拇指，搭了一辆车去西宁。

examples.english: Dan made a face and stuck his tongue out.
examples.chinese: 丹做了个鬼脸，还伸出舌头。

examples.english: A young doctor poked his head out, and called me into the examination room.
examples.chinese: 一位年轻的医生探出头来叫我进检查室。

examples.english: He poked out his tongue and looked at it carefully in the mirror.
examples.chinese: 他伸出舌头，在镜子里仔细地看。

examples.english: He put his head out slowly and looked up the corridor.
examples.chinese: 他慢慢地探出头来看着走廊。

examples.english: He put out his hands and Officer Johnson clicked on the handcuffs.
examples.chinese: 他伸出双手，约翰逊警官给他戴上了手铐。

centers.english: to join one thing to another, especially using glue

examples.english: It took hours to stick all these photos in my album.
examples.chinese: 把这些照片都贴在相册里花了我好几个小时。

examples.english: She stuck her chewing gum on the bottom of the chair.
examples.chinese: 她把口香糖粘在椅子的底部。

examples.english: Stick this note to Chris's computer so he sees it when he gets back.
examples.chinese: 把这张纸条贴在克里斯的电脑上去，让他一回来就能看到。

examples.english: Paul stuck two pieces of paper together.
examples.chinese: 保罗把两张纸粘起来。

examples.english: The vase broke into several pieces, but I was able to stick them all back together.
examples.chinese: 花瓶碎成了好几块，不过我把它们全部粘回去了。

examples.english: The label's coming off. Can you stick it down again?
examples.chinese: 标签要掉下来了，你把它再贴一下好吗?

examples.english: Make sure you stick down the envelope properly, the contents are confidential.
examples.chinese: 一定要把信封粘牢，里面的内容是机密的。

examples.english: I tried to glue the handle back onto the cup.
examples.chinese: 我试图把茶杯的把粘回去。

examples.english: You make the model by cutting out these shapes and gluing them together.
examples.chinese: 做这个模型是把这些形状剪下来，再用胶水粘起来。

centers.english: when one thing sticks to another

examples.english: She pressed down the flap of the envelope, but it didn't stick.
examples.chinese: 她把信封的口盖往下压，但是粘不住。

examples.english: Peter was very hot, and his shirt was sticking to his back.
examples.chinese: 彼得很热，衬衫都贴背了。

examples.english: I spilled coffee on my book and some of the pages stuck together.
examples.chinese: 我把咖啡泼到了书上，有几页粘在了一起。

examples.english: The chocolates are covered with powdered sugar to keep them from sticking together.
examples.chinese: 巧克力上裹有糖粉，使它们不会粘在一起。

examples.english: The car has wide tyres which grip the road really well.
examples.chinese: 这辆汽车的轮胎很宽，抓地性能很好。

examples.english: If you're going rock-climbing, make sure you wear shoes that will give you a good grip.
examples.chinese: 去攀岩一定要穿抓地力很强的鞋子。

examples.english: Peeling paint must be scraped away so that new paint will adhere.
examples.chinese: 剥落的油漆必须铲掉，这样新的油漆才能刷得牢。

examples.english: The machine is cleaned regularly to stop dirt adhering to the working parts.
examples.chinese: 机器要定期清洗以防止灰尘粘附在运转部件上。

examples.english: Edam cheeses have waxed coatings which adhere tightly to the cheese.
examples.chinese: 埃丹干酪表面涂有一层粘得很牢的蜂蜡。

centers.english: something that will stick to something else
centers.chinese: 可以粘到其他物体上去的东西

examples.english: Wait for the glue to dry before you sit on it.
examples.chinese: 等胶干了再坐上去。

examples.english: Add flour to the mixture to prevent it from becoming sticky.
examples.chinese: 在混合物中加面粉，防止它变黏。

examples.english: She wrote the address on a sticky label and stuck it to her computer.
examples.chinese: 她把地址写在一张粘贴标签上，然后把它贴到电脑上。

examples.english: The first aid box has adhesive dressings and antiseptic cream in it.
examples.chinese: 急救箱里有胶布和抗感染药膏。

examples.english: He attached the paper to the wall with special adhesive tape.
examples.chinese: 他用一种特殊的胶带把纸贴在墙上。

examples.english: self-adhesive envelopes
examples.chinese: 自粘信封

centers.english: to throw something

examples.english: The boys were throwing and catching a frisbee on the beach.
examples.chinese: 男孩子们在海滩上玩掷接飞盘。

examples.english: John stood on the beach, throwing stones into the waves.
examples.chinese: 约翰站在海滩上朝浪里丢石头。

examples.english: She was so angry that she threw the pan straight at my head.
examples.chinese: 她气得把平底锅朝我头上扔过来。

examples.english: A couple of kids started throwing stones at my window.
examples.chinese: 几个小孩开始朝我的窗户扔石头。

examples.english: Carrie threw him a box of matches.
examples.chinese: 卡丽把一盒火柴扔给他。

examples.english: The La Scala crowd cheered and threw flowers to the 57-year-old tenor.
examples.chinese: 斯卡拉歌剧院的观众大声喝彩，并把鲜花扔向这位57岁的男高音。

expressions.text: chuck something on/out of/into etc something

examples.english: She took off her shoes and chucked them on the floor.
examples.chinese: 她脱下鞋子，把它们乱扔在地上。

examples.english: Chuck me those cigarettes, would you?
examples.chinese: 把那些香烟扔给我好吗？

examples.english: The baggage handlers just sling the cases in the back of the bus -- they don't care if anything gets broken.
examples.chinese: 行李搬运工就把箱子扔到公共汽车后部，也不管东西是否会打碎。

examples.english: He watched horrified as they slung the body over the cliff.
examples.chinese: 他惊恐地看着他们把尸体扔下悬崖。

expressions.text: toss something into/out of/down etc something

examples.english: The fire was started when a passing motorist carelessly tossed a cigarette out of his car.
examples.chinese: 一个过路的司机随手把烟扔出车外，引起了这次火灾。

examples.english: He tossed her last week's edition of the ‘Herald’.
examples.chinese: 他把上星期的《先驱报》扔给她。

examples.english: ‘Catch!’ said Sandra, tossing her bag to Andy.
examples.chinese: “接住！”桑德拉边说边把包扔给安迪。

examples.english: Local kids keep lobbing empty beer cans over our fence.
examples.chinese: 当地的小孩子不停地把空啤酒罐扔过我们的篱笆。

examples.english: Someone lobbed a book at me, and it hit me in the face.
examples.chinese: 有人向我抛来一本书，打在了我的脸上。

examples.english: Tod pitched his coat onto the sofa and ran toward the kitchen.
examples.chinese: 托德把外衣扔在沙发上，向厨房跑去。

examples.english: She pitched the ball to the little boy.
examples.chinese: 她把球扔给小男孩。

examples.english: Some demonstrators began hurling bricks at the police.
examples.chinese: 一些示威者开始向警察扔砖头。

expressions.text: hurl something into/out of/across etc something

examples.english: He picked up the chair and hurled it across the room.
examples.chinese: 他抓起椅子朝房间另一头扔去。

expressions.text: fling something out of/down/into etc something

examples.english: He pulled the knife from her hand and flung it out of the window.
examples.chinese: 他从她手中夺过刀子，把它扔出了窗外。

examples.english: When he gave her the tickets she ripped them up and flung them at him.
examples.chinese: 他把票给她时，她把它们撕碎并用力扔向他。

examples.english: We flung him the safety rope.
examples.chinese: 我们用力把安全绳扔给他。

centers.english: to throw a ball in a game

examples.english: He threw the ball so hard it went over their heads.
examples.chinese: 他扔得太用力了，球从他们的头顶上飞了过去。

examples.english: Julie threw the basketball straight into the net.
examples.chinese: 朱莉径直把篮球投进了篮网。

examples.english: Cromartie ran after the ball and threw it back to the pitcher in one smooth movement.
examples.chinese: 克罗马蒂追到球后把它抛回给投手，整个动作平稳流畅。

examples.english: That was a very long throw -- at least 80 yards.
examples.chinese: 那一投距离很远—至少有80码。

examples.english: You should have passed and let Joe take the shot.
examples.chinese: 你应该把球传给乔让他来射门。

examples.english: Johnson passes to White, White passes to Eliot, and Eliot scores!
examples.chinese: 约翰逊把球传给怀特，怀特传给埃利奥特，埃利奥特得分了！

examples.english: The quarterback passed the ball to Olson, who ran in for a touchdown.
examples.chinese: 四分卫把球传给奥尔森，奥尔森跑到底线触地得分。

examples.english: Johnny learned to pitch by aiming at a target his Dad had painted on the side of the garage.
examples.chinese: 约翰尼对着他父亲在车库的一面墙上画的一个靶子来练习投球。

examples.english: Ryan pitched a curve ball which easily beat the batter.
examples.chinese: 瑞安投出一个弧线球，轻易就骗过了击球手。

examples.english: The pitch went wide.
examples.chinese: 这个球投偏了。

examples.english: The batsman straightened up as Warne came in to bowl.
examples.chinese: 沃恩过来投球时，击球手直起了身子。

examples.english: He's a very aggressive bowler -- he always bowls the ball straight at the batsman's body.
examples.chinese: 他是个很有攻击性的投球手一他总把球径直往击球手的身上投去。

centers.english: to throw something small and light with your fingers
centers.chinese: 用手指扔小而轻的东西

examples.english: Mum tried to toss the pancake but unfortunately it missed the pan and hit the floor.
examples.chinese: 母亲想抛起薄煎饼，可惜饼没掉进平底锅里却落在地上。

examples.english: Josh took a mint and flipped it into his mouth like a dime.
examples.chinese: 乔希拿了一颗薄荷糖，像扔一角硬币一样把它扔进嘴里。

examples.english: We couldn't decide which movie to go to, so in the end we just flipped a coin.
examples.chinese: 我们决定不了去看哪部电影，所以到最后我们就抛硬币来决定。

examples.english: He paused and flicked a mosquito off his arm.
examples.chinese: 他停下来，把一只蚊子从胳膊上弹走。

examples.english: Stop flicking water at me!
examples.chinese: 别再把水往我身上弹了！

centers.english: to attack someone by throwing things at them
centers.chinese: 向某人扔东西来攻击他们

examples.english: The boys sat in the back of the class, pelting each other with pieces of rolled up paper.
examples.chinese: 男孩子们坐在教室后面互相扔纸团。

examples.english: When the Vice-president toured the area in 1958 he was pelted with rotten eggs by angry farmers.
examples.chinese: 1958年副总统到这地区来参观时，愤怒的农民向他投掷臭鸡蛋。

examples.english: Demonstrators were pelting rocks and bottles at police.
examples.chinese: 示威者向警察扔石块和瓶子。

examples.english: During the riot the mob started stoning the British embassy.
examples.chinese: 暴乱中，一群暴徒开始向英国大使馆扔石块。

examples.english: The thieves were caught and sentenced to be stoned to death.
examples.chinese: 小偷被抓住了，被判乱石砸死。

examples.english: My brothers bombarded me with snowballs as soon as I stepped out of the house.
examples.chinese: 我一走出房子，我的兄弟们就不停向我扔雪球。

examples.english: When the police tried to advance they were bombarded with petrol bombs.
examples.chinese: 警察试图前进时受到了汽油弹的攻击。

centers.english: when a sudden force throws someone or something through the air
centers.chinese: 一股突如其来的冲力将某人或某物抛到空中

expressions.text: throw somebody/something into/off/out of etc something

examples.english: The blast from the explosion threw debris high up into the air.
examples.chinese: 爆炸产生的气浪把碎片炸到半空中。

examples.english: I was cycling home when I got hit by a car and thrown off my bike.
examples.chinese: 我骑自行车回家时被一辆汽车撞了，从车上摔了出去。

examples.english: A small plane was lifted up and thrown across the tarmac by a freak gust of wind.
examples.chinese: 一架小型飞机被一阵怪风吹起摔到柏油碎石跑道的另一边。

examples.english: He swung round suddenly, sending the papers on his desk flying.
examples.chinese: 他突然转过身来，把他桌上的文件带得飞散。

examples.english: Her foot caught on something on the ground. Whatever it was, it sent her flying.
examples.chinese: 她的脚绊到了地上什么东西。不管那是什么，反正这一绊使她人仰马翻。

expressions.text: catapult somebody/something into/over/out of etc something

examples.english: The car crashed into a tree and the driver was catapulted through the windshield.
examples.chinese: 汽车撞到了树上，司机被抛出了挡风玻璃。

examples.english: I felt myself being propelled into the air by the force of the explosion.
examples.chinese: 我感觉自己被爆炸的冲力抛向空中。

expressions.text: pitch somebody into/over/out of etc something

examples.english: A sudden gust of wind pitched him off the ledge and he was left hanging by his safety rope.
examples.chinese: 一阵突如其来的强风把他抛离了岩架，然后他被自己的安全绳悬在半空。

examples.english: Two of the crew were pitched overboard when a big wave hit their ship.
examples.chinese: 一个大浪拍到他们的船，有两个船员被抛出船外。

centers.english: to throw something away

examples.english: I've thrown his photograph away and I never want to see his face again.
examples.chinese: 我扔掉了他的照片，再也不想看到他的脸。

examples.english: It's a nice dress. It would be a shame to chuck it away.
examples.chinese: 那件衣服很不错，扔掉太可惜了。

examples.english: To avoid the risk of infection, needles must be used once and then thrown away immediately.
examples.chinese: 为了避免感染，针头用过一次后必须立即扔掉。

examples.english: Some employers throw away all incorrectly hand-written applications without even reading them.
examples.chinese: 对于书写有误的求职信，一些雇主甚至看也不看就扔掉了。

examples.english: In the bin there were a few scraps of food his mum had chucked away.
examples.chinese: 垃圾桶内有一些他母亲倒掉的残羹剩饭。

examples.english: You haven't thrown those magazines out have you?
examples.chinese: 你还没有扔掉那些杂志，是吗?

examples.english: We chucked a lot of stuff out when we moved house.
examples.chinese: 我们搬家时扔掉了许多东西。

examples.english: She would do nothing for days then spring into action and spend a day throwing out all the garbage, cleaning the bathroom, and dusting.
examples.chinese: 她会一连好几天什么都不做，然后突然行动起来，花一整天时间扔掉所有的垃圾，清洁浴室，抹去灰尘。

examples.english: Why don't you toss out all those old books. You'll never want to read them again.
examples.chinese: 你再也不会读这些旧书了，干吗不把它们都清理掉。

examples.english: I think it's time we got rid of all these old toys.
examples.chinese: 我想现在是我们扔掉所有这些旧玩具的时候了。

examples.english: If you hate that furniture so much why don't you get rid of it and buy some new stuff.
examples.chinese: 如果你这么讨厌那件家具，为什么不把它扔掉然后买件新的。

expressions.text: get rid of something for somebody

examples.english: Give me the empty bottles -- I'll get rid of them for you.
examples.chinese: 把那些空瓶子给我—我帮你把它们处理掉。

examples.english: If you don't want any of these shoes, I'm going to toss them.
examples.chinese: 如果你不要这些鞋子的话，我就把它们都扔了。

examples.english: ‘What should I do with all your old text books?’ ‘Just chuck them out - I'm never going to need them.’
examples.chinese: “我该怎么处理你那些旧课本？”“都扔了吧，我不会再用它们了。”

examples.english: Please dispose of this wrapper carefully.
examples.chinese: 请小心地把这包装纸处理掉。

examples.english: Nuclear waste can cause serious damage to the environment if not disposed of properly.
examples.chinese: 如果核废料得不到妥善处理就会对环境造成严重破坏。

examples.english: This is an incinerator for the disposal of hospital waste, such as used syringes.
examples.chinese: 这是焚化炉，用来销毁医院里的废弃物，如用过的注射器等。

examples.english: People who discard their litter in the streets should have to pay heavy fines.
examples.chinese: 在街上乱丢废物的人应该付高额罚款。

examples.english: A child had become trapped in a refrigerator discarded in a vacant lot.
examples.chinese: 一名小孩被困在一台弃置在空地上的电冰箱里。

examples.english: The police believe that the fire was started by a discarded cigarette.
examples.chinese: 警方认为火灾是由一根乱丢的香烟引起的。

examples.english: People who want to get rid of old cars sometimes dump them in the woods.
examples.chinese: 想扔掉旧汽车的人有时会把旧车丢弃在树林里。

examples.english: Toxic waste is being dumped into the ocean.
examples.chinese: 有毒废弃物不断被倒进海里。

examples.english: The half-burned bodies were dumped in mass graves.
examples.chinese: 烧得半焦的尸体被扔进了万人坑。

centers.english: designed to be used once and then thrown away
centers.chinese: 设计成使用一次后就丢弃的

examples.english: Didn't you know you can buy disposable contact lenses now?
examples.chinese: 你不知道现在可以买到日抛型隐形眼镜了吗？

examples.english: a disposable toothbrush
examples.chinese: 用后即弃的牙刷

examples.english: disposable nappies
examples.chinese: 用后即弃的尿布

centers.english: to close a door, window, gate etc

examples.english: Come in and shut the door behind you.
examples.chinese: 进来，把门关上。

examples.english: Someone had shut the gate to stop the sheep getting out onto the road.
examples.chinese: 有人已把大门关上，防止羊跑到路上去。

examples.english: She heard Charlotte downstairs shutting the windows, and locking up for the night.
examples.chinese: 她听到夏洛特在楼下关窗锁门准备睡觉。

examples.english: Do you mind if I close the window?
examples.chinese: 我把窗户关上行吗?

examples.english: She took the necklace out of the box and closed the lid.
examples.chinese: 她从盒子里取出项链，然后盖上盖子。

examples.english: Jane marched out of the room slamming the door behind her.
examples.chinese: 简大步走出房间，把门砰地关上。

examples.english: He slammed the door so hard that the glass cracked.
examples.chinese: 他重重地摔上门，玻璃都震碎了。

examples.english: The woman pushed the door shut with her foot.
examples.chinese: 那女人用脚把门带上。

examples.english: It started raining, so I quickly pulled the window shut.
examples.chinese: 开始下雨了，所以我匆匆把窗拉上。

examples.english: ‘Sorry, we're closed,’ said the official, sliding the wooden panel shut.
examples.chinese: “对不起，我们关门了。”那位官员边说边拉上木窗格。

examples.english: ‘Tell me what's wrong,’ I said, pulling the door to.
examples.chinese: “告诉我怎么了。”我边说边把门拉上。

examples.english: She pushed the door to against the blinding sunlight.
examples.chinese: 她把门推上，挡住刺眼的阳光。

examples.english: Let's draw the curtains. We don't want people looking in.
examples.chinese: 把窗帘拉上吧，我们不想外面的人看进来。

examples.english: The curtains were closed and the room was in darkness.
examples.chinese: 窗帘是拉着的，房间里一片漆黑。

centers.english: to close a container

examples.english: As the teacher appeared, Matt shut the box quickly.
examples.chinese: 老师来了，马特急忙把盒子盖上。

examples.english: Russell shut his briefcase with a snap, and the meeting was over.
examples.chinese: 拉塞尔啪嗒一声合上公文包，会议就结束了。

examples.english: Put lids on all the jars and close them tightly.
examples.chinese: 给所有的瓶子都盖上盖子并旋紧。

examples.english: Put the lid on the cookie jar when you've finished with it!
examples.chinese: 你吃完了就把曲奇罐子盖上！

examples.english: If you leave cooked food in a pan, you should always put the lid on.
examples.chinese: 如果把煮好的东西留在锅里，就一定要把盖子盖上。

examples.english: I screwed on the top of the bottle as tightly as I could.
examples.chinese: 我使劲地把瓶盖拧紧。

examples.english: The fuel tank cap hadn't been screwed back on properly, and it came off when I drove away.
examples.chinese: 油箱盖子没有拧好，我一开车离开它就掉下来了。

examples.english: She screwed the lid of the jar on again.
examples.chinese: 她再次把罐子的盖子拧上。

centers.english: to close your eyes/mouth

examples.english: I lay down and closed my eyes.
examples.chinese: 我躺下来闭上眼睛。

examples.english: He shut his eyes and listened to the music.
examples.chinese: 他闭上眼睛听音乐。

examples.english: Lara opened her mouth to speak, then closed it again.
examples.chinese: 劳拉张嘴想说话，但是又闭了起来。

centers.english: to become shut

examples.english: He walked out and the door shut behind him.
examples.chinese: 他走了出去，门在他身后关上了。

examples.english: There was a bang as the gates shut.
examples.chinese: 大门砰的一声关上了。

examples.english: Her eyes closed, and she fell into a deep sleep.
examples.chinese: 她闭上眼睛，沉沉睡去。

examples.english: Outside in the street, car doors slammed and people were shouting.
examples.chinese: 在外面街道上，车门被猛力关上，人们在大声喊叫。

examples.english: She heard a door slam shut and the sound of footsteps on the path.
examples.chinese: 她听到门砰的一声关上了，随后就是小路上响起了脚步声。

examples.english: The window suddenly blew shut, with a loud bang.
examples.chinese: 窗户突然砰的一声被风吹上了。

examples.english: The elevator doors silently slid shut.
examples.chinese: 电梯的门轻轻地滑上了。

centers.english: to shut something so that it cannot be opened

examples.english: As she left the house she locked the door.
examples.chinese: 她离家时把门锁上了。

examples.english: Don't forget to lock the car.
examples.chinese: 别忘了把车锁上。

examples.english: He locked the safe and put the key in his pocket.
examples.chinese: 他锁上保险箱，把钥匙放在口袋里。

examples.english: I had locked up my office for the night and gone home.
examples.chinese: 我锁上办公室的门下班回家。

examples.english: He always keeps his desk locked up.
examples.chinese: 他总是把办公桌锁起来。

examples.english: You should take basic precautions like locking your car up.
examples.chinese: 你应该采取基本的防盗措施，比如说把汽车锁好。

examples.english: My husband always bolts all the doors before going to bed.
examples.chinese: 我丈夫上床前总要把所有的门都闩好。

examples.english: The owner of the house had barred the back door.
examples.chinese: 屋主把后门闩起来了。

examples.english: Some of the survivors said that one of the fire exits had been barred.
examples.chinese: 有些幸存者说有一个消防通道出口的门被闩起来了。

examples.english: If she wasn't home by midnight her father would lock her out.
examples.chinese: 如果她半夜前还没回家，她父亲就会把她锁在门外。

examples.english: We always leave a key with a neighbour in case we lock ourselves out.
examples.chinese: 我们总是留一把钥匙在邻居那儿，以防万一把自己锁在门外。

centers.english: to close an entrance or opening

examples.english: Martha tried to block the mouse holes up, but new ones kept appearing.
examples.chinese: 玛莎试图把老鼠洞堵住，但是新的洞又会不停地出现。

examples.english: Some of the windows in the church had been blocked up.
examples.chinese: 教堂的一些窗户被封起来了。

examples.english: He blocked up the entrance to the tunnel with stones.
examples.chinese: 他用石头堵住了地道的入口。

examples.english: We tried to plug the hole in the bottom of the boat with a plastic bag.
examples.chinese: 我们试图用塑料袋堵住船底的漏洞。

examples.english: They didn't have enough material to plug up the gaps around the pipe.
examples.chinese: 他们没有足够的材料堵住管子的裂口。

examples.english: If you seal the jars well, the jam will keep for months or even years.
examples.chinese: 瓶子如果密封得好，果酱可以保存几个月甚至几年。

examples.english: In this experiment, the chamber must be completely sealed.
examples.chinese: 做这个实验，房间必须完全密封。

centers.english: when a door, entrance, lid etc has been shut
centers.chinese: 门、入口、盖子等已被关上

examples.english: Make sure all the windows are shut before you go out.
examples.chinese: 出去之前一定要把所有窗户都关好。

examples.english: The gates were closed, and there was no other way in.
examples.chinese: 大门关着，而且没有其他的入口。

examples.english: Keep your eyes tight shut.
examples.chinese: 把眼睛紧紧闭上。

examples.english: Jamie tried the door. ‘It's locked,’ he said.
examples.chinese: 杰米推了推门，“锁上的。”他说。

examples.english: All office workers should keep their personal belongings in a locked drawer.
examples.chinese: 办公室里所有员工都应该把私人物件放在抽屉里锁好。

examples.english: I need my coat out of your car -- is it locked?
examples.chinese: 我要把外套从你的汽车里取出来——车门锁着吗？

examples.english: The door's bolted, we'll have to break it down.
examples.chinese: 门是闩着的，我们只得把它撞开。

examples.english: Burglars can always find a way in, in spite of bolted doors and windows.
examples.chinese: 虽然门窗都门好，但是小偷总能找到办法进来。

examples.english: Plants cannot survive in a sealed jar.
examples.chinese: 植物在密封的瓶子里不能存活。

examples.english: Sealed nuclear waste containers are then enclosed in concrete.
examples.chinese: 密封的核废料容器然后就被埋在混凝土里。

centers.english: when a shop or office is closed

examples.english: ‘What time does the bank shut?’ ‘Four o'clock.’
examples.chinese: “银行几点钟关门？”“4点钟。”

examples.english: Most of the stores close at 6:30.
examples.chinese: 大多数商店在6点半关门。

examples.english: The ticket office was closed.
examples.chinese: 售票处关门了。

examples.english: It was nine o'clock and all the stores were shut.
examples.chinese: 那时候是9点钟，所有的商店都关门了。

centers.english: to fasten something

examples.english: Fasten your coat - it's cold outside.
examples.chinese: 把外套扣上，外面冷。

examples.english: He fastened the bracelet for her.
examples.chinese: 他为她扣上手镯。

examples.english: Ella fastened her blouse with shaking fingers.
examples.chinese: 埃拉手指颤抖着扣好衬衫。

examples.english: Please keep your seat belts fastened.
examples.chinese: 请系好安全带。

examples.english: He began buttoning his shirt and putting on his tie.
examples.chinese: 他开始扣上衬衫纽扣，系上领带。

examples.english: Stone buttoned up his heavy jacket.
examples.chinese: 斯通扣上他那件厚厚的夹克。

examples.english: She buttoned her cardigan up all the way to her neck.
examples.chinese: 她把羊毛衫一直扣到脖子。

examples.english: I adjusted my tie and buttoned up my coat.
examples.chinese: 我正了一下领带并扣上外套。

examples.english: It was cold and his coat was completely buttoned up.
examples.chinese: 天气寒冷，他的外套扣子全都扣上了。

examples.english: I can't zip up these jeans - they're too tight.
examples.chinese: 我拉不上牛仔裤拉链，裤子太紧了。

examples.english: Can you zip my dress for me?
examples.chinese: 你能为我拉上连衣裙的拉链吗？

examples.english: She took some money out of her purse and quickly zipped it up again.
examples.chinese: 她从手提包里取出一些钱，又很快拉好拉链。

examples.english: Roger zipped up the battered black case he carried his guitar in.
examples.chinese: 罗杰拉上破旧的黑色吉他匣套的拉链。

examples.english: My sleeping bag was fully zipped up.
examples.chinese: 我的睡袋全部拉上了。

examples.english: Come on then, do up your coat and let's go.
examples.chinese: 快点，扣好外套我们走吧。

examples.english: When I walked into the room, Allen was doing up his trousers.
examples.chinese: 我进房间时，艾伦正在拉裤子拉链。

examples.english: I can't do this zip up - it's stuck.
examples.chinese: 我拉不上拉链，它卡住了。

examples.english: Are your shoelaces done up properly?
examples.chinese: 你的鞋带系好了吗？

examples.english: The toggles on his duffel coat were done up wrongly.
examples.chinese: 他那连帽粗呢外套上的栓扣没扣对。

examples.english: She tied a scarf around her neck.
examples.chinese: 她在脖子上系了条围巾。

examples.english: Do you know how to tie a bow tie?
examples.chinese: 你知道怎么打蝶形领结吗？

examples.english: Eighty percent of motorists now buckle up, studies show.
examples.chinese: 研究显示现在80%的驾车者都系安全带。

examples.english: Nancy got behind the wheel and buckled up.
examples.chinese: 南希坐到驾驶座上，系上了安全带。

examples.english: The new law will require passengers in the rear seats of automobiles to buckle their seatbelts.
examples.chinese: 这条新法令要求汽车后座上的乘客系安全带。

centers.english: to unfasten something

examples.english: It was hot in the waiting-room, so I unfastened my coat.
examples.chinese: 等候室里很热，因此我就解开了外衣。

examples.english: Jack unfastened his seatbelt and stepped out of the car.
examples.chinese: 杰克解开安全带走出汽车。

examples.english: The back of her dress was unfastened.
examples.chinese: 她连衣裙的背面松开了。

examples.english: My fingers were so cold that I couldn't undo the buttons.
examples.chinese: 我手指冻得解不开纽扣了。

examples.english: Rosie undid her necklace and put it on the bedside table.
examples.chinese: 罗茜解下项链把它放到床头柜上。

examples.english: Your zip's undone!
examples.chinese: 你的拉链松开了！

examples.english: One of his shoelaces had come undone.
examples.chinese: 他的一根鞋带松开了。

examples.english: She slowly unbuttoned her blouse.
examples.chinese: 她慢慢地解开衬衫扣子。

examples.english: Father Poole began to unbutton his overcoat.
examples.chinese: 普尔神父开始解开大衣纽扣。

examples.english: His shirt was completely unbuttoned.
examples.chinese: 他的衬衫完全敞开着。

examples.english: She unzipped the case and took out a thick file.
examples.chinese: 她拉开箱子的拉链，拿出一个厚厚的文件夹。

examples.english: He unzipped his jacket and flung it on a chair.
examples.chinese: 他脱下夹克，把它扔到椅子上。

examples.english: An unzipped bag was sitting on one of the benches.
examples.chinese: 长凳上有一只拉链开着的包。

examples.english: He untied his right shoe first.
examples.chinese: 他先解开右脚的鞋带。

examples.english: Amy untied her apron and folded it neatly.
examples.chinese: 埃米解下围裙，把它叠得整整齐齐。

examples.english: He's always walking around with his shoelaces untied.
examples.chinese: 他老是鞋带不系好就到处走。

examples.english: I'd eaten so much that I had to loosen my belt.
examples.chinese: 我吃了这么多，得松松裤带了。

examples.english: Carter took off his jacket and loosened his tie.
examples.chinese: 卡特脱下外套，松开领带。

examples.english: It was very hot and the men had their shirts open to the waist.
examples.chinese: 天气非常热，男人们把衬衫敞到腰上。

examples.english: Emily realised that her handbag was open and her money gone.
examples.chinese: 埃米莉发现手提包开着，钱不见了。

examples.english: Her blouse came open in front of a nationwide TV audience!
examples.chinese: 在全国的电视观众面前，她的衬衫突然松开了！

centers.english: to open a door, window, box etc

examples.english: It's very hot in here. Do you mind if I open the window?
examples.chinese: 这里很热，我开窗行吗?

examples.english: The drawer's locked - you need a key to open it.
examples.chinese: 抽屉锁着，你需要钥匙才能把它打开。

examples.english: He opened the door wide, and gestured for me to come in.
examples.chinese: 他把门敞开，示意我进去。

examples.english: Unlock the door! We can't get out!
examples.chinese: 把门锁打开！我们出不去了！

examples.english: Which of these keys unlocks the safe?
examples.chinese: 哪一把钥匙是开保险箱的？

examples.english: ‘Come and see’, Jo said, unlocking a huge iron gate.
examples.chinese: “来看看。”乔说着，打开一扇大铁门的锁。

examples.english: The door's stuck - we'll have to force it open.
examples.chinese: 门卡住了—我们只能把它砸开了。

examples.english: The burglars had forced open the window with an iron bar.
examples.chinese: 窃贼用铁棍撬开了窗子。

examples.english: It looked as though the shed had been forced open.
examples.chinese: 小屋看来好像被人砸门进去了。

examples.english: We managed to break open the crate with an axe.
examples.chinese: 我们设法用斧头把板条箱砸开了。

examples.english: There's no key for the suitcase - we'll have to break it open.
examples.chinese: 这旅行箱没有钥匙，我们只能把它砸开。

examples.english: Gulls carry shellfish into the air, then drop them onto hard surfaces to break them open.
examples.chinese: 海鸥把水中的贝类动物携至空中，然后把它们摔落在坚硬的表面上砸开。

examples.english: All the flats were boarded up, but we managed to prise a few boards open.
examples.chinese: 所有的公寓都用木条封了起来，我们想办法撬开了几块木条。

examples.english: Laura leaned forward to pry open the crate.
examples.chinese: 劳拉俯下身子撬开了板条箱。

examples.english: I picked up the coffee tin and, using a knife, prised the lid off.
examples.chinese: 我拿起咖啡罐，用小刀把盖子撬开了。

examples.english: I knocked, then tried the door. It was open, but the room was empty.
examples.chinese: 我敲了敲门，又试着推了一下。门开着，但房间里没人。

examples.english: I went around the back to try the windows, but they were all locked.
examples.chinese: 我绕到后面试试打开窗子，但都锁上了。

examples.english: Firemen had to break the door down.
examples.chinese: 消防队员不得不破门而入。

examples.english: Open the door now or we'll break it down!
examples.chinese: 立即开门，否则我们就砸门了！

examples.english: The driver wound down his window and asked us the way to the stadium.
examples.chinese: 那司机摇下车窗，问我们体育馆怎么走。

examples.english: Mom, will you roll your window down a little please?
examples.chinese: 妈妈，你把窗子摇下来一点好吗？

examples.english: Open up - it's the police!
examples.chinese: 开门一我们是警察！

centers.english: when a door or window opens

examples.english: The train doors open and close automatically.
examples.chinese: 列车车门是自动开关的。

examples.english: The door slowly opened and a small boy entered the room.
examples.chinese: 门慢慢打开，一个小男孩走进了房间。

examples.english: After a short discussion with the customs officers, the gates opened and the truck moved off.
examples.chinese: 和海关官员商量了一下之后，大门打开，卡车开走了。

examples.english: Before Mr Carey could speak, the door burst open and Mr Watson rushed into the room.
examples.chinese: 凯里先生还没来得及开口，门砰的一声打开了，沃森先生冲进了房间。

examples.english: Every time we go round a corner, the passenger door flies open.
examples.chinese: 我们每转个弯，乘客门就会弹开。

centers.english: an open door or window

examples.english: Carrie stood in front of the open window.
examples.chinese: 卡丽站在开着的窗子前。

examples.english: The office door was open, and I could hear everything they said.
examples.chinese: 办公室的门开着，他们的话我全听到了。

examples.english: In the mirror, she saw him slide open one of his drawers.
examples.chinese: 她从镜子里看到他拉开一个抽屉。

examples.english: Do you know you left the window wide open all night?
examples.chinese: 你知道吗？你让窗子一整夜都大开着。

examples.english: She had left her bedroom door ajar and could hear her parents talking downstairs.
examples.chinese: 她卧室的门半开着，所以她能听到父母在楼下说话。

examples.english: To his right was a large walk-in cupboard, its door slightly ajar.
examples.chinese: 他右边是一个步入式的大壁橱，橱门微微开着。

centers.english: to open a bottle, can, jar

examples.english: Ask the waiter to open another bottle of champagne.
examples.chinese: 叫服务员再开一瓶香槟。

examples.english: a little gadget that helps you to open jars
examples.chinese: 开罐子用的小东西

examples.english: Robyn unscrewed a jar of moisturiser and smoothed it over her face.
examples.chinese: 罗宾旋开一罐润肤霜，把润肤霜均匀地涂在脸上。

examples.english: Pills are stored in containers with tops that are difficult for children to unscrew.
examples.chinese: 药片装在小孩子不容易打开的容器里。

examples.english: Let's crack open a bottle of champagne.
examples.chinese: 我们来开一瓶香槟吧。

examples.english: We cracked a few cans of beer open and sat down to watch the game.
examples.chinese: 我们开了几罐啤酒，坐下来看比赛。

examples.english: Ray uncorked the bottle and offered me a glass of wine.
examples.chinese: 雷拔去瓶塞，给我倒了一杯葡萄酒。

centers.english: to open a packet or something that is folded or rolled
centers.chinese: 打开包裹，打开折起或卷起的物品

examples.english: Aren't you going to open your letter?
examples.chinese: 你不准备拆信吗?

examples.english: Judy opened another pack of cigarettes.
examples.chinese: 朱迪又打开了一包香烟。

examples.english: When I received the parcel, it had already been opened.
examples.chinese: 我收到包裹的时候，它已经被人打开了。

examples.english: I just love unwrapping Christmas presents!
examples.chinese: 我就喜欢拆开圣诞礼物！

examples.english: Sarah sat down and unwrapped her sandwiches.
examples.chinese: 萨拉坐下来，打开三明治的包装。

examples.english: They unfolded the tablecloth and set out the picnic.
examples.chinese: 他们摊开台布，摆出野餐的食物。

examples.english: The receipt had been folded and unfolded so many times that it was almost in pieces.
examples.chinese: 那张收条反复地折起打开，打开又折起，都快成碎片了。

examples.english: He unrolled the map and spread it on the table.
examples.chinese: 他摊开地图铺在桌子上。

examples.english: While we were admiring the rugs, the shopkeeper started to unroll a splendid carpet.
examples.chinese: 我们在欣赏小地毯的时候，店主开始铺开一卷华丽的大地毯。

centers.english: to open your eyes or mouth

examples.english: She opened her eyes and sat up in bed.
examples.chinese: 她睁开双眼，在床上坐起来。

examples.english: The dentist told me to open my mouth a little wider.
examples.chinese: 牙医叫我再张大一点嘴巴。

examples.english: Open wide. I need to look at the back of your throat.
examples.chinese: 张大嘴巴，我要看看你喉咙的后部。

examples.english: She sleeps with her eyes half open.
examples.chinese: 她睡着的时候眼睛半闭半开。

expressions.text: can hardly keep your eyes open

examples.english: I was so tired I could hardly keep my eyes open.
examples.chinese: 我累得眼睛都睁不开了。

examples.english: Ben was staring at her with his mouth wide open.
examples.chinese: 本盯着她看，嘴巴张得大大的。

examples.english: Flies crawled over the gaping mouth of the injured man.
examples.chinese: 那名受伤男子张开着嘴巴，苍蝇在上面爬。

centers.english: when a shop, bank, restaurant etc opens
centers.chinese: 商店、银行、餐馆等开门（营业）

examples.english: ‘What times do the banks open?’ ‘Normally at around 9.30.’
examples.chinese: “银行什么时候开门？”“一般9点半开门。”

examples.english: On Saturdays, the restaurant opens at 7 p.m.
examples.chinese: 星期六这家餐馆都是晚上7点开始营业的。

examples.english: Unknown to me, the office had opened early, and all the tickets had been sold.
examples.chinese: 我不知道票房提早开门，所有的票都卖完了。

examples.english: The World Café is open from 10 a.m. till 11 p.m.
examples.chinese: 世界咖啡馆从早上10时到晚上11时营业。

centers.english: to visit a person

examples.english: I visit my grandparents at least once a month.
examples.chinese: 我至少每月去看祖父母一次。

examples.english: Paul visited her every day when she was in hospital.
examples.chinese: 她住院的时候，保罗每天去看她。

examples.english: We won't be that far away - you'll be able to come and visit.
